itineraries.title: Say Yes in the Island of Serendipity
itineraries.summary: This romantic itinerary offers couples unforgettable proposal and wedding experiences across Sri Lanka’s most scenic locations, from beachfront ceremonies to hill country retreats and cultural celebrations.
itineraries.detail_title: Say ‘Yes’ in the Island of Serendipity
itineraries.detail_text: Say ‘Yes’ in the Island of Serendipity If you are dreaming of a one-of-a-kind romantic escape, choose Sri Lanka with no doubts! Sri Lanka is overflowing with everything from cultural temples to lush green lands to thrilling wildlife and stunning beaches in every corner, though it seems like a tiny island in shape. Are you ready to create your customized version of “Forever Begins” in Sri Lanka? Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka is known as world’s one of the best destinations for honeymooners and romantic gateways, as it offers an amazing blend of leisure and adventure experiences to make your journey truly unforgettable. If you are dreaming to popping the question in a hot air balloon at sunrise, dining in a candlelit cave, under a canopy of stars in the heart of the jungle, or of course, while sailing through a luxury Yatch in the middle of the Indian ocean – Sri Lanka offers unforgettable moments at every turn. Unlike anywhere else, this island paradise wraps up with everything in a compact land including culture, nature, wilderness, pristine beaches, mouthwatering cuisine, and much more unique experiences which can be explored just within less travel time. From luxurious adults-only retreats to boutique hideaways nestled in less touristy parts of the country, will undoubtably take your journey in Sri Lanka to a lavish experience, with indulgent amenities and breathtaking views. This curated plan invites you to discover the island’s hidden beauty while indulging in variety of romantic experiences such as candlelit dinners under the stars, high tea experiences, rejuvenating couple spa treatments, UNESCO World Heritage Sites exploration and more. The tropical climate will certainly add its own magic and blessing – that seem made just for you both. Feel free to reach out to one of the travel designers at Blue Lanka Tours and let us to assist to unfold your love story, in a land where every detail feels destined . Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Bia to Kotugoda Welcome to Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport. Transfer to Kotugoda. Check-Into Your Hotel and Recover Any Jetlag. Overnight Stay in Kotugoda. Exclusive for you! – You May Take a Dip in The Hotel Pool or Drink Sri Lanka’s Famous ‘King Coconut’ to Refresh Your Senses and Let Any Tiredness from Your Journey to Sri Lanka Melt Away! Day2 Kotugoda to Kandy Transfer to Kandy. En Route Visit to Elephant Freedom Project to Show Your Love Towards the Giant Mammals there! You may Feed, Wash and Walk with Elephants. Continue Your Drive to Kandy. Check-Into the Hotel and Spend the Evening at Leisure, while Enjoying Hotel Facilities. Overnight Stay in Kandy. Exclusive for you! – Dining experience in terrace overlooking the hills! Day3 Kandy to Ella Morning Pay a Visit to Tooth Relic Temple of Kandy and Take Blessings. Take a Scenic Train Ride to Ella from Kandy. You will Journey Passing Misty Hills; Green Tea Fields; Cascading Waterfalls, etc. Check-Into The Hotel and Enjoy the Resort Facilities. Overnight Stay in Ella. Exclusive for you! – Indulge in a Romantic Couple Treatment at Resort Spa (Love and Desire) Day4 Ella Morning Hike to Little Adam’s Peak to Witness Stunning Views of Sunrise! Take a Walk along the Nine Arch Bridges. Evening is Free! You May Enjoy Resort Facilities or Explore Ella Outdoors on Your Own. Overnight Stay in Ella. Day5 Ella to Yala Morning Transfer to Yala – Ideal place to enjoy a taste of the Sri Lankan wilderness and Spot Wildlife to the Fullest! En Route Witness Ravana Waterfall (Sightseeing Only). Join an Afternoon Game Drive in Yala National Park. Overnight Stay in Yala. Exclusive for you! – A Dune Dining Experience Followed by a Chilled Beer/Bottle of Wine! Day6 Yala Morning Go on another wildlife adventure of a lifetime at Yala National Park! With any luck you will spot Elephants and Leopards as well as Sloth Bears and more! Afternoon is Free! Enjoy Ann/All the Resort Activities exclusively available for you. Overnight Stay in Yala. Exclusive for you! – Pamper yourself with a couple’s massage at the inhouse spa | Visit to Jungle Monastery of Sithulpawwa | Explore the Countryside of Yala by 02-Wheels Day7 Yala to Galle Transfer to Galle. En Route Stop in Thalpe. Learn to create a gastronomic Sri Lankan masterpiece at a fun Sri Lankan cooking class with a Local, Humble Lady! Check-Into the Hotel and Enjoy the Facilities. Evening Go for a Sunset Walk on the Ramparts of Galle Fort. Overnight Stay in Galle. Day8 Galle Full Day is Unscheduled! Relax and Enjoy Hotel Facilities. Overnight Stay in Galle. Exclusive for you! – Enjoy lunch at Pedlar’s Inn Café | Cool off with some authentic Sri Lankan Gelato at Il Gelato | Visit Barefoot Galley and Shop for colorful Sri Lankan souvenirs – a fantastic collection of ethical Sri Lankan handloom fashion, jewelry, souvenirs and coffee table books to remind you of Sri Lanka’s finest once you’re back home | Enjoy a sundowner at the timeless Galle Fort Hotel Day 9 Galle to Bia Transfer to Airport Safely. Exclusive for you! – You have the choice to finish off your 09 days of romance on a high note by taking domestic flight transfer to Airport from Koggala with Cinnamon Air! Journey Highlights Trip length: 9 Days Experience a magical morning as you and your loved one float peacefully among the clouds – in a hot air balloon. Indulge in a romantic dining experience where candlelight meets centuries of stone at Kandalama. Heal yourselves with a soulful couple spa treatment in Ella. Dine by the wilderness of Yala, while listening to the wild symphony of stars and silence. Try Sri Lanka’s liquid gold – with a traditional arrack tasting session at Galle Fort. Partake in a hands-on cooking class to learn about the Lankan flavors. Places you will stay See available hotels The Wallawwa Kotugoda Heritance Kandalama Dambulla W15 Hanthana Estate Kandy Wild Coast Tented Lodge Palatupana Jetwing Saman Villas Bentota See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Top proposal spots: during the hot air balloon ride, cave dining, jungle dining, or atop Pidurangala Rock Bring an extra kit when visiting Millennium Elephant Foundation – it will come in handy. Thrill yourself with a couple’s swing experience in Ella. This tour is not only for relaxation, but also for exploration as you collect memories together Sunset walk along the Galle fort is something you shouldn’t miss – it’s truly magical! Visit the Bawa Garden, but leave snacks behind, as no outside food or beverages are permitted on the premises. This itinerary is best enjoyed during Winter months. If you are traveling in summer, feel free to request a revised version! FAQ Q: Will the hot air balloon tour be on a private or shared basis? It will be on a shared basis, but reach out to your travel designer to book a private experience. Q: Would it be possible to extend the duration of this tour, probably the stay at W15 Hanthana? Absolutely! We can tweak this itinerary just as you require. Q: Will the Yala safari be exclusively available for both of us, or should we join with anyone? It will be on a private basis – just two of you in the jeep. Q: Can we do watersports during the stay in Bentota? Certainly! Bentota is known for amazing watersports like jet skiing, water skiing, banana boat rides, tube rides, etc. You can book through us, the resort, or get the assistance from your designated chauffeur guide. Q: Is it possible to opt in for the car transfer rather than taking the seaplane transfer for the departure? Yes, it will also be a cost-effective option.

itineraries.title: Purify and Discover Yourself
itineraries.summary: This wellness itinerary offers transformative spiritual journeys through ancient Ayurveda treatments, meditation retreats, and yoga sessions amidst Sri Lanka’s serene natural landscapes and sacred sites.
itineraries.detail_title: Purify and Discover Yourself
itineraries.detail_text: Purify and Discover Yourself Explore More Tailormade This Tours This thoughtfully crafted 14-day wellness journey is designed for those seeking physical, emotional, and spiritual rejuvenation in Sri Lanka’s most tranquil settings. From meditative retreats in tea country and immersive Ayurvedic therapies, to UNESCO heritage landmarks and holistic nutrition, this tour balances gentle adventure with deep relaxation. Reconnect with yourself as you explore ancient temples, take part in daily yoga and meditation, and let expert practitioners guide you toward renewed wellbeing. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Negombo Arrive in Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport Transfer to Negombo. Check-Into Your Hotel. Relax and Recover Any Jetlag Overnight Stay in Negombo Optional – If you have spare time, speak to your chauffeur and ask him to introduce you to the sights and sounds of Negombo or do an Excursion to Colombo Day2 Negombo to Dambulla Transfer to Dambulla En Route Visit Ridee Viharaya Evening Pay a Visit to Dambulla Cave Temple – A UNESCO World Heritage Site Overnight Stay in Dambulla Day 3 Dambulla Morning Visit to Explore Rose Quartz Mountain Range (Jathika Namal Uyana) – This has a history that spans back over 550 million years! Reach the Top and Meditate at this Spiritual Place Evening Treat Yourself with a Herbal Foot Massage Overnight Stay in Dambulla Day 4 Dambulla to Kandy Morning Climb to Explore the Rock Fortress of Sigiriya – A UNESCO World Heritage Site Transfer to Kandy En Route Stop at Nalanda Gedige Take Blessings at Temple of the Tooth and Take a Tour Around Kandy City and Lake Overnight Stay in Kandy (Nilambe) Day5 Kandy It’s time to Learn the Lessons of Meditation! You May Follow Below Day Schedule on this Day. 04:45am – Wake-up 05:00am – 06:00am – Guided Meditation 06:00am – 06:30am – Tea 06:30am – 07:30am – Mindfulness in Motion *(Self practice) 07:30am – 08:00am – Breakfast 08:00am – 09:30am – Working Meditation 09:30am – 11:00am – Guided Meditation (Standing/Sitting/Walking) 11:00am – 11:45am – Talk 12:00pm – 02:00pm – Lunch, Reflection and Rest 02:00pm – 02:30pm – Walking Meditation 02:30pm – 03:30pm – Group Meditation 03:30pm – 04:30pm – Herbal Drink/Working Meditation 04:30pm – 05:30pm – Group Meditation 06:00pm – 06:30pm – Evening Snack 06:30pm – 09:00pm – Chanting, Group Meditation and Discussion Overnight Stay in Kandy (Nilambe) Day6 Kandy Today also You will Follow the Day Schedule of Day 05 Enjoy Available Facilities at the Center Overnight Stay in Kandy (Nilambe) Day7 Kandy to Aluthgama Morning Transfer to Aluthgama Check-Into Your Ayurvedic Resort Refresh Yourself, Relax and Enjoy Resort Facilities Overnight Stay in Aluthgama Day8 – 13 Aluthgama Spend 06 Full Days Experiencing Ayurvedic Treatment, Meditation, and Yoga at the hotel! Partake in Initial Medical Consultation Indulge-In Daily Treatments Depending on the Package You Choose Wellness Pure Princess Beauty Cellulite Treatment Enjoy All Three Meals plus Non-Alcoholic Beverages Throughout the Stay Overnights Stay in Aluthgama Day14 Aluthgama to BIA Prior to the Departure, your ayurvedic Doctor will provide you instructions on the ways and means of leading a healthy life! Later Transfer by Airport for a safe flight back home Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days When to go: November to April Transfers: Airport – Hotel – Tour attractions – Airport Recover from travel with serene lakefront stays and nature walks in Negombo. Marvel at Dambulla’s ancient cave temples and the Rose Quartz mountain range. Hike Sigiriya (Lion’s Rock) and explore Sri Lanka’s spiritual heritage. Find inner peace and discipline at the Nilambe Meditation Centre with structured practice and mindfulness training. Spend six transformative days in an acclaimed Ayurveda resort with tailored treatments, daily yoga, and healing meals. Places you will stay See available hotels Jetwing Lagoon Negombo Jetwing Lake Dambulla Nilambe Buddhist Meditation Centre Nilambe Lanka Princess Hotel Bentota See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Arrive early for meditation sessions—mornings are especially peaceful. Bring white or light-colored, loose-fitting clothes for meditation and yoga. Hydration is important—carry a reusable water bottle, especially in dry zones. Journal your thoughts daily to capture your growth and insights. Discuss your dietary preferences with the Ayurveda resort for a more personalized experience. Respect silent hours and community rules at wellness and meditation centers. FAQ Q: Is prior experience in yoga or meditation necessary? No, all levels are welcome; instructors and facilitators will guide you. Q: Are the Ayurveda treatments personalized? Yes, you’ll receive an initial consultation, and therapies are tailored to your needs. Q: What’s included in the resort package? Daily meals, most treatments, yoga sessions, and use of resort facilities are included; some premium therapies may incur extra charges. Q: Is this tour suitable for solo travelers? Absolutely, both solo travelers and groups can benefit equally; it’s especially well-suited for those seeking renewal.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Land and Sea
itineraries.summary: This comprehensive itinerary combines cultural exploration of ancient cities with beach relaxation, offering diverse experiences from wildlife safaris to coastal activities across Sri Lanka’s varied landscapes.
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Land and Sea
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Land and Sea Explore More Tailormade This Tours This 14-day itinerary offers an immersive exploration of Sri Lanka’s diverse landscapes, culture, and coastlines. Embark on a journey from vibrant fishing villages, lush hill-country tea plantations, and UNESCO-listed cultural heritage sites, to thrilling wildlife safaris, and finish with a unique cruise experience along the island’s picturesque southern shores. Discover the pulse of Sri Lanka through authentic local experiences, scenic train rides, hands-on cooking, and time spent amidst nature and the sea. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Negombo Welcome to Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport Transfer to Negombo Check-Into Hotel. Relax and Recharge After Your Flight Overnight Stay in Negombo Day2 Negombo to Kandy Morning Explore the Fishing Village of Negombo Including a Visit to ‘Lellama’ Transfer to Kandy En Route Stop to Explore the Royal Botanical Garden in Peradeniya Check-Into the Hotel. Refresh and Enjoy Hotel Facilities Overnight Stay in Kandy Day 3 Kandy Morning Explore Kandy City and Lake Take in the Kandy Markets Evening Witness Cultural Dance Show Take Blessings at Tooth Relic Temple of Kandy During ‘Theva’ Time Overnight Stay in Kandy Day 4 Kandy to Ella Morning Transfer to Nuwara Eliya En Route Witness Stunning Ramboda Waterfalls Drive Through Nuwara Eliya and Discover the Landmarks there Take a Scenic Train Ride to Ella from Nanu Oya Overnight Stay in Ella Day5 Ella Morning Hike to Little Adam’s Peak Visit to Nine Arch Bridge Visit a Tea Estate and a Factory to Learn the Process of Making Ceylon Tea Evening Enjoy a Cooking Demonstration of Classic Homestyle Sri Lankan Dishes at a Typical Local Place Overnight Stay in Ella Day6 Ella to Udawalawe Transfer to Udawalawe En Route Witness Ravana Waterfall (Sightseeing Only) Visit to Elephant Transit Home Afternoon Go on a Wildlife Safari Through Udawalawe National Park Check-Into the Campsite. Relax with a BBQ Dinner Around the Campfire Overnight Stay in Udawalawe Day7 Udawalawe to Unawatuna Transfer to Unawatuna Check-Into the Beach Resort and Relax Evening Visit to Roomassala and Japanese Peace Pagoda Overnight Stay in Unawatuna Day8 Unawatuna to Mirissa to Weligama Bay Spend the Morning at Leisure Enjoying Resort Facilities Transfer to Mirissa Harbour On Board the Cruise at 04:00 PM. Take a Short Cruise into the Stunning Weligama Bay, where You can relax with a dinner on board under the stars! Overnight Stay in Cruise Day9 Weligama to Kudawella Following Breakfast on Board, Sail Towards the virgin and preserved beaches of Kudawella! Keep your eyes peeled for dolphins and whales along the way! Visit Fishing Villages and Local Temples there. Later You May Relax, take a Swim or Experience a Stand-Up Paddle board (SUP) session Enjoy a Seafood Dinner under the Stars Overnight Stay in Cruise Day10 Kudawella to Kalametiya After Breakfast, Continue to Sail Along the Southern Coast up to Kalametiya. Explore the Lagoon Afternoon, opt to visit the Kalametiya Village and Sanctuary, or soak up the sun on the beach Dine On Board Under the Stars Overnight Stay in Cruise Day11 Kalametiya to Tallala After a Good Breakfast, Visit Beaches of Hiriketiya for a Surf or Swim Then Sail Towards Tallala! Keep a lookout for marine life along the way! Sun Bathe on the Beautiful Tallala Beach. Optionally, you can visit local fish or fruit market. Overnight Stay in Cruise Day12 Tallala to Galle Following Heart Breakfast, Sail Towards Rumassala Watch For Whales and Dolphins on The Way Afternoon Relaxing on Jungle Beach Alternatively, you can Hike Up to the Japanese Peace Pagoda with Sweeping Views Over the Galle Bay! Overnight Stay in Cruise Day13 Galle to Weligama Bay Spend the Morning Exploring Galle and the Dutch Fort Sail Back to Weligama Bay Enjoy a BBQ on the beach with Your Family/Friends Overnight Stay in Cruise Day14 Weligama Bay to Mirissa to BIA Following Breakfast on Board, Sail back to the Mirissa Fisheries Harbour You will Disembark at 10:00 AM Later Transfer to Airport for Flight Home! Journey Highlights Trip length: 14 Days When to go: November to April Transfers: Airport – Hotel – Tour attractions – Airport Take part in city and village explorations, including Negombo fish markets and Kandy’s cultural dance shows. Enjoy scenic journeys such as the iconic train ride from Nuwara Eliya to Ella. Discover tea country, waterfalls, and heritage sites. Go on a wildlife safari and visit the Elephant Transit Home at Udawalawe. Embark on a multi-day coastal and marine adventure: cruise along the south coast, relax on palm-fringed beaches, snorkel, watch whales, and enjoy BBQ sunsets. Experience local cuisine with culinary demos and seafood BBQs. Places you will stay See available hotels Heritance Negombo Negombo Hide Ella Hotel and Resort Ella Banyan Camp Udawalawa Earl’s Regency Kandy See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Start your adventure with an open mind—each region of Sri Lanka offers its own culture and pace. Dress appropriately for sacred sites and local customs (shoulders and knees covered). Bring a rain jacket for hill country and reef-safe sunscreen for the coast. Early mornings are best for wildlife viewing; keep your camera handy. Seasickness remedies are recommended for cruise segments. Enjoy local seafood and don’t miss a sunset BBQ on the beach or boat. FAQ Q: What is the best time of year for this itinerary? The best period is November to April, offering the most pleasant weather, especially for the south and west coasts. Q: Are whale-watching trips included? Whale watching is available on specified days during the southern coastal cruise, especially near Mirissa and Weligama. Q: What should I pack? Comfortable walking shoes, beachwear, a light raincoat, motion sickness tablets, and culturally appropriate outfits for temple visits. Q: Is this tour suitable for families? Yes, with a balance of wildlife, culture, relaxation, and adventure, it’s ideal for both adventurous adults and families with children.

itineraries.title: Into the Wild with Rustic Retreats
itineraries.summary: This adventure itinerary offers immersive wildlife experiences and eco-friendly stays in national parks, providing authentic encounters with Sri Lanka’s diverse flora and fauna in their natural habitats.
itineraries.detail_title: Into the Wild, with Rustic Retreats
itineraries.detail_text: Into the Wild, with Rustic Retreats Looking for an exclusive plan to discover the untamed beauty of Sri Lanka? Here’s the perfect plan for it as it features everything from rainforest walks, safaris in untouched national parks to soulful stays in just two weeks. It’s not just travel – it’s a reconnection with the wild, rustic routes and rugged roads. Explore More Tailormade This Tours This is an itinerary which speaks all about wilderness and soulful wonders in this island paradise. Embark on this 14-days tour to explore the ancient traditions, untamed beauty and back to nature experience with stays in rustic hideaways in the wild. Anyone who is keen on wanderlust, nature calls, animal lovers and curious about soulful stays, you are welcome to be a part of this adventure. We will take you through the UNESCO listed rainforest, remote bush camps, tranquil lakes, art-filled jungle sanctuaries, off the beaten tracks, lesser-known national parks, and other rich biodiversity hotspots with this plan. In addition, you may get the chance to watch a movie under the stars, meet indigenous Vedda tribe, canoeing across butterfly-shaped lakes in Belihuloya, learn the art of ancient martial arts, treat yourself with ayurvedic treatment treatments while celebrating this wilderness and soulful wonders of the country. Campfire dinners, mindful moments through yoga and off-the-grid stays these itinerary covers will certainly add a remark on your Sri Lanka tour with us. Along the way, you can enjoy overnight stays in jungle hideouts, eco-friendly retreats while learning about their sustainable practices, and mobile camping experiences that add a touch of thrill and authenticity to your tour. Do not hesitate to connect with one of the Blue Lanka Tours travel designers right away, to plan your customized tour and let Sri Lanka’s wild heart echo in your own. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Kalutara Welcome to Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport. Transfer to Kalutara. Check-Into Hotel. Relax and Recharge After Your Flight. Overnight Stay in Kalutara. Day2 Kalutara Spend the Day Enjoying the Resort Facilities and Activities. Facilities – The Colonial Spa | Swimming Pool | The Scottish Bar | Yoga Pavilion | Exquisite Outdoor Dining | Sundown Picnic | A floating breakfast in the privacy of your villa. Activities – Yoga for Beginners | Meditation to Soothe the Soul | The View Point Hike The Cinnamon Field Trek | Glenross Rubber Experience | Traditional Sri Lankan Cooking Experience | Board Games for Children | Learn ancient martial art form of Angampora | Movie Night | ‘Dhyanavabodhya’ – The Mindfulness Stroll. Overnight Stay in Kalutara. Day3 Kalutara After having a hearty breakfast at the Hotel, Leave to Sinharaja, for a Guided Walk through the UNESCO-listed forest, discovering unique flora (orchids, towering trees). Spot endemic wildlife and rare birds (e.g. Blue Magpie, Red-faced Malkoha). Enjoy Mid-day breakfast with a packed lunch while Relaxing in the tranquil rainforest setting. Transfer Back to the Property. Indulge in a Foot massage along with refreshing beverages upon arriving. Overnight Stay in Kalutara. Day4 Kalutara to Belihul Oya Morning Transfer to Belihuloya. Upon Reaching the Meeting Point of the Campsite, Start a Short Welcome Trek Through the Lost World with a Campsite Naturalist! Check-Into Your Suite-Tent. Refresh and Relax | Alternatively You May opt for 2-3-Hour Adventure Bushwalk that will take you through the grassland and forest. Enjoy a Magical Sunsets at a Viewpoint Around the Campsite. Take a Relaxing Soak in Your Bush Bathtub and Spend Evening by the Campfire Stories Under the Stars. Spend a Restful Night Isolating with Billion Stars. Overnight Stay in Belihuloya. Day5 Belihuloya Following Early Breakfast, Start the Activities Lined-Up for the Day. It’s Adventure Time! Cycle to an Old Village Named ‘Kinchigune’ Where Your Adventure Begins Fly Through the Forest with an Exciting Zip-Line Down to the Lake Bank Experience Canoeing in Beautiful “Samanala Wewe” (Butterfly Lake) Enjoy an Authentic Village Lunch Transfer Back to the Campsite by Vehicle Relax in your Day Bed / Star Bed, unwind in your Bush Bathtub, or Simply Fall Asleep in Your Private Garden Hammock Relish a delicious BBQ dinner under the stars by a Campfire Take a Relaxing Night Walk for an Immersive Experience of Nocturnal Wildlife Overnight Stay in Belihuloya. Day6 Belihuloya to Gal Oya Early Morning Guide Nature Trail with an In-House Naturalist. Enjoy a Hearty Breakfast at the Campsite. Transfer to Gal Oya. Enjoy a Picnic Lunch Next to a Beautiful River. Go On a Jungle Drive Through Gal Oya National Park. Overnight Stay in Gal Oya. Day7 Gal Oya Morning Enjoy a Boat Ride on Sri Lanka’s Largest Lake. With a Little Luck You Could Spot Elephants Swimming Between Islands or Bathing at the Lake’s Edge. Evening Explore the picturesque surroundings on a bike, discovering the Authentic day-to-day routines of local communities. Overnight Stay in Charming Gal Oya. Optional Excursions – Enjoy a Walk of Discovery with Chief Veddha | Cycling Tour | Partake in the Animal Monitoring Project | Jungle Night Walk to Spot Nocturnal Neighbors | Day8 Gal Oya to Maduru Oya Morning Transfer to Maduru Oya. Arrival at the Pre-Agreed Meeting Location in Henanigala (11:30 AM). Greetings by ‘Aadivasi (Vedda Community) of Henanigala’ and Walk in the Forests to Experience the Traditional Livelihood of Vedda People. Drive to Explorer by Mahoora Campsite Situated Inside the Maduru Oya National Park in a 4WD Safari Jeep. Lunch at Campsite. Evening Jeep Safari at Maduru Oya National Park (03:00 PM). Encounter Traditional Practices of Aadivasi Prior to Dinner. Enjoy a BBQ Dinner Around a Campfire. Overnight Stay in Maduru Oya. Day9 Maduru Oya Early Morning Go on a Safari at Maduru Oya National Park (05:30 AM). Enjoy a Picnic Style Breakfast During the Safari. Later Visit the Fishing Village inside the Maduru Oya National Park – Pick Day’s Catch! Return to the Campsite by Mid-Afternoon. Lunch at the Campsite. Evening Jeep Safari at Maduru Oya National Park. Overnight Stay in Maduru Oya. Day10 Maduru Oya to Dambulla Relax at the Campsite and Enjoy Breakfast Prior to Leave. Transfer to Dambulla. Visit to Dambulla Cave Temple. Check-Into Your Eco Retreat. Refresh and Relax at Your Own. Overnight Stay in Dambulla. Day11 Dambulla Early Morning Climb to Pidurangala Rock to Witness Stunning Views of Sunrise! Enjoy Picnic Breakfast on the Top of the Rock. Explore the Countryside of Sigiriya. Meet Locals and Partake in Local Experiences. Evening Experience Jungle Kayaking. Overnight Stay in Dambulla. Day12 Dambulla Spend Full Day at Leisure Relaxing and Enjoying Hotel Facilities. Your May Experience the Activities Onsite Walk on the ‘’Healing Path’’ Bird Watching Take time to draw, paint, sketch at Laki’s studio. Overnight Stay in Dambulla. Day13 Dambulla to Makandura Morning Exploration of Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Transfer to Makandura. Check-Into Your Resort and Relax. Alternatively, You Can Spend the Day Time Experiencing Following Activities Bird-Watching | Countryside Cycling. Overnight Stay in Makandura. Day14 Makandura to BIA Transfer to Airport for Flight Home! Journey Highlights Trip length: 14 Days Embrace luxury and serenity together during your stay at Glenross Living. Step off the beaten tracks while whispering the wilderness stories of wind in Belihuloya. Cruise through Sri Lanka’s best kept secret – Gal Oya and Senanayake Samudraya to witness elephant swim and roam. Enjoy a torchlight trail with a naturalist to meet Gal Oya’s mysterious nocturnal residents. Discover the wild frontier of Maduru Oya and spot majestic wildlife there. Let yourself indulge in the spiritual silence above Sigiriya’s shadow with the climb of Pidurangala Rock. Spend an overnight at a poetic hideaway in a forest. Places you will stay See available hotels Ahaspokuna Bushwalk Camp Belihuloya Gal Oya Lodge Gal oya Diyabubula, the Barberyn Art & Jungle Hideaway Dambulla The Notary’s House Makandura Glenross Living Kalutara Explorer by Mahoora Ampara See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Though children of all ages are welcome to partake in this adventure, highly recommended for anyone from 16 and above and certain walks are not ideal for little ones. This tour focusses more into rustic stays and off-grid paths – so don’t forget your digital must-haves and a power bank to stay connected with the outside world, when you need to. It will be wise to wear long-sleeved shirts during the dusk and dawn to be safe from mosquitoes and other insects. Feel free to bring your own insect repellent, or try locally available Citronella oil to stay bite-free. No bright clothes during safaris! Stick to neutral colors, wear closed shoes, dust scarf, bring sunscreen and binoculars. A lip balm, a headlamp and a light raincoat will come in handy when trekking, just in case. Plan your holiday during March to September with warm temperatures and lower rainfall to enjoy the best in this route. FAQ Q: Do I have access to swimming pools at these chosen properties? Certain properties like Glenross Living, Gal Oya Lodge, Diyabubula, The Barberyn Art and Jungle Hideaway and the Notary’s House do come with a swimming pool. On the same note, bathing pools are available in certain destinations like Belihuloya. Q: Will I be able to communicate with the outside world once I check-into these rustic hideaways? Yes, limited mobile coverage will be available. But there are CDMA lines which clients can use to contact family etc. in case of emergency. Q: Are the roads to these properties or excursions fine for all the vehicles? Mostly yes. However, a few properties like Ahas Pokuna, do offer alternative transportation options such as jeep or tuk-tuk services to reach the premises. Sometimes, road conditions may appear poor due to heavy rains which should be expected as after all, this is a getaway into the heart of nature! Q: Is there air-conditioning rooms or tents in each property? While the rooms, suites or cabanas in Glenross Living, Diyabubula and The Notary’s House are equipped with air conditioning; some tented accommodations only offer fans. This is often due to the minimal need for air conditioning and the nature of mobile camping, where fewer facilities are available. Q: What should be in my packing list? Pack light and only bring essentials for your stay as follows: Clothing Essentials: Light, neutral-colored clothes Long trousers, track-bottom pants and comfy shorts T-shirts or breathable cotton shirts Sun hat for daytime walks Footwear: Trekking or walking shoes with good grip Comfortable sandals and covered shoes for evenings Personal Care: Sunscreen (SPF 30 or higher) Insect repellent Leech socks Useful Extras: Binoculars Dust scarf or light jacket for variable weather Weather Prep: Pack for warm, sunny, rainy, and windy conditions

itineraries.title: Healthy Holiday with Anasa Wellness Resort
itineraries.summary: This wellness-focused itinerary offers rejuvenating experiences including yoga, meditation, and holistic treatments at Anasa Wellness Resort amidst Ella’s tranquil mountain surroundings and natural beauty.
itineraries.detail_title: Healthy Holiday with Anasa Wellness Resort
itineraries.detail_text: Healthy Holiday with Anasa Wellness Resort If you are looking for a holistic experience in the heart of Sri Lanka, step into Anasa Wellness as it will offer you an essence of tranquility, life-enhancing yoga and wellness practices, fruitful food habits and mindful living while being away from alcohol and tobacco. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka is known for its ancient healing traditions passed down through generations and spiritual experiences and currently ranks well in global wellness tourism. Currently, holistic experiences that nurture both body and mind are widely available in Sri Lanka, along with the facilities required, like Ayurveda and yoga, for forest bathing and mindful retreats. If anyone is looking for a detox, rejuvenation, weight loss or simply a peaceful reset, Sri Lanka invites you to connect yourself with the best healing treatments, together with authentic products and guidance. This country is perfect for wellness seekers, as it’s rich with Ayurveda, meditation, yoga, organic food and tranquil settings across the island. The retreats like Santani in Digana, Ulpotha in Anuradhapura and Talalla Retreat on the Southern Coast are ideal for a meaningful experience blended with nature, luxury and tradition. Just imagine waking up to misty hills, meditating while listening to the lovely songs of birds, and partaking in ayurveda and herbal treatments while listening to the flowing sounds of streams! itineraries.title: Tour on Leopards Trails in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: This wildlife-focused itinerary offers specialized leopard tracking experiences in Yala and Wilpattu national parks, providing exceptional opportunities to observe Sri Lanka’s iconic big cats in their natural habitat.
itineraries.detail_title: Tour on Leopards Trails in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Tour on Leopards Trails in Sri Lanka This is an exclusive itinerary which we have designed to let our clients discover the ‘leopard trails’ of Sri Lanka, featuring the top four national parks where Leopards are often seen. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Leopards are among the most captivating big cats in the wild and they have own way of stealing hearts. They are stunningly beautiful, elusive and one of a kind. With their golden coats dappled in rosette-shaped spots, they move through the wild like living art and also making every sighting feel like a rare privilege anyone. Wildlife lovers and specially the wildlife photographers are drawn to their graceful movements as well as powerful hunting skills in their natural habitats. Sri Lanka is lucky to call a rare subspecies its own: the Sri Lankan leopard, Panthera pardus kotiya. You won’t find them anywhere else on Earth. They roam freely here, unchallenged by lions or tigers, making this island one of the finest places in the world to see leopards in the wild. Here’s the exciting part – Sri Lanka isn’t just good for leopard sightings, the island has earned global recognition as one of the top places on earth to see these majestic cats in the wild. What makes Sri Lanka truly special is that its leopards reign supreme. With no lions or tigers to compete with, they roam with ease and confidence, often appearing more relaxed and visible than in other parts of the world. Yala National Park is leading being home to the highest leopard density in the world. That means your chances of spotting one – especially during daylight hours are better here than anywhere else, but again depending on the twist of your luck. The numbers speak for themselves, as around 800 mature leopards are estimated to live across the island while major national parks boast numbers as Yala’s Block 1 alone boasts up to 21.7 leopards per 100 km². Wilpattu follows with 16.2 and Horton Plains with 11.7. Kumana, Sri Lanka’s rising star – now ranks second globally in leopard density. For this itinerary, we have handpicked four of the best national parks where you will have the highest chances of spotting leopards; Wilpattu National Park Yala National Park Kumana National Park Horton Plains Best time to see leopards in each park: National Park Best Time to Visit Notes Yala February to August Closed in September–October due to monsoon Wilpattu February to September Dry season draws wildlife to waterholes Kumana May to September Active around swamps and estuaries Horton Plains Year-round (esp. dry spells) Sightings near World’s End Road Do not hesitate to speak to one of our Travel Designers to start planning your customized Leopard journey in Sri Lanka, that brings you face-to-face with the elusive beauty of Sri Lanka’s leopards, and the magic of the island’s untamed wilderness. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Wilpattu Pickup and Assistance at the Airport. Transfer to Wilpattu. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Overnight stay in Wilpattu. Day2 Wilpattu Today head out for a full-day safari in Wilpattu – Sri Lanka’s largest and oldest national park! It’s a quiet, less touristy spot where you might just catch a glimpse of a leopard in the wild. Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Picnic Lunch. Overnight stay in Wilpattu. Day 3 Wilpattu Morning Jeep Safari in Wilpattu National Park. Relax at The Campsite. Evening Enjoy a Guided Bush Walks. Experience Private Bush Dinner. Overnight stay in Wilpattu. Day 4 Wilpattu to Habarana Transfer to Habarana. Experience a Sri Lankan Village Tour. Evening Go on a Jeep Safari in Minneriya/Kaudulla or Hurulu Eco Park, which is popular for spotting elephant gathering depending on the period of your travels. Overnight stay in Habarana. Day5 Habarana to Kandy Morning Climb and Explore Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Transfer to Kandy. En Route Visit to Nalanda Gedige. Evening Witness Cultural Dance Show. Overnight stay in Kandy. Day6 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Morning Pay a Visit to Tooth Relic Temple of Kandy. Transfer to Nuwara Eliya. En Route Stop at Ramboda Waterfall for Photographic Opportunities. Visit a Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory and Experience Tea Tasting and Tea Plucking with Local Tamil Tea Workers. Overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day7 Nuwara Eliya Morning Trek to Horton Plains and World’s End. Discover the Diverse Collection of Flora and Fauna Endemic to the Region. Relax at The Hotel Enjoying the Facilities. Overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day8 Nuwara Eliya to Panama Morning Transfer to Panama. En Route Stop for Refreshments and Photography. Check-Into the Lodge and Relax. Overnight stay in Panama. Day9 Panama Transfer to Kumana. Go on a Full Day Safari in Kumana National Park, where a peaceful, experience is guaranteed when comparing with other national parks. This national park is popular for birds, wild elephants, and leopards. Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Picnic Lunch. Overnight stay in Panama. Day10 Panama Transfer to Kumana. Hop on a Jeep to Thrill Yourself with Another Full Day Safari Experience in Kumana National Park. Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Picnic Lunch. Overnight stay in Panama. Day11 Panama to Yala Morning Transfer to Yala. Afternoon, enjoy a Jeep Safari in Bundala National – one of the best national parks to spot migratory waterbirds, hosting over 200 bird species. Overnight stay in Yala. Day12 Yala Head out today, for a Full Day Safari experience in Yala National Park, which is the country’s most visited and second-largest national park. This national park is famed for having one of the highest leopard densities in the world, making it a premier destination for wildlife enthusiasts. Enjoy an Authentic Rice and Curry Lunch at a Shady Place Inside the Park. After The Safari, Refresh and Relax at The Campsite. Overnight stay in Yala. Day 13 Yala Hop on a Jeep to Thrill Yourself with Another Full Day Safari Experience in Yala National Park. Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Picnic Lunch. Indulge in a Mouth-Watering BBQ Dinner by a Roaring Campfire. Overnight stay in Yala. Day 14 Yala to Galle Transfer to Galle. Evening Explore the Ramparts of Galle Fort and City. Enjoy Hotel Facilities. Overnight stay in Galle. Day 15 Galle to BIA Airport Transfer Journey Highlights Trip length: 15 Days Get ready to explore Sri Lanka’s largest and oldest national park – Wilpattu. Step off the beaten path into Wilpattu’s wild heart to appreciate the hidden wonders of nature, with a naturalist. Spot leopards in Horton Plains, which is a rare, but if you’re lucky enough to catch even one, it’s breath-taking and utterly unforgettable moment. Spend two full days in Yala national park, where tuskers roam and leopards’ reign. Discover Kumana – a haven where silence speaks through paw prints and migratory melodies. Unwind after your safari in Yala, with a cozy bonfire – under a starlit sky. Places you will stay See available hotels Hanthana Boutique Villa by Amaya Hanthana W15 Lake Gregory Nuwara Eliya Wild Edge Panama Panama Leopard Trails Yala Yala The Galle Fort Hotel Galle Aranya Trails Wilpattu Taru Villas Maia Habarana See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Early morning safaris are recommended, as they do offer the best chances of spotting wildlife like leopards and birds. Avoid loud noises for better opportunities. At Horton Plains, trekking is involved – so be sure to trek while keeping an eye out for signs like paw prints. Pack light, but smart! It is wise to include binoculars, sunscreen, insect repellent, and neutral-colored clothing in your luggage. Properties like Wild Edge in Panama are rustic, and be ready to accept it, as the prime plan of this tour is following leopard trails. Spice Alert! Sri Lankan food can be fiery. If you are sensitive, ask for “mild” or “less spicy” when ordering food, even at hotels or outside eateries. Don’t forget to get a local SIM card when you arrive, as signal can be weak near national parks or remote areas you will be exploring with this plan. FAQ Q: Are there any precautions clients should take regarding leeches at Aranya Trails in Wilpattu? Due to the lush and forested terrain in the that area, leeches can be present, but not all the time and there are leech protections available in case required. Q: Do any of the properties, such as Galle Fort Hotel or Taru Villas Maia, offer laundry services? Yes, laundry services are available for stays of two nights or more. Q: Could you please confirm whether Leopard Trails, Aranya Trails, and Wild Edge Panama have a pool? Both Aranya Trails and Leopard Trails offer select tents with private plunge pools. However, Wild Edge Panama does not have swimming pool facilities. Q: How about the network availability at the properties or national parks proposed in this itinerary? You will have to expect limited connectivity when you are on safaris inside the national parks. But the chosen properties do have Wi-Fi or mobile coverage, so it’s best to have a Mobitel or Dialog SIM with you before starting the tour. Q: What is the best time of day to see wildlife in the suggested national parks? Early mornings and the hours preceding sunset are ideal times to view wildlife. Usually, we call them as golden hours.

itineraries.detail_text: Spend Your Holiday in the Homes of Authentic Sri Lankan Hospitality Get inspired not only by the true Sri Lankan hospitality, but also by the stunning surroundings and untouched getaways of this teardrop-shaped island, while staying at a few of the top-rated Jetwing properties scattered across Sri Lanka. This curated round tour features a selection of fantastic Jetwing Hotels – from legendary cultural triangle, misty mountains to sun-kissed shores – each offering a sanctuary of comfort, culture, and legendary Sri Lankan hospitality. Experience luxurious stays that harmonize with their surroundings, indulge in signature dining, and feel truly at home with the warmth of Jetwing’s heartfelt service. 1. Overnight Stay in Yala Optional Excursions Temple Tour Optional Resort Facilities Spa and Wellness Signature Dining Day13 Yala to Galle Transfer to the Maritime City of Galle in the South Explore the Galle Dutch Fort and Old Colonial Town Overnight Stay in Galle Day14 Galle Take Your Last Day to Spend in the Waters of Galle or Unawatuna White Sand Beaches Walk along the Sandstone Reefs and Rock Pools by the Coastal Trunk Overnight Stay in Galle Optional Excursions Firsthand Insight to Cinnamon Cultivation Deep Sea Fishing Inland Boat Safari Whale and Dolphin Watching Tour Learn to Carve or Paint a Traditional Sri Lankan Mask with a Local Artist Optional Resort Facilities Yoga Spa Pool Table 02 Swimming Pools and Kiddies Pool Signature Dining Day15 Galle to Colombo Enjoy a Late Breakfast After a Restful Overnight Sleep and Transfer to Colombo, the Capital of Sri Lanka On Your way to Colombo, Boat Safari in Madu River in the Coastal South. FAQ Q. Are children allowed to stay at Jetwing Hotels? Generally, children of all the ages are welcome at Jetwing Hotels.
