From April to October, is the best time, with peak conditions between June and August. Q. What would be the perfect period to visit Yala National Park for better wildlife sightings? Best time is during the dry season, which is from June to September. Q. What are the best locations in Sri Lanka to find the street food? Galle Face Green in Colombo is a top spot to try street food in Sri Lanka. In addition, Jaffna, Kandy, Ella, and Trincomalee also offer unique regional flavors.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Tour with a Baby
itineraries.summary: This family-friendly itinerary offers comfortable, baby-safe travel experiences with child-friendly accommodations and gentle activities suitable for families traveling with infants and young children throughout Sri Lanka.
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Tour With A Baby
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Tour With A Baby This 15-day itinerary is thoughtfully designed for families traveling with babies and young children, ensuring comfort, safety, and a genuinely memorable Sri Lankan adventure. Stay at family-friendly luxury resorts, experience gentle wildlife encounters, and immerse yourself in the tropical paradise’s celebrated hospitality, all at your family’s pace. Discover Sri Lanka’s diverse wonders—from ancient citadels and misty hills to sandy beaches and serene retreats—while enjoying personalized service, child-friendly amenities, and flexibility for restful, happy family moments. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Experience the best of Sri Lanka with handpicked luxury stays in six beautiful regions, each offering unique scenery and activities tailored for families with babies: 1. The Wallawwa, Kotugoda – An elegant colonial manor close to the airport, perfect for easing into your adventure and recovering from jetlag. 2. Water Garden, Sigiriya – A luxurious sanctuary amid the ancient wonders of the Cultural Triangle. 3. Ellerton Bungalow, Kandy – Tranquil hillside escape, ideal for relaxation and exploring Kandy’s cultural hotspots. 4. 98 Acres Resort and Spa, Ella – Breathtaking mountain vistas and leisure, with spacious grounds and baby-friendly services. 5.Jetwing Yala, Yala – Family comfort meets wilderness, with close access to Yala National Park for gentle wildlife experiences. 6.Anantara Peace Haven Resort and Spa, Tangalle – Blissful beachside luxury, offering endless relaxation and child-friendly activities. Your journey begins with a smooth arrival in Sri Lanka, assisted airport pickup, and a restful stay at The Wallawwa. Each destination is chosen for its family-friendly atmosphere, with structured travel between regions allowing ample rest and flexibility for naps, feeding, and baby routine. Unwind in scenic surroundings—climb ancient fortresses, glimpse wild elephants, and relax on golden beaches, all while your needs as a family are front and center. Highlights include jeep safaris tailored for little ones, cultural excursions with gentle guides, and days purposefully unscheduled to allow pure family downtime. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Kotugoda Arrive in Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport Transfer to Kotugoda Check-Into Your Hotel and Recover Any Jetlag Overnight Stay in Kotugoda Day2 Kotugoda Today Remain Unscheduled, Allowing You to Spend Time with Your Partner and Kids Alternatively, You Can Do Some Excursions with Your Baby Sunset Boat Safari in Muthurajawela Wetland (Optional) Overnight Stay in Kotugoda Day 3 Kotugoda to Sigiriya Transfer to Sigiriya En Route Visit to Elephant Freedom Project Join Fixing Elephant Bed, Walking Elephants & Giving Them Baths with Fellow Travelers Continue Your Drive to Sigiriya Where You Spend Overnight Overnight Stay in Sigiriya Note – The memory of this activity will probably slip away from your baby as he/she growsup, but the memory of how he/she was excited around these giant elephants will neverforget! Day 4 Sigiriya The Day Unscheduled. You Can Climb and Explore Sigiriya Rock Fortress if You Don’t Mind Climbing a Few Steps with a Baby! Spend Rest of the Day Enjoying Hotel Facilities Overnight Stay in Sigiriya Note – If you find Sigiriya Rock Fortress too challenging with a baby, you may visit Polonnaruwa Ancient City; Anuradhapura Ancient City or Ritigala Monastery instead. Day5 Sigiriya to Kandy Morning is Free in Sigiriya! Alternative Options – Dambulla Cave Temple | Sigiriya Village Tour Late Morning Transfer to Kandy En Route Stop Nalanda Gedige Overnight Stay in Kandy Note – You will have to climb 300-400 steps to reach Dambulla Cave Temple top. Day6 Kandy Full Day Remains Unplanned to Relax at Your Hotel with Your Baby Opt in Taking a Tour to Explore Following! Temple of the Tooth | Peradeniya Botanical Garden | Bahirawakanda Temple | Tuk-Tuk Ride Around Kandy City and Lake Overnight Stay in Kandy Note – All the Mentioned Attractions are Located within Close Proximity to Your Hotel. Please Inform your chauffeur what you would like to do and where you wish to do, he would be happy to assist you anytime! Day7 Kandy to Ella Morning Transfer to Ella En Route Make a Stop at Ramboda and Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice & Curry Lunch Viewing the Picturesque Ramboda Waterfall Check-Into the Hotel and Refresh Relax and Enjoy the Hotel Facilities with Your Baby! Overnight Stay in Ella Day8 Ella Leisure Day in Ella! Enjoy Resort Facilities and Stunning Views of Surrounding Optionally, You Can: Trek to Little Adam’s Peak Visit 9 Arch Bridge Take a Cooking Class to Learn Few of Traditional Sri Lankan Dishes Tour a Tea Plantation and a Factory Overnight Stay in Ella Note – All the above-mentioned excursions are perfectly safe and practical with a baby. Please let your guide know how you would like to spend the day and he will make the necessary arrangements accordingly! Day9 Ella to Yala Transfer to Yala En Route Witness Ravana Waterfall Pay a Visit to Buduruwagala Temple Check-Into Your Family Hotel and Relax Overnight Stay in Yala Day10 Yala Spend the Morning at Leisure Afternoon Enjoy a Jeep Safari with Your Baby in Yala National Park Dine in the Dune with a Bottle of Sparkling Wine or a Cocktail Overnight Stay in Yala Note – The roads in the park are pretty bumpy, so you may feel more secure to strap in your little one, so feel free to organize a car seat for your child with your tour operator. Day11 Yala to Thangalle Transfer to Tangalle – Just to Enjoy Sandy Beach and Beautiful Sunsets! Check-Into Your Kid-Friendly Beach Resort Rest and Enjoy Resort Facilities with Your Baby Overnight Stay in Tangalle Day12 – 14 Tangalle Three Full Days Remain Unscheduled. It’s all About Resting Your Feet and Just Chill on the Beach! Enjoy Resort Facilities Overnight Stay in Tangalle Note – The Vehicle and Chauffer will be at Your Disposal within these 03 Days. You can discuss with him and Schedule to go and Explore Tangalle Outdoors; enjoy some fresh seafood at nearby restaurants; Do an Excursion to Galle; etc. Day15 Tangalle to BIA Transfer by Airport Hope you had a wonderful time with your baby and partner Have a safe flight back home! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 15 Days Ideal Timing: November to April Family First: Activities planned with baby safety and comfort in mind—strollers, car seats, and slow-paced transfers. Flexible Exploration: Options for gentle adventures—boat safaris, wildlife sanctuaries, tea estates, and scenic train rides—all adaptable based on family preferences. Luxury Rest Stops: Each hotel/resort provides special amenities for families, including cribs, healthy cuisine, and baby-friendly spaces. Personal Chauffeur: Your own vehicle and driver throughout the journey, ready to accommodate your family’s schedule. Places you will stay See available hotels The Wallawwa Kotugoda Ellerton Kandy 98 Acres Resort Ella Jetwing Yala Yala Anantara Peace Haven Tangalle Water Garden Sigiriya Sigiriya See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Family First: Activities planned with baby safety and comfort in mind—strollers, car seats, and slow-paced transfers. Flexible Exploration: Options for gentle adventures—boat safaris, wildlife sanctuaries, tea estates, and scenic train rides—all adaptable based on family preferences. Luxury Rest Stops: Each hotel/resort provides special amenities for families, including cribs, healthy cuisine, and baby-friendly spaces. Personal Chauffeur: Your own vehicle and driver throughout the journey, ready to accommodate your family’s schedule. FAQ Q. When is the best time for this trip? November to April for the best weather. Q. Are activities safe with babies? All excursions are selected for safety and practicality with infants. Q. Can I customize day plans? Yes! Chauffeur and guides are flexible to meet your family’s needs. Q. Are special children’s meals available? Most luxury hotels offer kid’s menus and can cater to dietary requirements. Q. Is child care available? Many hotels provide babysitting on request—enquire during booking.

itineraries.title: Experience True Elegance and Luxury with W15 Collection
itineraries.summary: This luxury itinerary offers exclusive stays at W15 properties, providing premium experiences with sophisticated accommodations, personalized services, and access to unique attractions across curated destinations.
itineraries.detail_title: Experience True Elegance and Luxury with W15 Collection
itineraries.detail_text: Experience True Elegance and Luxury with W15 Collection It’s time to indulge in an all-inclusive experience and enjoy the exceptional hospitality of the renowned W15 Collection, guided by their ethos of turning ‘moments into memories’. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Immerse yourself in the W15 luxury boutique collection as they have six elegance properties locates in some of the most stunning regions of Sri Lanka and in this itinerary, we have chosen only four properties of them to let you explore the beauty of Sri Lanka while experiencing a world class service. W15 Headquarters Ideal place to appreciate the panoramic views of the Colombo city while sipping a cocktail with selected Sri Lanka’s Street food, at the rooftop restaurant and bar. W15 Hanthana Estate Nestled just a few minutes away from Kandy, blessed with colonial charm and magnificent scenery making it the perfect gateway for anyone seeking for a luxurious unwinding experience. W15 Ambuluwawa A hidden gem perched closer to the iconic Ambuluwawa Tower with sweeping vistas. Would be best for adventurers and romantics. W15 Lake Gregory A tranquil setting locates overlooking the popular Lake Gregory in the heart of ‘Little England’. W15 Weligama Beachfront resort which has received the attractions of ocean lovers and young travelers alike. The journey will begin with a restful overnight stay in Colombo. Get a fresh start to your adventure as you continue your drive to Hanthana, for a few relaxing days while also partaking in nature-based activities. Along the way, make quick stop at the sacred tooth relic temple and royal botanical gardens in Kandy. Next head to Gampola to explore the Ambuluwawa Tower and enjoy some outdoor activities, all while enjoying the comforts of the luxurious amenities of W15 Ambuluwawa – the newest addition to their collection. The adventure moves to Nuwara Eliya, with visits to waterfalls, tea plantations, and colonial landmarks. Before driving towards Weligama, which is the final leg where you will spend relaxing few days on pristine beaches of Weligama, take a scenic train ride to Ella and a visit to the iconic Nine Arches Bridge. The trip concludes with a transfer back to the airport, leaving you with cherished memories this tropical island with the warmth hospitality led by W15 Collection. Ready to experience Sri Lanka like never before? Speak to one of our travel designers to create your personalized itinerary, featuring W15 signature stays. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Colombo Upon Your Arrival on the Island, One of Our Representatives will Meet You at the Bandaranaike International Airport. Packed Too Many Suitcases? Don’t Worry! Luggage Assistance is Part of the Service. You will get driven to the Multi-Cultural City of Colombo, where your Journey Begins. Treat yourself to an unforgettable dinner by stepping into Headquarters by W15, which locates in the heart of Colombo’s spirited soul. It’s time to relax and sleep off any jet lag with an Overnight Stay in Colombo. Day2 Colombo to Hanthana After a Good Night’s Sleep and an Early Breakfast, Transfer to Hanthana – Salubrious Climes of Sri Lanka’s Hill Country Along the Way to Hanthana, Take Blessings at Kandy Temple, which Enshrines the Most Sacred Tooth Relic of the Lord Buddha Check-Into W15 Hanthana Estate, a Beautifully Restored Bungalow from the Heyday of the British Empire. Spend the Evening at Leisure. Overnight Stay in Hanthana Day 3 & 4 Hanthana Spend Couple of Days Taking in the Sights and Multitude of Adventures Ranging from Hikes, Bird Watching or a Game of Croquet on the Sun-Drenched Lawn Alternatively, you may Unwind, take a Dip in the Pool, or Spend time Simply Exploring the Property Overnight Stay in Hanthana Optional Excursions Exploring the Wilderness Charming Life in the Hills E-Biking Meditation Countryside Temple Visit Bird Watching Indiana Jones Tour Cooking Demonstration Open-Top Defender Ride High-Tea Experience Optional Resort Facilities Piano Room Infinity Pool Garden Games Library Personal Spa Day 5 Gampola Begin Transfer to Ambuluwawa – Another Peaceful Destination Ideal to be Away from the Hustle and Bustle of the City Visit Ambuluwawa Tower, which is a breathtaking spiral tower which offers a thrilling climb and panoramic views of the highlands. Overnight Stay in Gampola Day 6 & 7 Gampola Enjoy the surrounding natural beauty of Gampola within next couple of days while partaking in available experiences or enjoying the resort facilities W15 Ambuluwawa is one of the perfect places to indulge in luxury while reconnecting with the nature in the highlands. The elevation of Ambuluwawa is a highlight as it results in offering amazing weather changes within just seconds, from clean & clear skies to fog-kissed environment. Overnight Stay in Gampola Optional Excursions Bird Watching Nature Walks Ambuluwawa Adventure Park Star Gazing Picnic Fishing High-Tea Experience Take a Serene Paddle Boat Ride on Ambuluwawa Lake Exhilarating Ambuluwawa Swing Optional Resort Facilities Piano Room Infinity Pool Garden Games Library Italian Al Fresco Dining Experience Day 8 Gampola to Nuwara Eliya Morning Venture Further into the Hills of Nuwara Eliya which Mimicked Life Back in England. En Route, Sightseeing at Ramboda Waterfall. Stop at a Tea Factory & Tea Plantation and Watch Tea Gets Processed. Sample Some of the Best Tea You Have Ever Had! Grab Some Bicycles or Take a Walk in Little England, Where You Can Explore the Remnants of a Bygone Era. Unwind in the Green Lawns of Victoria Park; Relive the Horseracing Heritage of Nuwara Eliya; Indulge in a Game of Golf; Visit one of the Oldest Old Post Offices in Sri Lanka or Visit the Buzzing Bale Bazaar/market for a More Localized Experience! Overnight Stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day 9 Nuwara Eliya After a Truly Sri Lankan yet Personalized Breakfast, it’s Time to Explore the Possible Experiences in this Beautiful Hill Station on Your Own or a Guided Tours. Overnight Stay in Nuwara Eliya Optional Excursions Picnic at Pedro Tea Estate Take a Hike to Single Tree Mountain Explore the Dense Montane Forest – Galway National Park Enjoy a Walk or a Boat Ride in/around Lake Gregory Paddle-Boarding or Jet-Skiing Optional Resort Facilities Heated Pool Day 10 Nuwara Eliya to Ella to Weligama Today Take a Scenic Train Journey from Nanu Oya Railway Station to Picturesque Demodara Visit the Bridge in the Sky or the Nine Arches Bridge, a Spectacular Viaduct and Engineering Marvel Set Off Towards Weligama, where the Sun-Kissed Beaches Await! Beach Coming Alive with Music and Color when the Evening Draws Closer. Chill Yourself with Some of the All-Time Favorite Dishes Served by W15 Weligama Like Wood-Fire Pizza, Big Hearty Burghers and Whimsical Cocktails. Overnight Stay in Weligama Day 11, 12 & 13 Weligama Spend Couple of Days in the Crystal Waters of Weligama’s White Sand Beaches! You also have the Opportunity to Discover the Enthralling Wonders of Southern Sri Lanka. Overnight Stay in Weligama Optional Excursions Organic Fruit Farm Visit Coconut Factory Snake Farm Rubber Factory Gem and Jewelry Factory Cinnamon Island Stilt Fisherman Surfing Whale and Dolphin Watching Galle Fort Sea Turtle Conservation Center Optional Resort Facilities Spa Infinity Pool Signature Dining Surf Points and Camps Day14 Weligma to BIA Pick up from the hotel and Ride to the Airport for Your Outbound Flight As We Say Goodbye, We Hope You Will Have Beautiful Memories of the Island to Reminisce and will Visit Again for More! Journey Highlights Trip length: 14 days Visit Ambuluwawa Tower, one of the Insta-famous attractions in the hill country. An all-inclusive stay at W15 Hanthana Estate, a beautifully restored bungalow coupled with top-notch service. Trek to Single Tree Mountain, the tenth highest peak in Sri Lanka. Join a whale and dolphin watching tour to spot two of the big five in Sri Lanka. Places you will stay See available hotels Maniumpathy Colombo W15 Hanthana Estate Kandy W15 Lake Gregory Nuwara Eliya W15 Weligama Weligama W15 Ambuluwawa Gampola See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Recommended to bring a drone with prior plan to get the perfect drone shots or wide-angle photos from Ambuluwawa Tower. Make sure to pack lightweight, breathable fabrics as hiking and trekking experiences are included. Stay hydrated while trekking – it’s even better if you bring a reusable water bottle, which is both practical and eco-friendly. If you are a surf lover, don’t forget to bring your rash guard, board shorts, and reef-safe sunscreen as Weligama beach is calling you! Keep an eye on the station stops like Pattipola, Idalgashinna, Hanthana, Haputale as they are truly scenic. Hoping to play a round of golf? Reserve tee times in advance. FAQ Q. What are the operating hours of Headquarters by W15 Headquarters in Colombo? It is open daily from 1100 hrs. until late, with extended hours on weekends. Q. What does the Indiana Jones trail at W15 Hanthana include? It features a hike through the Hanthana mountain range, along with visits to the filming locations from Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom. It’s a scenic path which also can be done by a jeep or as bike rides too. Q. What’s the child policy of W15 Properties? They welcome travelers of all the ages. 12 years and above will be considered as adults, and additional charges will apply. Q. Is the sea at Weligama rough during the off-season? Yes, during the South-West monsoon month, from May to September, the sea can be rough with strong currents, especially for beginners. Q. What are the adventure activities available at Ambuluwawa Adventure Park? It’s an ideal spot for outdoor adventure seekers as it offers fantastic experiences like Tree-to-Tree Walking Challenge, Suspended Rope Courses, Obstacle Challenges, Rock Climbing, etc.

itineraries.title: Re-discover Luxury With Amaya Retreats
itineraries.summary: Amaya Hotels and Resorts showcases a wonderful collection of properties scattered in different but main regions of the country and with this itinerary, we let you explore six of the main properties..
itineraries.detail_title: Re-discover Luxury With Amaya Retreats
itineraries.detail_text: Re-discover Luxury With Amaya Retreats This 12-day itinerary features a few of the amazing properties that come under a beloved local hospitality brand in Sri Lanka – Amaya. Experience their signature warmth and service as you journey through the country’s most scenic and historic regions; from the cultural triangle, the lush central highlands, the vibrant east coast, and the golden beaches of the south to the city escapes of the west. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Amaya Hotels and Resorts showcases a wonderful collection of properties scattered in different but main regions of the country and with this itinerary, we let you explore six of the main properties as follows: 1. Tri Koggala by Amaya – An eco-friendly property nestled over-looking the stunning shores of Koggala Lake2. Langdale Boutique Hotel by Amaya – A colonial-style hideaway in misty Nuwara Eliya featuring a mini golf and a temperature-controlled pool.3. Hanthana Boutique Villa – A peaceful hill side villa, ideal for anyone looking for a holistic wellness and nature-friendly experience.4. Amaya Beach Hotel – A beachfront paradise on the pristine shores of Pasikudah.5. Amaya Lake Hotel – A perfect gateway to explore Sri Lanka’s cultural triangle.6. The Kingsbury – An Iconic property locates in the heart of Colombo. Your unforgettable island adventure begins with a warm welcome at Bandaranaike International Airport, followed by a relaxing stay at Tri Koggala – a beautiful lakeside sanctuary. Then you will be headed to Langdale by Amaya, in the fog-kissed Nanu Oya which will the home for another few days. In the second phase, take a scenic train ride whisks you to Kandy, where culture and nature intertwine in peaceful harmony. After explore the greenside of the country, it’s time to off to the tropical haven of Pasikudah, for beachfront bliss and aquatic thrills. From there, you journey into the heart of Sri Lanka’s ancient heritage to discover some significant historical sites such as Dambulla Cave Temple, iconic Sigiriya Rock and a rustic village visit to share smiles over clay hearths and woven mats. The final leg brings you to Colombo’s vibrant streets with a memorable stay at urban heartbeat. If you are considering embarking on this extraordinary journey with Blue Lanka Tours, speak to one of our travel designers to start planning your personalized Sri Lanka tour with Amaya’s curated stays ! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Koggala Upon your arrival on the island, one of our representatives will meet you at the Bandaranaike International Airport. Packed too many suitcases? Don’t worry! Luggage assistance is part of the service. You will get driven to Koggala; an escape close to nature! Every Stress or Fatigue that You Might Had Will Melt away once you checked-into this Environmentally-Friendly Oasis of – Tri Koggala by Amaya It’s time to relax and sleep off any jet lag with an Overnight Stay in Koggala. Day2 Koggala Spend the Day While Enjoying the Sustainable Luxury at Tri. You May Simply Sit by the Main Deck, Looking Out Over the Infinity Pool – which is an Insta-Famous Sri Lankan Spot in its Own Right! and Let Your Worries Drift Away Overnight Stay in Koggala. Optional Excursions. Take a Boat Ride Across the Lake to a Cinnamon Island. Cycling Tour Through the Tiny Villages and Scenic Rice Paddies Around Koggala Lake. Visit Handunugoda Tea Estate where Virgin White Tea is Produced. Sail Alongside Whales in Mirissa. Sunset Sail on a Yatch or a Catamaran. Optional Resort Facilities. Library Signature Dining Take a Quantum Yoga Class. Enjoy an Ayurvedic Massage Treatment. Day 3 Koggala to Nanu Oya Following a Mouthwatering Breakfast, Begin Your Journey to Hill Country – Nanu Oya. On Your Way, Make Stops for Refreshments and Photography as You will Drive Passing Some Landmarks – Coconut Tree Hills | Buduruwagala Temple | Ravana Waterfall | Nine Arch Bridge Check-Into Langdale by Amaya, an Old Colonial Bungalow Sit on the Sprawling Tea Estate of Somerset. Evening is Free to Relax. Overnight Stay in Nanu Oya. Day 4 Nanu Oya The Day Remains Unscheduled! You May Either Go Trekking or Just Relax by the Poolside while Enjoying the Beautiful Views of Stunning Mountains Around. Overnight Stay in Nanu Oya. Optional Excursions. Hill Country Train Station Hopping. Sommerset Tea Experience. Nanu Oya Trekking Tour. Explore Little England by a Tuk Tuk. Mountain Biking Adventure. Ramayana Trail Around Nuwara Eliya. Refreshing Trail to Horton Plains & World’s End. Photography Expedition Optional Resort Facilities. Mini Golf Course Tennis Court High Tea. Day 5 Nanu Oya to Kandy Morning Take a Local Train Ride to Kandy from Nanu Oya. Enjoy the Breath-taking Sceneries – Cascading Waterfalls; Green Carpet of Tea Bushes Rolling by with Colorful Sari-Dressed Hindu Tea Pickers Popping Out. Check-Into Hanthana Boutique Villa by Amaya – Another Ideal Place to Take a Break in the Mountains! Overnight stay in Kandy. Day 6 Kandy Spend Your Day at Hanthana Boutique Villa – a Home Away from Home Either Relaxing or Partaking in Some of the Many Activities on Offer! Overnight Stay in Kandy. Optional Excursions: Temple Run Tour Nature Trail Followed by a Picnic at “Hulan Ground” Guided Tour of Kandy – which is All About God, Kings and Common People. Trek to Mystical Mountain of Hanthana. Enjoy Post-Evening Game Drive Cocktails & Canapés on Beautiful Beach Garden Overlooking the Ocean. Optional Resort Facilities: Swimming Pool Fitness Center Rooftop Terrace Day7 Kandy to Pasikudah It’s Time for Some Relaxation on the East Coast Beaches, specially Glistens with Possibilities During European Spring and Summer! On Your Way, Visit Nalanda Gedige, the Central Point of Sri Lanka. Check-Into Amaya Beach – the Pearl of Iconic Pasikudah in East Sri Lanka. Here, you will find a Great Setting to Sit Back and Relax as You Have the Inviting Ocean on Your Doorstep! Overnight stay in Pasikudah. Day8 Pasikudah Spend Full Day Completely Unwinding on the Beaches of Pasikudah! If You’re Feeling Active, Consider Available Watersports or Excursions and Break the Boredom. Overnight Stay in Pasikudah. Optional Excursions: Watersports (Jet Skiing; Banana Boat Ride; Diving; Snorkeling) Kayaking and Canoeing. Speed Boat and Glass Boat Rides. Fishing Trip. Whale and Dolphin Watching Tour. Sunset Cruise on a Private Yatch. Optional Resort Facilities: Swimming Pool Fitness Center Signature Dining Spa Day 9 Pasikudah to Dambulla Morning You will be Moved to Dambulla, Heart of Island’s Cultural Triangle. En Route to Dambulla, Explore Polonnaruwa, the Thriving Commercial and Religious Center of Sri Lanka Some Hundreds of Years Ago. Check-Into Amaya Lake, Set on the Banks of Kandalama Lake with Glistening Views Tranquil Woodland. Evening is Free to Relax and Enjoy the Peaceful Environment. Overnight Stay in Dambulla. Day10 Dambulla In the Morning, Explore the Fascinating Lion Rock of Sigiriya. Discover Authentic Charm of Sri Lankan Rural Life by Hopping into a Bullock Cart. Take a Scenic Catamaran Ride; Walk Through Lush Paddy Files and Savor a Traditional Meal at a Village House Before Wrapping up Your Sojourn in this Village. Evening, Indulge in Some Me-Time at the Hotel. Overnight Stay in Dambulla. Optional Excursions: Sunset Barge Boat Ride Amuna Experience Jeep Safari in Minneriya or Kaudulla National Park Optional Resort Facilities: Swimming Pool Yoga Pavilion Signature Dining Spa Day11 Dambulla to Colombo Morning Transfer to Colombo, the ‘Garden City of Asia’. En Route, Pay a Visit to Dambulla’s Ancient Cave Temple. Arrive in Colombo, Take a City Walk into Fort and Pettah Markets; Explore Temples; Kovils and Other Historic Areas; Sip Sunset Cocktails at Galle Face Hotel Before Checking-Into the Hotel. Overnight stay in Colombo Day12 Colombo to BIA Pick up from the hotel and Ride to the Airport for Your Outbound Flight As We Say Goodbye, We Hope You Will Have Beautiful Memories of the Island to Reminisce and will Visit Again for More! Journey Highlights Trip length: 12 Days Discover the art of harvesting one of Sri Lanka’s most iconic crops – Cinnamon, which the island proudly produces over 75% of for the entire world. Appreciate the stunning views from the tranquil landscapes in Hanthana Mountain Range that awaits you. Rediscover yourself with Ayurvedic and holistic healings. Learn some sailing techniques during your Yacht experience, as the sun beams down. Places you will stay See available hotels Tri Koggala by Amaya Koggala Langdale by Amaya Nuwara Eliya Hanthana Boutique Villa by Amaya Hanthana The Kingsbury Colombo Amaya Beach Pasikuda Amaya Lake Dambulla See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Arrive early when visiting the Handunugoda Tea Estate, to learn about the tea plucking and production process during the working hours. As the weather can shift quickly, and the trail includes uneven terrains, make sure to wear layers and a good pair of comfortable shoes when trekking through the Horton Plains. Dress respectfully before entering sacred places. Recommended to pack motion sickness remedies, as the sea can be choppy if you are partaking in any boat tours during the stay in Pasikudah. Minneriya is famous for elephant gathering and better to visit during dry season from July to October, for better sighting. FAQ Q. What is the best time to do whale watching in Pasikudah? Whale and dolphin watching takes place in Trincomalee, which locates around 02.5 hours away from Pasikudah itself. The ideal time would be from the end of March till end of June. Q. What kind of clothing and gear should I pack when staying in Kandy and Nuwara Eliya? You may pack light cotton clothing, a light jacket, a sweater or fleece, rain gear, and closed shoes. Don’t forget a hat and sunscreen for daytime outings and a camera to capture the scenic beauty. Q. Can I extend my stay at Amaya Lake with wellness experiences? Absolutely! Amaya Lake offers access to the Amuna Ayurveda Retreat, just minutes away. You can extend your stay to enjoy Ayurvedic treatments, yoga, meditation, and healthy cuisine. Q. Will I be able to join a Yacht or do I have to book it on private basis? Both private and shared basis yachts are available. It’s best to check the pricing as well as availability, before booking.

itineraries.title: Off Road Adventure Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Embark on a rugged 15-day expedition across Sri Lanka’s wildest terrains, designed for adventure-seekers and nature lovers.
itineraries.detail_title: Off Road Adventure Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Off Road Adventure Tour in Sri Lanka Embark on a rugged 15-day expedition across Sri Lanka’s wildest terrains, designed for adventure-seekers and nature lovers. Traverse forests, jungles, and sand dunes by 4WD, trek through misty hills, and immerse yourself in authentic village life. End each day with warm hospitality at unique lodges and resorts, seamlessly blending comfort with untamed adventure. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Stay At Adventure-Friendly And Boutique Accommodations Across Stunning Regions Dolphin Beach Resort (Kalpitiya) Seaside comfort on the northwest. Cinnamon Lodge (Habarana) Subtle luxury in the heart of the cultural triangle. Gal Oya Lodge (Gal Oya) Eco-lodge set in unspoiled wilderness. Tip of Ella (Ella) Hill country charm with adventure on your doorstep. The Glenrock, Luxury Nature Resort (Belihuloya) Immersed in the riverside tranquility. Banyan Camp (Udawalawe) Unique, offbeat camp for the wild at heart. Tintagel Hotel (Colombo) Ultimate relaxation before your departure. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Kalpitiya Welcome to Sri Lanka! Pickup and Assistance at the Airport Transfer to Kalpitiya Check-Into Hotel. Relax and Recharge After Your Flight Overnight Stay in Kalpitiya Day2 Kalpitiya Begin Your Off-Road Adventure! Cruising Along the Sand Dunes in Between the Ocean and Lagoon at Kalpitiya Evening Explore Local Attractions in the Area Overnight Stay in Kalpitiya Day 3 Kalpitiya to Habarana Transfer to Habarana En Route Explore Ancient Kingdom of Anuradhapura Evening Explore a Sri Lankan Village by a Classic Landcruiser Overnight Stay in Habarana Day 4 Habarana It’s a Day Full of Adventure! Take Part in a 4 x 4 Safari Through Variable Terrains; Mud, Water, Rocks, Forest, Rivers, Ditches, Hill Climbs and Undergrowth Evening Enjoy Breath-Taking Sun Downs at a Kamatha Overnight Stay in Habarana Day5 Habarana to Gal Oya Morning Exploration of Sigiriya Rock Fortress Transfer to Gal Oya Check-Into the Hotel and Relax Overnight Stay in Gal Oya Day6 Gal Oya Morning Meet Chief Vedda and Take a Walk through the Jungles of His Ancestors Enjoy a Delicious Picnic Lunch Before the 4WD Jeep Adventure Afternoon Hop on a 4X4 Jeep for a Jungle Drive in Gal Oya National Park Overnight Stay in Gal Oya Day7 Gal Oya to Ella Transfer to Ella Visit to Nine Arch Bridge Evening Enjoy an Authentic Culinary Experience Along with Dinner at a Typical Local Place Overnight Stay in Ella Day8 Ella Morning Trek to Little Adam’s Peak Thrill Yourself with a Zip Lining Session in Fastest and Longest Zip Line in Sri Lanka Hop on to a Four-Wheeler and Experience the ATV Discovery Try Air Rifling and Archery Overnight Stay in Ella Day9 Ella to Belihuloya Morning Take a Scenic Train Ride to Haputale from Ella Visit to a Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory Transfer to Belihuloya Take a Walk and Experience a Simple Lifestyle of a Villager Overnight Stay in Belihuloya Day10 Belihuloya Leave the Hotel Early for a Thrilling Adventure Experience 4WD Adventure to Devil’s Staircase Back to the Hotel and Relax Enjoy a BBQ Dinner Overnight Stay in Belihuloya Day11 Belihuloya Early Morning Leave for Another Adventurous Drive! Stop and Witness Bambarakanda Waterfall – Highest Waterfall in Sri Lanka Continue the Ride. Drive up to World’s End via Satan’s Steps Day12 Belihuloya to Udawalawa Transfer to Udawalawe via Kuragala En Route Stop and Witness Duvili Ella Visit to Explore Waulpane Cave Overnight Stay in Udawalawe Day13 Udawalawa Morning Hop on a 4WD Jeep and Leave for a Safari in Udawalawe National Park Enjoy Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Lunch at a Typical Local Place Go On Another Jeep Safari in Udawalawe National Park – You can Get Close to Majestic Creatures There! Overnight Stay in Udawalawe Day14 Udawalawe to Colombo Morning Visit to Elephant Transit Home Transfer to Colombo Check-Into the Hotel and Relax Overnight Stay in Colombo Day16 Colombo to BIA Transfer to Airport for Flight Home! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 15 Days 4WD adventures through jungles and sand dunes. Safari experiences at Minneriya, Udawalawe, and Gal Oya National Parks. Meet Chief Vedda and explore indigenous Sri Lankan culture. Trek to waterfalls: Bambarakanda, World’s End. Zip lining, archery, ATV rides, and nature walks in Ella and Belihuloya. Comfort and cuisine at distinctive eco-resorts. Places you will stay See available hotels Dolphin Beach Ilanthadiya Cinnamon Lodge Habarana Gal Oya Lodge Gal oya The Glenrock Hotel Belihuloya Banyan Camp Udawalawa Tip Top Ella Ella Paradise Road Tintagel Colombo See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Wear sturdy, closed-toe shoes for hikes and excursions. Carry insect repellent and sunscreen for all outdoor activities. Pack rain gear and a warm layer for mountainous and hill regions. Be prepared for early-morning safari departures. Respect local customs when visiting indigenous villages and sacred sites. FAQ Q: What is the physical fitness level required? The tour includes moderate hikes, adventurous drives, and some water activities—generally suitable for anyone with average fitness. Q: Are the safaris suitable for children? Yes, but younger children may find long drives tiring. Check with individual lodges for family-friendly accommodations. Q: What’s the best season for this adventure? November to April is ideal for most destinations, with less rain and more predictable wildlife sightings. Q: Will this tour be done entirely in a 4X4 Jeep or will be utilizing it only for certain excursions? Yes, this tour will be conducted in an air-conditioned car, van or similar vehicle, and 4X4 jeeps will be provided when you partake in exclusive activities which are done in them. Q: What should be in my packing list? In addition to your essential list, below would also be helpful: Clothing and footwear T-shirts which would be Quick-dry Cargo trousers or hiking pants which are light weight Waterproof jacket Trail shoes or hiking boots. Flip flops will also be needed. Adventure Specific Extras Reusable water bottle which comes handy. Dry bags or zip locks Light weight towel or microfiber towel GoPro or action camera, may be with mounts

itineraries.title: Infuse Your Holiday with Local Vibe at Flagship of Cinnamon
itineraries.summary: This 14-day immersive journey showcases the best of Sri Lanka’s heritage, wildlife, and coastline, combining comfort, culture, and adventure.
itineraries.detail_title: Infuse Your Holiday with Local Vibe at Flagship of Cinnamon
itineraries.detail_text: Infuse Your Holiday with Local Vibe at Flagship of Cinnamon This 14-day immersive journey showcases the best of Sri Lanka’s heritage, wildlife, and coastline, combining comfort, culture, and adventure. Enjoy handpicked accommodation at celebrated Cinnamon properties as you travel through the country’s lush hills, ancient cities, golden beaches, and wildlife havens. The itinerary offers personalized experiences, including safaris, village tours, boat rides, and beach leisure, ideal for families or travelers seeking a blend of culture, relaxation, and discovery. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Explore Sri Lanka’s Highlights While Staying at These Renowned Hotels Cinnamon Grand (Colombo) A premier city hotel ideal for a comfortable start. Cinnamon Lodge (Habarana) A tranquil retreat in the cultural triangle. Trinco Blu by Cinnamon (Trincomalee) Beachfront charm on the east coast. Cinnamon Citadel (Kandy) Modern luxury blending with historic heritage. Galway Heights Hotel (Nuwara Eliya) A scenic hill country stay. Cinnamon Wild (Yala) Rustic luxury at Yala’s edge, close to nature. Cinnamon Bentota Beach Iconic, stylish resort on Sri Lanka’s pristine southwest coast. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Colombo Upon your arrival on the island, one of our representatives will meet you at the Bandaranaike International Airport. Packed too many suitcases? Don’t worry! Luggage assistance is part of the service. You will get driven to Colombo, Country’s Capital and Where Your First Accommodation is. Check-Into Cinnamon Grand – One of the most sought-after Hotel and an ideal resting point to start your journey! Overnight Stay in Colombo. Optional – A dining experience at one of the finest restaurants in Cinnamon Grand is recommended. Day2 Colombo to Habarana Following breakfast, Transfer to the quaint village of Habarana On your way to Habarana, Visit Dambulla Rock Cave Temple – Once a Sacred Refuge, now a UNESCO World Heritage Site! Check-in at Cinnamon Lodge – a beautiful setting in the heartland of cultural triangle Overnight Stay in Habarana Optional Excursions: Bird & Butterfly Trail Chena Hut Cooking Experience Loris Watching Primate Watch Optional Resort Facilities: Dine in the Jetty Yoga Champagne Breakfast in the Butterfly Garden Day 3 Habarana Transfer to Habarana En Route Explore Ancient Kingdom of Anuradhapura Evening Explore a Sri Lankan Village by a Classic Landcruiser Overnight Stay in Habarana Day 4 Habarana to Trincomalee Morning Explore the enigmatic Sigiriya Lions Rock Head to Trincomalee and Check-Into Trinco Blu by Cinnamon – a haven set on the sparkling eastern shores where is home for your next couple of days! Spend Rest of the Day overlooking the deep blue sea, get chilled with a beer or two, read a book or just lounge around Overnight Stay in Trincomalee Optional Excursions: Snorkeling at Pigeon Island Marine Mammal Safari Scuba Diving Watersports Explore Trinco by Tuk-Tuk Optional Resort Facilities: Azmaara Spa Beach BBQ Dinner Day5 Trincomalee Spend the Day Relaxing on the Pristine Beaches of Trincomalee! If it’s the right season, you may go on a whale and dolphin watching tour and get close look at amazing Marine giants in the deep sea Overnight Stay in Trincomalee Day6 Trincomalee to Kandy Morning Begin transfer to Kandy, the sacred capital of the central hills. Stop by Nalanda Gedige, the Center Point of Sri Lanka Explore the Spectacular Peradeniya Botanical Garden by a Golf Cart Check-Into Cinnamon Citadel – a perfect place to indulge in Kandy’s ancient heritage blended with modern living! Overnight stay in Kandy. Day7 Kandy Morning Take Blessings at Kandy Tooth Relic Temple Rest of the Day Remains Unscheduled! Indulge in Some Me-Time at the Hotel Overnight Stay in Kandy Optional Excursions: High Tea overlooking the Mahaweli River Mahaweli River Boat Ride Scenic Tour to Knuckles Mountains Day8 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Morning Transfer to the Cooler and Hillier Nuwara Eliya in the Highlands. En Route, Sightseeing at Ramboda Waterfall. Arrive in the Tea Country Nuwara Eliya, where Tea Plantations Stretch for Miles and Miles as far as the Eye Can See. Stop at a Tea Factory & Tea Plantation and Watch Tea Gets Processed. Sample Some of the Best Tea You Have Ever Had! Evening Discover Little England. Overnight Stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day9 Nuwara Eliya to Yala Start the Scenic Train Journey from Nanu Oya Railway Station to Picturesque Ella Visit the Bridge in the Sky or the Nine Arches Bridge, a Spectacular Viaduct and Engineering Marvel Transfer to Yala in the Southeast of the Island Check-Into Cinnamon Wild – an eco-friendly rustic retreat on the edge of Yala National Park Overnight Stay in Yala Optional Excursions: Dining in the Dark Dine in the Wild Guided Nature Walk Morning with Dairy Farmer Three Lake Revisited Optional Resort Facilities: AV Room Swimming Pool Day10 Yala Today you may go on a full day safari in Yala, the Most Popular National Park in Sri Lanka Spot Sri Lanka’s ‘BIG THREE’ – which includes the majestic Sri Lankan elephant, the elusive Sri Lankan leopard, and the charismatic Sloth Bear Depending on the Twist of Your Luck! Enjoy a traditional rice and curry lunch at a shady area inside the park Overnight Stay in Yala Day11 Yala to Bentota Morning Transfer to Bentota, home of the Stunning beaches in South Coast of Sri Lanka En Route to Bentota, Explore the Maritime City of Galle Visit a Sea Turtle Conservation Project Evening Boat Safari in Madu River in the Coastal South. Cruise through Bio-Diversity-Rich Mangroves and Quaint Islands. Overnight Stay in Bentota Day12 Bentota Spend Full Day at Leisure enjoying the facilities at Cinnamon Bentota Beach – which set on the mesmerizing Indian Ocean overlooking breathtaking stretches of palm-fringed beaches! Enjoy available watersports and experiences offered by the resort Overnight Stay in Bentota Optional Excursions: Lunuganga Estate Tour Cinnamon Peeling & Toddy Tapping experience Boating through Bentara River Toddy and Rubber Tapping Pottery Mark Carving and Painting Puppetry Optional Resort Facilities: Swimming Pools Kids Club Day13 Bentota Take Your Last Day to Spend in the Waters of Bentota White Sand Beaches Overnight Stay in Bentota Day14 Bentota BIA Pick up from the hotel and Ride to the Airport for Your Outbound Flight As We Say Goodbye, We Hope You Will Have Beautiful Memories of the Island to Reminisce and will Visit Again for More! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days Experience ancient wonders: Dambulla Cave Temple, Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Unwind on golden sands at Trincomalee and Bentota. Encounter wildlife: safaris at Minneriya, Yala, and sea turtle conservation. Board a scenic train from Nuwara Eliya to Ella. Try local crafts: pottery, toddy tapping, and spice experiences. Engage in optional activities: high tea, spas, village tours, and more. Places you will stay See available hotels Cinnamon Grand Colombo Cinnamon Lodge Habarana Trinco Blu by Cinnamon Trincomalee Cinnamon Citadel Kandy Galway Heights Hotel Nuwara Eliya Cinnamon Wild Yala Cinnamon Bentota Beach Bentota See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Dress respectfully for temple visits, especially at Kandy’s Tooth Relic Temple. Minneriya National Park’s elephant gathering is best from July to October. The train ride from Nuwara Eliya to Ella is highly scenic—reserve seats in advance. Take seasickness remedies for boat safaris and coastal cruises. Sun protection and light clothing are ideal for both hill country and beach. FAQ Q: What should I pack for this trip? Light, breathable clothing for the coasts, a light jacket or sweater for the hill country, comfortable shoes, swimwear, sunscreen, and a camera. Q: When is the best time for wildlife viewing? Elephant gatherings at Minneriya peak between July and October; leopards in Yala are best spotted during the dry season (Feb–July). Q: Are there family-friendly activities? Yes, most hotels have kids’ clubs and pools, and activities like boat safaris, wildlife parks, and beach sports are suitable for families.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka vs Australia 2025 – Cricket Tour
itineraries.summary: Witness the excitement of the Sri Lanka vs Australia 2025 Test Series while exploring Sri Lanka’s most iconic attractions.
itineraries.detail_title: Cricket Tour 2025 – Sri Lanka VS Australia
itineraries.detail_text: Cricket Tour 2025 – Sri Lanka VS Australia Witness the excitement of the Sri Lanka vs Australia 2025 Test Series while exploring Sri Lanka’s most iconic attractions. This tour offers cricket action and cultural adventure in one unforgettable experience! Explore More Tailormade This Tours Join us for an unforgettable journey through Sri Lanka as you cheer on your favorite team in the Sri Lanka vs Australia 2025 Test Match Series! This exclusive tour combines the thrill of world-class cricket with Sri Lanka’s top attractions. Watch live matches in Galle, while also exploring the island’s breathtaking landscapes and experience the best of Sri Lanka’s culture, cuisine, and cricket for a tour you will never forget! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 24th January Upon your arrival at the airport, you will be greeted and escorted to your private vehicle. Your journey will then take you to the charming coastal town of Negombo, where you will find your comfortable accommodation. To help you adjust to the time zone, we recommend a restful night’s sleep to recuperate from your travels. Day 2 25th January Transfer to Sigiriya, Sri Lanka’s iconic heritage site. Delightful visit to the historic Ridi Viharaya Temple along the way. Explore the breathtaking Dambulla Caves Temple, renowned for its art and architecture. Relaxing overnight stay at Sigiriya. Day3 26th January Morning exploration of the iconic Sigiriya Lion Rock. Immerse yourself in local village life with a scenic catamaran ride on the lake and a hands-on experience preparing traditional Sri Lankan meals. Evening relaxation with a soothing herbal foot massage. Overnight stay at Sigiriya. Day 4 27th January Transfer to Kandy, the sacred capital of the central hills. Visit the Rangiri Dambulu Cricket Stadium. Explore Nalanda Gedige, the geographic center of Sri Lanka. Receive blessings at the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic. Indulge in a delightful overnight stay in Kandy. Day 5 28th January Embark on a scenic journey to Galle. Visit the Millennium Elephant Foundation along the way. Arrive in Galle and check in at your hotel. In the evening, enjoy a leisurely stroll through the historic Galle Old Dutch Fort. Enjoy an overnight stay in Galle. Day 6-10 29th January – 2nd February Enjoy the first test cricket match – Sri Lanka vs Australia, at the iconic Galle International Cricket Stadium. Enjoy an overnight stay in Galle. Day 11 3rd February Begin transfer to Yala in the southeast of the island, the most popular national park in Sri Lanka. Experience an afternoon game drive in Yala National Park. Stay overnight in Yala. Day 12 4th February Rise early and embark on a thrilling game drive in Yala National Park. Enjoy some “me-time” – take a refreshing dip in the pool, catch up on reading, or simply unwind. Gear up for another exciting game drive in Yala National Park. Stay overnight in Yala. Day 13 5th February Discover the enchanting Curd Trail in Tissamaharama, where the exquisite art of curd making unfolds before your eyes. As part of this immersive experience, partake in a pottery-making session, crafting your own unique piece of art with the guidance of local artisans. Begin transfer to the maritime city of Galle in the south. Enjoy an overnight stay in Galle. Day 14-18 06th to 10th February Don’t miss the cricket action of the second test match – Sri Lanka vs Australia, at the iconic Galle International Cricket Stadium. Enjoy a comfortable overnight stay in Galle. Day 19 11th February We will arrange a convenient transfer service to the airport for your ease and comfort. Journey Highlights Trip length: 19 Days Discover the rich cultural heritage of the Cultural Triangle before the battle begins. Experience the majestic elephants up close and personal. Cheer on as Sri Lanka faces Australia in an exciting test match series. Enjoy thrilling game drives for wildlife encounters. Immerse in local village life with a curd-making experience. Places you will stay See available hotels Heritance Negombo Negombo Heritance Kandalama Dambulla Jetwing Kandy Gallery Kandy Tribe Yala Yala The Fortress Resort & Spa Galle Le Grand Galle Galle See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Immerse in Local Cricket Culture Try Local Food & Street Snacks Capture Memories through Photography FAQ Q. Are tickets guaranteed for all matches? Tickets are included and early booking is recommended. Q. Are there any age restrictions for participants? Absolutely No! Q. Is transportation provided during the match days? Yes, the vehicle and your chauffeur guide will be at your disposal to provide transfers. Q. What is the dress code for the cricket matches? Casual attire like jeans, t-shirts, and comfortable shoes, team jerseys or colors. Q. What to bring and not? Small cameras, small bags, water bottles, sun protection and flags or banners. Umbrellas and outside food usually restricted. Q. Are train tickets included in the tour? Yes, train tickets are included. Q. Are you able to accommodate any dietary restrictions? Yes of course, dietary restrictions can be accommodated. Q. Where can I get money exchanged? The exchange of foreign currency is permitted only at banks, licensed money changers, and hotels. Q. What is the weather like in Sri Lanka during the tour dates? Warm and pleasant. The southern and southwestern coasts will have plenty of sunshine. Q. Could we explore some alternative hotel options beyond those suggested? If you share your preferences with us, we can arrange accordingly.

itineraries.title: Classic Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Our exclusive classic tour itinerary provides gateway to have a great travel memory in this tiny island Sri Lanka blended with an overwhelming dose of history…
itineraries.detail_title: Classic Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Classic Tour in Sri Lanka Our exclusive classic tour itinerary provides gateway to have a great travel memory in this tiny island Sri Lanka blended with an overwhelming dose of history… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Our exclusive classic tour itinerary provides gateway to have a great travel memory in this tiny island Sri Lanka blended with an overwhelming dose of history, UNESCO World Heritage Sites, thriving green tea regions, thrilling wildlife excursions and breathtakingly beautiful beaches to suit your fancy together staying at star class accommodation settings throughout your holiday in Ceylon! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Negombo Welcome to Sri Lanka! On the first day of your holiday, you will meet and greet by Blue Lanka representative and your private chauffeur guide at the lobby of Colombo International Airport. Take a leisurely drive to Negombo, where your journey begins. Check-into the hotel, recover any jetlag and spend your time at leisure. Day2, 3 Dambulla Enjoy an early breakfast before heading to Dambulla. Along the way, stop at Pinnawala to witness orphaned gigantic animals – elephants, in their natural habitats. Other than an orphanage, it functions as a captive, nursery and a breeding ground for the elephants in need and as a home for the homeless elephants who need special care. Do not miss out milk feeding time, hence ecommend to reach there at least by 09:00 AM! Spend next couple of days in Dambulla, climbing the magnificient ancient rock fortress and one of the archeological treasure in South Asia, Sigiriya; discovering scale models of the buildings and temples of the Polonnaruwa city, a former royal city dating back to the fifth century and go on a safari at Minneriya National Park to spot herds of elephants congregate near the giant reservoir. Day4 Kandy Next morning after a hearty breakfast at the hotel, leave for Kandy. On the way, visit Dambulla Cave Temple with spectacular Buddha-filled interiors and also known as the best-preserved cave temple complex in the country. Thereon, stop by a spice garden to get knowledge into a deep-rooted industry of flavor generation along with their attributes. Having arrived in Kandy, visit the Temple of the Tooth Relic which houses the only surviving relic of Buddha, during evening prayer services. Evening witness traditional Kandyan dancing and drumming performance. This is an energetic show accompanied by dancing troupes and drummers clad in extravagant traditional costumes ensuring a great way to spend the evening in Kandy. Day5 Nuwara Eliya After a peaceful stay in Kandy, travel towards the tea country – Nuwara Eliya which is also known as ‘Little England’. Be amazed with emerald green tea leaves, cascading waterfalls and quaint colonial buildings dotted all over place which would also make you feel like you are in an English city! During your stay, experience the art of making Ceylon tea from withering to bulk packing with a visit to a working tea plantation and a factory taste a freshly brewed cup of Ceylon tea. Capture absolute stunning sceneries and natural beauty of Nuwara Eliya, breath in fresh air and take a pleasant stroll around Gregory lake in the evening are some other experiences you can enjoy. Day6 Yala Following a mouthwatering breakfast at the hotel, journey from hills to islands’ wild – Yala. Along the route, witness Ravana waterfall and do not forget to take a selfie with the plunging falls in the background. Later, gather for an evening safari through Yala National Park with an expert naturalist. Keep your eyes peeled for the endangered Sri Lankan leopard as well as on other widlife such as elephants, wild boards and birdlife. In the late evening, expect a BBQ dinner by a beautiful roaring campfire. Day7, 8 Galle After a restful sleep in the wild, set off to Galle which is home to an impressive World Heritage-listed Dutch fort. This is popular as the best example for the fortified city in Asia built by Europeans by showing the mix of European architectural styles as well as South Asian traditions. In the evening, you may wander around this historic fort and watch the sunset from the ramparts. It would make sure a picture perfect end for the day! Day9 Transfer to Airport After all good things come to an end, you will be safely driven to the Colombo international airport for a safe journey home with cherishable memories of Sri Lanka Classic Holiday! This itinerary barely scratch the surface of the accommodation settings and experiences we offer. Be inspired and let us know what you’re looking for! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 9 Days Unlock the contrasts of the colourful city of Kandy Capture shots of wildlife inclduing endangered Sri Lankan leopard in Yala Enjoy the Beach Induced Bliss at Galle Elephants Bathing And Playfully Splash Around In The Shallow Water In Pinnawala The Majestic Golden Buddha Temple At The Bottom Of The Hill In Dambulla Cave Temple Complex An Astonishing Wildlife Spotting Safari In Yala National Park Fascinating Rumassala Temple And Its Secret Tales Places you will stay See available hotels The Radh Kandy Cinnamon Wild Yala Jetwing St. Andrews Nuwara Eliya See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Trekking at the Knuckles Mountain Range Water Rafting Hike Little Adam’s Peak See the Beautiful Coral Reefs FAQ Do we have a chance to experience tea plucking? Yes, during the tea factory visit you can enjoy a tea plucking experience. How many steps are there in Dambulla Cave Temple? 364 steps are there. How can I apply visa? You can apply visa through online portal https://etgov.lk/slvisa/ Are there ATM’s available? ATMs are available at many banks throughout the country.

itineraries.title: Explore Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Bentota in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Sri Lanka is quickly is one of Asia’s most popular tourist destinations. This nation is rich in old historic sites, gorgeous beaches, and breathtaking natural beauty.
itineraries.detail_title: Explore Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Bentota in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Explore Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Bentota in Sri Lanka Sri Lanka is quickly is one of Asia’s most popular tourist destinations. This nation is rich in old historic sites, gorgeous beaches, and breathtaking natural beauty. The Kandy Nuwara Eliya Bentota Colombo Tour in Sri Lanka allows you to experience the finest of Sri Lanka within 6 days. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Kandy, nestled among the highlands, lies at the heart of Sri Lanka. It is home to a broad range of tea plantations and biodiverse rainforest, as well as the hallowed Buddhist shrine of Tooth Relic. Nuwara Eliya is home to a diverse range of flora and wildlife, as well as several Hindu religious sites, thanks to the Horton Plain National Park and other botanical gardens. Bentota, Sri Lanka’s vacation town consists of the Bentota lagoon, which has a beautiful beach and a large statue of Gautam Buddha. Describing Sri Lanka’s history, Colombo, the main city, includes everything from lofty skyscrapers to dedicated architecture. The following is the daily itinerary for your Sri Lanka tour covering Kandy – Nuwara Eliya – Bentota and Colombo. Examine the activities and excursions to personalize them to your liking. Hit us up for further information. The Kandy Nuwara Eliya Bentota Colombo Tour is ready to give you an amazing experience. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Colombo Airport Pickup and Assistance at the Airport. Transfer to Colombo. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Late Evening Enjoy a Street Food Tour. Overnight Stay in Colombo Day2 Colombo to Kandy Morning Explore the Landmarks in Colombo. Transfer to Kandy. En Route Visit to Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage. Take Blessings from Kandy Tooth Relic Temple During Theva Time. Overnight stay in Kandy. Day 3 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Morning Visit to Peradeniya Botanical Garden. Take a Local Train Ride to Nanu Oya. Journey Passing Endless Tea Estates; Cascading Waterfalls and Misty Mountains. Evening Experience a High Tea at Grand Hotel Lawn. Overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day 4 Nuwara Eliya to Bentota Morning Visit to a Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory. Drive Through Little England. Pay a Visit to Seetha Amman Temple. Transfer to Bentota En Route Witness St. Clair and Devon Waterfalls. Overnight stay in Bentota. Day 5 Bentota Morning at Leisure By The Beach. Late Afternoon Take a Scenic Boat Ride in Madu River. Visit a Sea Turtle Conservation Center. Partake in Turtle Releasing with Other Fellow Travelers Overnight stay in Bentota Day 6 Bentota to BIA Airport Transfer Journey Highlights Trip length: 6 Days Enjoying a scenic train ride through tea estates and misty mountains. Walking around the Kandy Lake. Enjoying a sundowner at Bentota beach. Exploring Colombo with a City Tour. Boat Ride in Madu River. Blessings at the Kandy Temple of the Tooth Places you will stay See available hotels Colombo Court Hotel & Spa Colombo Amaya Hills Kandy Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya Centara Ceysands Resort Aluthgama See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Bring warm clothes for Nuwara Eliya. Clothes that can cover your shoulders and knees are recommended for Temple visits. Be aware of leeches in cool climes. Bring your swimsuits and flip flops. FAQ Q. Are Ramayana places covered in this itinerary? It is not. But we can always customize for your need and add the Ramayana locations for your tour itinerary. Q. How long is the Train ride from Kandy to Nanu Oya? It is about 2 1/2 hrs. Q. How is the weather in Nuwara Eliya like? Is it very cold? It can be chilly and sometimes wet. Always bring your cardigans and pullovers. Q. How long does it take to complete the boat ride in the Madu River Approximately 2 hours.

itineraries.title: Feathered Trails of Ceylon
itineraries.summary: This specialized birdwatching itinerary offers exceptional avian experiences across diverse habitats, providing opportunities to observe Sri Lanka’s endemic and migratory bird species in prime locations throughout the island.
itineraries.detail_title: Feathered Trails of Ceylon
itineraries.detail_text: Feathered Trails of Ceylon This 15-day bird-watching tour in Sri Lanka is a paradise for nature enthusiasts, offering a chance to explore the country’s diverse ecosystems and spot a wide variety of bird species: Explore More Tailormade This Tours This itinerary combines the best of coastal, wetland, and forest habitats, promising an exceptional bird-watching adventure in the captivating paradise of Sri Lanka. The journey includes must-visit bird-watching hotspots such as Kitulgala, Sinharaja, Bundala, and more, ensuring an experience tailored for avid bird enthusiasts around the world. Are you ready to embark on this remarkable journey with Blue Lanka Tours? Trust us – it truly sounds like a dream come true for every bird lover! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Wilpattu Upon your arrival at the airport, you will be greeted and escorted to your private vehicle. Your journey will then take you to Wilpattu, where your adventure begins. A good night’s sleep is the perfect way to adjust to the time zone and recover from your trip. Day2 Wilpattu Today, go on a full day jeep safari in Wilpattu National Park, Sri Lanka’s largest and the oldest national park, home to a rich array of resident and migratory birds. Take a break amidst nature to enjoy a picnic lunch along with some refreshing drinks on your safari. Overnight stay in Thrilling Wilpattu. Day3 Wilpattu to Mannar Leave to Discover the coastal beauty of Mannar. Evening Explore the countryside of Mannar including Visits to Thiruketheeswaram (Paalavi) Tank, Ranapadu Fishing Village & Lagoon, Mannar Fort, Etc. Overnight stay in untouched Mannar. Day4 Mannar Early Morning leave to spot migratory birds like flamingos and other waders in Korakulam Wetland, Mannar Salt Pans, Keeri Beach and Thallady Junction Ponds. Overnight stay in untouched Mannar Day5 Mannar to Sigiriya Head towards Sigiriya. Experience the awe-inspiring Sigiriya Rock Fortress during sunset, when the play of light creates a breathtaking view. Overnight stay in Historic Sigiriya. Day6 Explore Sigiriya Early Morning visit Sigiriya sanctuary, which is a Scrubland, dry mixed evergreen forest with small to medium tanks where you can spot bird species. Discover the essence of Sri Lankan village life through a scenic lake journey, meaningful exchanges with the locals, and a delightful cooking experience rooted in tradition. Overnight stay in Historic Sigiriya. Day7 Sigiriya to Kitulgala Begin your morning by exploring the historic Dambulla Cave Temple. Transfer to Kitulgala, Embark on a thrilling white-water rafting session, to spice up your journey with some adventure. Relaxing Overnight stay at Adventurous Kitulgala. Day8 Kitulgala to Nuwara Eliya Morning, Take a trek through the lush rainforest of Kitulgala, a haven for endemic birds such as the Sri Lanka Blue Magpie. Transfer towards Nuwara Eliya with en route stop to witness beautifully cascading St. Clair and Devon Waterfalls. Evening tour through the picturesque “Little England” of Sri Lanka, with stops at a tea plantation and factory, the charming old post office, Victoria Park, and the scenic Gregory Lake, where you can spot highland bird species. Stay overnight in misty Nuwara Eliya. Day9 Kitulgala to Nuwara Eliya Begin your day with a trek to Horton Plains, leading to the breathtaking World’s End. Revel in the spectacular views and discover unique endemic flora and fauna. Keep an eye on spotting Sri Lanka Junglefowl, Sri Lanka Wood Pigeon, Sri Lanka Whiteeye, Sri Lanka Bush Warbler, and more bird specifies during this trek. Overnight stay in Misty Nuwara Eliya. Day10 Nuwara Eliya to Yala Transfer to Yala. En route make a stop at Ravana waterfall for some photography opportunities. Afternoon, go on a jeep safari in Yala national park, where you can spot a variety of wildlife, including birds such as painted storks and pelicans. Overnight Stay in Serene Bundala. Day11 Bundala Dedicate today to a full-day or half-day safari in Bundala National Park, renowned for its wetlands that host vibrant waterbirds and migratory species, including flamingos, herons, and egrets. Overnight Stay in Serene Bundala Day12 Bundala to Udawalawe Transfer to Udawalawe. Visit the elephant transit home, a conservation center where you can observe rescued baby elephants in their natural habitat. In the afternoon, embark on an exciting jeep safari in Udawalawe National Park to see freely roaming elephants and a variety of bird species. Overnight stay in Adventurous Udawalawe. Day13 Visit Sinharaja Head to Sinharaja, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. In the evening, take a leisurely night walk, perfect for spotting nocturnal wildlife and soaking in the tranquil rainforest atmosphere. Overnight stay in Tranquil Sinharaja Day14 Sinharaja Spend the day trekking through the Sinharaja Rainforest, a paradise for bird enthusiasts. Look out for endemic bird species such as the Sri Lanka Spurfowl, Black-capped Bulbul, Sri Lanka Blue Magpie, Gray Hornbill, and many more during this immersive experience. Overnight stay in Tranquil Sinharaja. Day15 Departure Enjoy a hassle-free transfer to the airport, thoughtfully arranged for your comfort and convenience, with Blue Lanka Tours! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 15 Days Discover vibrant flora and endemic wildlife in Sri Lanka’s last rainforest. Spot flamingos and raptors at Mannar Island’s wetlands. Explore rainforests with endemic bird species like the junglefowl. Encounter unique wildlife in misty grasslands and cloud forests in the Horton Plains. Places you will stay See available hotels Leopard Trails Wilpattu Palmyrah House Mannar Palmstone Retreat Kitulgala W15 Lake Gregory Nuwara Eliya Kalu’s Hideaway Udawalawe Udawalawe The Rainforest Ecolodge Sinharaja Malala Retreat Hambantota See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Early mornings and late afternoons are the best times for bird activity while, migratory species are most abundant from November to April. Carry a pair of high-quality binoculars, a bird guidebook specific to Sri Lanka, and a camera with a good zoom lens if you are keen on photography. Wear neutral, earth-toned clothing to blend into the environment. Move slowly, avoid sudden movements, and keep noise to a minimum to prevent startling the birds. Keep a notebook, noting the time, location, and behavior. FAQ Q. Will a guide be provided throughout the tour? Absolutely! A knowledgeable guide will be available throughout the tour to ensure you have a fabulous, stress-free experience. Q. How accessible are the attractions in Mannar, such as the salt pans and wetland areas? The sites are generally accessible to all, but some may require moderate walking. Comfortable shoes or slippers are advised for exploring coastal and countryside areas. Q. What are the highlights of trekking in Sinharaja Rainforest? Sinharaja offers an exceptional experience of spotting endemic bird species, lush greenery, and unique fauna. It’s perfect for nature enthusiasts and bird watchers. Q. Are medical facilities available near the destinations? Medical facilities are available in most regions, while basic facilities will be available in rustic destination. Guides will assist in emergencies.

itineraries.title: Echoes of the Wild in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: This wildlife adventure itinerary offers immersive safari experiences in multiple national parks, providing encounters with elephants, leopards, and diverse fauna across Sri Lanka’s protected wilderness areas.
itineraries.detail_title: Echoes of the Wild in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Echoes of the Wild in Sri Lanka This 15-day wildlife tour in Sri Lanka offers a delightful mix of adventure and relaxation, showcasing the country’s rich biodiversity and stunning landscapes. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka is one of the few places on earth where you can encounter both land and marine wildlife in such striking diversity, and all within a compact, accessible island. Often referred to as the best place outside Africa to witness a version of the ‘Big Five’, the island offers a rare chance to see elephants, leopards, sloth bears, blue whales, and sperm whales in their natural habitats. Despite its modest size, Sri Lanka is home to 26 national parks and a remarkable range of ecosystems, from rainforest and dry scrub to misty highlands and coastal lagoons. With over 400 bird species (including 33 endemics), more than 120 mammals, and rich marine life, it’s a biodiversity hotspot unlike any other in South Asia. This 15-day wildlife itinerary has been thoughtfully designed for nature lovers and photographers alike, offering immersive experiences across Wilpattu, Wasgamuwa, Gal Oya, Kumana, and more. Each location reveals a different facet of Sri Lanka’s wild heart, whether you’re tracking leopards through forest trails, watching elephants swim between islands, or setting sail off the coast of Mirissa in search of whales. Along the way, you’ll also enjoy encounters with ancient cities, rural life, and spectacular scenery from mist-covered tea country to golden coastlines. Ideal for those seeking an active and meaningful journey, this experience blends wildlife, culture, and comfort into a seamless adventure. Speak to one of our Destination Specialists to start planning your personalized Sri Lanka wildlife tour. A journey that leaves lasting impressions and unforgettable encounters. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Wilpattu Upon your arrival at the airport, you will be greeted and escorted to your private vehicle. Your journey will then take you to Wilpattu, where your adventure begins. To help you adjust to the time zone, we recommend a restful night’s sleep to recuperate from your travels. Day2 Wilpattu Today, go on a full day jeep safari in Wilpattu National Park, known as Sri Lanka’s largest national park, famous for its natural lakes and elusive leopards. Enjoy picnic lunch, refreshments during the safari. Overnight stay in Thrilling Wilpattu. Day 3 Wilpattu to Sigiriya Transfer to Sigiriya, which is home to amazing cultural and wildlife wonders. Explore the legendary Sigiriya Rock Fortress as the sun dips below the horizon, casting a magical glow over the landscape. Evening relaxation with a soothing herbal foot massage. Overnight stay in Historic Sigiriya. Day 4 Anuradhapuara to Polonnaruwa In the morning Explore the breathtaking Dambulla Caves Temple, renowned for its art and architecture. Experience the charm of rural Sri Lankan life with a picturesque catamaran ride across the lake, engage with welcoming locals, and get involved in crafting authentic Sri Lankan cuisine. Head out after dusk for a unique loris watching tour, offering chances to observe the vibrant nocturnal life of the area with a naturalist. Overnight stay in Historic Sigiriya Day 5 Sigiriya to Polonnaruwa Start your day with a transfer to Polonnaruwa, offering an opportunity to marvel at the preserved relics of this ancient kingdom, by bicycles. Move to Wasgamuwa. Overnight stay at Charming Wasgamuwa. Day 6 Explore Wasgamuwa Embark on a Morning Jeep Safari Adventure in Wasgamuwa National Park, which is Home to a Variety Of Wildlife, such as the Majestic Sri Lankan Elephants, Water Buffalo, Deers, and Wild Boars. Relaxing Overnight stay at Charming Wasgamuwa. Day 7 Wasgamuwa to Nuwara Elliya Transfer towards Nuwara Eliya, where you can experience the cool climate and lush tea plantations, along with some scenic hikes. Evening Drive through the little England city including visits to a tea plantation, tea factory, old post office, Victoria park and Gregory Lake. Overnight stay in Misty Nuwara Eliya Day 8 Trek to Horton Plains Begin the day with a refreshing trek to Horton Plains and World’s End, immersing yourself in stunning scenery and the opportunity to observe endemic species. Overnight stay in Misty Nuwara Eliya. Day 9 Nuwara Elliya to Gal Oya Transfer to Gal Oya Take a Thrilling Safari through Gal Oya National Park, where you will Encounter the Beauty of its dense jungle and diverse Animal Species. Take a magical evening stroll at the lodge premises, uncovering the secrets of the nocturnal world around you. Overnight Stay in Untouched Gal Oya Day 10 Galoya Morning Enjoy a Boat Ride on Sri Lanka’s Largest Lake. With a Little Luck You Could Spot Elephants Swimming Between Islands or Bathing at the Lake’s Edge. Evening Explore the picturesque surroundings on a bike, discovering the Authentic day-to-day routines of local communities. Late evening, Partake in Animal Monitoring Project with a Naturalist. Overnight Stay in Untouched Gal Oya. Riding Distance: None Day 11 GalOya to Kumana Early Morning, Drift through Kiri Oruwa Lake & Immerse yourself in the rich biodiversity as you spot a diverse range of birds. Transfer to Kumana. Overnight stay in Wilderness Kumana Day 12 Kumana Today Go on a Full Day Jeep Safari in Kumana National Park. Nestled in the southeastern corner of Sri Lanka, Kumana National Park is a hidden gem that’s ideal for nature enthusiasts and bird watchers. The park is also home to elusive leopards, elephants, deer, and crocodiles, making it an exciting destination for wildlife enthusiasts. Despite its rich biodiversity, Kumana remains less crowded than other national parks in Sri Lanka, offering visitors a peaceful and immersive experience in nature. Overnight stay in Wilderness Kumana. Day 13 Kumana to Mirissa Transfer to Mirissa, which is the last destination of this amazing adventure. Evening Visit coconut tree, parrot rock and spend the time leisurely. Stay overnight in Sandy Mirissa. Day 14 Mirissa Early Morning, Set sail from Mirissa to Witness the Breathtaking Spectacle of Whales and Dolphins in Their Natural Ocean Habitat. Relaxing overnight stay in Sandy Mirissa. Day 15 Departure from Colombo Enjoy a hassle-free transfer to the airport, thoughtfully arranged for your comfort and convenience, with Blue Lanka Tours! Journey Highlights Trip length: 15 Days Witness herds of elephants and the elusive sloth bear in lush landscapes in Wasgamuwa National Park. Spot the charming slender loris during a magical night safari. See over 300 bird species, plus elephants and leopards, in beautiful mangroves in Kumana National Park. Hike to World’s End and Baker’s Falls, surrounded by unique wildlife. Watch elephants swim between islands on an unforgettable boat safari at Gal Oya Insightful Tips Start safaris early in the morning for the best chances to spot the wildlife ! Stay silent and patient while exploring the national parks to increase your odds of spotting sloth bears. Keep binoculars handy for identifying the rich variety of birds. Book whale-watching tours with ethical operators to ensure sustainable practices. FAQ Q. What is the best time to visit Sri Lanka and embark on this itinerary? Sri Lanka offers year-round beauty, but the best time to enjoy this particular itinerary is during the dry season, which spans from June to September as well as in the Winter months from February to April. Q. What kind of clothing and gear should I pack for this journey? Pack lightweight, breathable clothing for warm weather, with a few layers for cooler evenings, especially in Nuwara Eliya. Essentials include trekking shoes, a hat, sunscreen, insect repellent, swimwear, and binoculars. For safaris and treks, bring a daypack, reusable water bottle, and a raincoat for unexpected showers. Q. How long does the boat safari on Sri Lanka’s largest lake typically last? The boat safari typically lasts around 2 to 2.5 hours. Q. Can private jeep safaris be arranged for the safaris in all the mentioned national parks? Yes, all the safaris will arranged in private jeeps, at all the national parks mentioned in the itinerary. itineraries.title: Unforgettable Adventures in Sri Lanka Maldives
itineraries.summary: This dual-destination itinerary combines Sri Lanka’s cultural and wildlife experiences with Maldives’ luxury island resorts, offering diverse adventures from ancient ruins to overwater villas and marine activities.
itineraries.detail_title: Unforgettable Adventures In Sri Lanka & Maldives
itineraries.detail_text: Unforgettable Adventures In Sri Lanka & Maldives Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a 15-day adventure spanning Sri Lanka’s lush landscapes and the idyllic shores of the Maldives. Your journey begins in Colombo, where you’ll explore the vibrant city attractions and enjoy an exhilarating karting experience in Bandaragama. Venture into the wilderness of Kitulgala for a thrilling rafting adventure and immerse yourself in birdwatching trails and water activities. Continue your expedition to Nuwara Eliya, where you’ll witness stunning waterfalls, visit tea plantations, and embark on a trek to Horton Plains and the Big World’s End. Experience the iconic train ride to Ella, stroll along the Nine Arch Bridge, and indulge in adventurous activities like zip-lining and ATV biking. Traverse to Yala for a jeep safari in the national park and unwind with a beach BBQ dinner. In the Maldives, spend three blissful days relaxing on pristine beaches, enjoying water sports, and embarking on family-friendly excursions. From snorkeling and diving to sunset cruises and island hopping, immerse yourself in the tropical paradise before bidding farewell and departing with cherished memories of your family adventures. itineraries.summary: This combined itinerary offers cultural exploration in Sri Lanka’s heritage sites followed by beach relaxation in Maldives’ luxury resorts, providing diverse experiences from ancient wonders to tropical paradise.

itineraries.detail_text: Twin Center Sri Lanka & Maldives Tour Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a 12-day journey through the picturesque landscapes of Sri Lanka and the paradisiacal shores of the Maldives. Your adventure begins in Kotugoda, where you’ll unwind at The Wallawwa, easing into your journey and recovering from any jetlag. In the latter half of your journey, journey to Hatton for a tea-infused experience amidst lush plantations and scenic landscapes. Then, bid adieu to Sri Lanka and jet off to the Maldives for five days of indulgence at your chosen beach resort. From swimming with baby sharks to snorkeling in the azure waters and savoring sunset cocktail sessions, immerse yourself in the enchanting allure of the Maldives before bidding farewell and returning home with cherished memories.
