itineraries.title: Thrilling Diving Experience with Maldives Explorer
itineraries.summary: This diving-focused itinerary offers exceptional underwater adventures in Maldives’ prime dive sites, exploring vibrant coral reefs, diverse marine life, and spectacular underwater landscapes with professional guidance.
itineraries.detail_title: Thrilling Diving Experience With Maldives Explorer
itineraries.detail_text: Thrilling Diving Experience With Maldives Explorer Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on an unforgettable 8-day diving expedition through the pristine waters of the Maldives aboard the MV Maldives Explorer. Arrive in Male, where you’ll be greeted at Velana International Airport and board the boat. Set sail at sunrise for your first dive site, Maagiri, in North Male Atoll. Encounter vibrant reef fish and sharks as you explore dive sites like Embudhoo Express and Kandooma/Guraidhoo in Vaavu Atoll. Spend your nights anchored near Alimatha, preparing for the next day’s adventures. Continue your journey to Ari Atoll, home to magnificent dive spots like Miyaru Kandu/Dhevana Kandu and Vilamendhoo. Dive among whale sharks in Maamigili Beyru and explore the depths of Kuda Rah Thila and Maamigili. Sail to Panathone, known as the “Shark City,” and dive sites like Rangali Manta and Rai Dhiga in Central Ari Atoll. Enjoy a BBQ dinner on Embudhoo Island before anchoring for the night. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations FAQs & Tips Day 1 Male Airport to MV Maldives Explorer Arrive in Maldives! Pickup and Assistance at Valena International Airport Board to the Boat Listen to the General Briefing of the Boat while Enjoying Welcome Refreshments Boat will Set Sail to the First Dive Site in the First Light of Sunrise! Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 2 North Male to Vaavu Atoll Experience Diving at Different Dive Sites You will Encounter Plenty of Reef Fishes and Sharks and Occasional View of Rays While Diving in Below Spots : Dive 1 – Maagiri, Dive 2 – Embudhoo Express, Dive 3 – Kandooma/Guraidhoo, Dive 4 – Alimatha Night Dive Boat will Anchot at ‘Alimatha’. You can Refresh and Prepare for the Next Day Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 3 Vaavu Atoll to Ari Atoll Morning Start on Sail for the Next Diving Stops – Ari Atoll Drive In Following Dive Spots in Ari Atoll : Dive 1 – Miyaru Kandu/Dhevana Kandu, Dive 2 – Vilamendhoo, Dive 3 – 5 Rocks Boat will Anchot at near ‘Dhangethi or Dhigurah’ for today’s night. You can Refresh and Prepare for the Next Day Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 4 South Ari Atoll Today is All About Spotting Whales Sharks! Once You Locate the Whale Shark, you will get a chance to snorkel or Scuba with them Dive In Following Spots too : Dive 1 – Kuda rah thila, Dive 2 – Maamigili beyru (whale Shark), Dive 3 – Whale Shark Dive (Dive site may change) Boat will anchor near ‘Maamigili’ for the night Following Dinner, You May Spend a Restful Night Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 5 South Ari to Central Ari Atoll Today Sail to ‘Panathone’ – The Shark City You Will also Dive in Following Dive Spots : Dive 1 – Rangali Manta, Dive 2 – Rai Dhiga, Dive 3 – Panathone Boat will anchor near the desert island for the night Enjoy a BBQ Dinner in ‘Embudhoo’ Island Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 6 Central Ari Atoll to North Ari Atoll Today You will Leave to Explore and Dive in Manta Cleaning Stations Dive in Following Dive Spots : Dive 1 – Moofushi Manta Point, Dive 2 – Himandhoo Thila, Dive 3 – Mayaa Thila, Dive 4 – Night Manta Fesdu Show/Mayaa Thila Boat will anchor near the ‘Fesdu’ for the night Enjoy a BBQ Dinner in ‘Embudhoo’ Island Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 7 Ari Atoll to Kaafu Atoll Explore ‘Bathala Maagau’ Atoll along with Schools of Fish and Many Other Marine You will also Dive in Below Spots : Dive 1 – Bathala Maagau / Fish Head, Dive 2 –Bathala Maagau / Fish Head, Dive 3 – Fish Tank (Kaafu) / Kuda haa Boat will anchor near the ‘Kurumba’ for this last night Overnight Stay in Mv Maldives Explorer Boat Day 8 Maldives to Home It’s Time to Say Goodbye to Maldives! Clean, Wash and Pack Your Dive Equipment Transfer Via a Sea Plane Back to Male Airport for Your Flight Back Home FAQ Q. How many cabins are there in the boat and what’s the maximum occupancy? There’s are 08 cabins [01 Family Quad Suite | 02 Seaview Suites | 04 Standard Suites | 01 Standard Seaview Suite] and can accommodate up to 16-22 pax. Q. Are the cabins air-conditioned and have attached bathrooms? All cabins are air-conditioned and have attached bathrooms with hot and cold water. Q.What are the activities and amenities in Maldives Explorer? Activities > Snorkeling, Island Hopping, BBQ, Fishing Amenities > Sun beds, Television, Library, English Socket, WiFi, Karaoke System, Multimedia Library and Board Games Q. Is there Wi-Fi/Internet facilities available? WiFi is available everywhere on the ship and runs for 24 hours a day, as does electricity.

FAQ Q. Are young children allowed to do White Water-Rafting? All children aged 5 and above are allowed. Q. What’s the weight limit for Zip Line? Minimum weight of the participant should be 30kg and maximum weight should be 110 kg. However, these weight limits are subjected to change depending in the weather Situation. Q. What are the precautions taken for the health & Safety during the stay at Maldives? All the resorts have developed an extensive health and safety guideline to make sure that they curate the most awesome and fun filled holiday experience for their guests throughout the stay. For more information, please contact a tour consultant. Q. Can we get free snorkeling equipment’s and life jackets at Maldives? Snorkeling equipment are free for guests on Dine Around All Inclusive and above basis. For guests on other meal plans, snorkeling equipment are available for rental at a daily rate. Lifejackets will be in your room, as for the snorkeling equipment, you can just drop by to our water sports center, Aquaholics and collect them.

Then, journey through picturesque Nuwara Eliya and Yala, where thrilling jeep safaris offer glimpses of exotic wildlife. Transition to Galle for a historical exploration of Galle Fort and a visit to a sea turtle hatchery before jetting off to the Maldives. Spend three blissful days at your chosen resort, indulging in watersports, family-friendly excursions, and sun-soaked relaxation. From diving to dolphin watching, each day promises new adventures amidst the idyllic backdrop of the Maldives’ pristine beaches. Cherish these precious moments with your loved ones before bidding farewell and returning home, leaving with hearts full of unforgettable memories. FAQ Q. How long does it take to complete Elephant Freedom Project activity? It takes 06 hours to complete the entire program. Refer the time table below. 09:00 AM – Welcome and safety talk 09:15 AM – Prepare the elephant food 09:30 AM – Walk with the elephant 11:30 AM – Tea time 12:00 PM – Bath the elephant 01:00 PM – Lunch time 02:00 PM – End Q. What are the morning and afternoon safari timings in Yala? Morning Round > From 05:00AM till 09:00 AM. Afternoon Round > From 03:30 PM till 06:00 PM. Departure time can be altered based on where you locate in as well as the park opens earlier during periods of the year when the sun rises earlier. Q.Is there an extra charge for the kids’ club at Maldives?? Kids’ Clubs are free of charge. Q. Can kids come on board when Diving in Maldives? Generally, yes, but that depends on the resort you choose. Each resort has their own rules and provide discounts. Please check with a tour consultant at the time of booking.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Maldives Water Sports Tour
itineraries.summary: This aquatic adventure itinerary offers extensive water sports activities across both destinations, from Sri Lanka’s surfing and whale watching to Maldives’ diving, snorkeling, and luxury marine experiences.
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka + Maldives Water Sports Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka + Maldives Water Sports Tour Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a 14-day water sports tour across Sri Lanka and the Maldives, starting with adventures in Negombo and Thirappane before transitioning to Kandy and Kitulgala for more thrilling experiences. itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka + Maldives Honeymoon Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka + Maldives Honeymoon Tour Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a 12-day honeymoon journey through the enchanting landscapes of Sri Lanka and the Maldives, designed to create unforgettable moments of romance and relaxation. From the lush greenery of Kotugoda’s The Wallawwa to the opulent paradise of Meeru Island Resort & Spa in the Maldives, every destination promises a blend of luxury and intimacy. Indulge in romantic candlelit dinners under the stars, soothing couple massages, and thrilling water sports adventures, all while surrounded by breathtaking natural beauty. In Sri Lanka, immerse yourselves in the cultural wonders of Kandy and Hatton, where you’ll explore ancient temples, traverse scenic tea plantations, and indulge in leisurely afternoon teas. Conclude your journey in the Maldives, where days are spent lounging on pristine beaches, snorkeling in crystal-clear waters, and creating unforgettable memories together. With seamless transfers and personalized experiences throughout, this honeymoon tour is the perfect beginning to your new chapter of love and adventure. Contact us to start planning your dream getaway today. itineraries.title: Best of Both Worlds Sri Lanka and Maldives
itineraries.summary: This comprehensive dual-destination itinerary combines Sri Lanka’s cultural heritage and wildlife experiences with Maldives’ luxury island paradise, offering diverse adventures from ancient ruins to overwater bungalows.
itineraries.detail_title: Best of Both Worlds! (Sri Lanka & Maldives)
itineraries.detail_text: Best of Both Worlds! (Sri Lanka & Maldives) Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a 21-day odyssey through the captivating landscapes and rich cultural heritage of Sri Lanka and the Maldives. Begin your adventure in Negombo, where the vibrant energy of the fish market sets the stage for an immersive experience. Traverse the untamed wilderness of Wilpattu, with guided night walks and thrilling game drives offering glimpses of Sri Lanka’s diverse wildlife. Discover the ancient wonders of Sigiriya and Polonnaruwa, where centuries-old ruins and sacred sites tell stories of bygone eras. Immerse yourself in the spiritual ambiance of Kandy, then journey through scenic Haputale and historic Galle, each offering its own unique charm and allure. In Colombo, immerse yourself in the vibrant tapestry of urban life before bidding farewell to Sri Lanka and setting sail for the Maldives. Indulge in four days of blissful relaxation amidst luxury accommodations, pristine beaches, and crystal-clear waters. Whether snorkeling amidst vibrant coral reefs, embarking on a sunset cruise, or simply unwinding with a rejuvenating spa treatment, each moment in this tropical paradise promises to replenish your spirit. As your journey draws to a close, cherish the memories forged amidst these idyllic landscapes, knowing that the serenity and beauty of Sri Lanka and the Maldives will stay with you long after your return home. Evening at Leisure Overnight Stay in Kandy Day 7 Sigiriya to Kandy Transfer to Kandy En Route Visit to Dambulla Cave Temple Check-Into The Hotel and Relax Evening Take Blessings from Kandy Tooth Relic Temple Overnight Stay in Kandy Day 8 Kandy Spend the Morning at Leisure Visit to Peradeniya Botanical Garden Take an Eclectic Visit to one of the Celebrated Sri Lankan Contemporary Artists Overnight Stay in Kandy Day 9 Kandy to Haputale Morning Take a Local Train Ride to Haputale Enjoy Beautiful Sceneries Passed by! – Tea Estates | Waterfalls | Mountains| Etc Check-Into the Bungalow and Relax. Enjoy the Bungalow Facilities which Exclusively Available for You Overnight Stay in Haputale Day 10 Haputale Spend Full Day at Leisure Alternatively, You Can Explore the Attractions in Haputale. Trek to Some Beautiful Viewpoints | Visit a Working Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory Indulge in a BBQ Dinner in a Unique Way with Thotalagala! Overnight Stay in Haputale Day 11 Haputale to Galle Transfer to Galle En Route Make a Stop in Ella to Explore the Countryside of Ella Little Adam’s Peak Hike | 9 Arch Bridge | Local Restaurants (Café Chill | Matey Hut) Check-Into Your Beach Resort and Relax Overnight Stay in Galle Day 12 Galle Morning is Free to Relax and Chill Out by The Beach Evening Take a Leisurely Walk in Galle Fort Witness Beautiful Sunset Views Overnight Stay in Galle Day 13 Galle Full day at Leisure! Enjoy Resort Facilities or Explore and Experience Attractions Nearby : Surfing in Koggala | Play a Round of Golf at Eagles Catalina Golf Course | Whale Watching in Mirissa (Based on Seasonality) Overnight Stay in Galle Day 14 Galle to Colombo Transfer to Colombo En Route Go on a Scenic Boat Ride in Madu River Visit to a Sea Turtle Conservation Center Check-Into The Hotel and Refresh Evening Experience a Street Food Tour Overnight Stay in Colombo Day 15 Colombo Explore the Commercial City of Colombo It’s All About Experiencing Cultural, Shopping, and Dining Options in Capital of Sri Lanka Spend the Evening at Leisure Overnight Stay in Colombo Day 16 Colombo to BIA to Maldives Morning Transfer to Airport (BIA) for Your Flight to Male Pick-Up and Assistance at Male Airport Transfer to Your Beach Resort Via Speed Boat or By Sea Plane Check-Into the Resort and Relax Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 17-20 Maldives Embark on a Retreat to Revive Your Senses and Relax Your Mind While Admiring the Blue Azures of the Indian Ocean You May also Participate in Yoga and Meditations Lessons Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 21 Maldives to Departure It’s Time to Say Goodbye to Maldives! Transfer to Male Airport Via a Sea Plane, for Your Flight Back Home! FAQ Q. How long would it take to complete both Sigiriya and Village Tour respectively? Sigiriya Rock Fortress > The climb all the way to the top can take between 2-3 hours, depending on your fitness level, how crowded the place is and how many pictures you are shooting. Sigiriya Village Tour > Around 02.5 hours with cooking class. Q. Should I skip beaches in Sri Lanka if I’m staying in Maldives? Sri Lanka is lot more diverse when comparing with Maldives when it comes to its landscapes, nature, fauna, history and architecture. You can get a true Asian experience in Sri Lanka while discovering rich heritage, wildlife, greenery and hills, etc. Depending on the season you travel, we would recommend 1-2 nights stay either on the South or East coast of Sri Lanka, unless if it’s a beach-oriented holiday. Q.When is the best time to visit Maldives for a perfect Beach Holiday? Maldives is an all-season destination! You can experience a tropical climate, sunshine-filled days and temperatures averaging 30°C all year round. Diving is good at any time of the year; yet the monsoon season (May-October) is the best season for Manta Rays and spot other marine life due to the higher quantities of plankton. Q. What are the most popular excursions that I shouldn’t miss in Maldives? Snorkeling Boat Trip House Reef Sunset Cruise Sandbank Picnic Night Fishing Spot Dolphins or Whale Sharks Visit a Deserted Island

itineraries.summary: This prenatal getaway itinerary offers relaxing and safe experiences for expectant parents, featuring gentle activities, spa treatments, and comfortable accommodations tailored for pregnancy in both destinations.
itineraries.detail_title: Baby mooning in Sri Lanka & Maldives
itineraries.detail_text: Baby mooning in Sri Lanka & Maldives Explore More Tailormade This Tours Embark on a relaxing 15-day babymoon journey across Sri Lanka and Maldives, ideal from November to April. Begin in Sri Lanka, staying at luxurious retreats like The Wallawwa, Jetwing Vil Uyana, Bougainvillea Retreat, Ceylon Tea Trails, and Maniumpathy. Explore village life, elephant conservation projects, ancient ruins, botanical gardens, and tea plantations. Indulge in spa treatments, candlelit dinners, and culinary experiences tailored for expecting couples. Then, escape to a private island resort in the Maldives, choosing from acclaimed properties like One&Only Reethi Rah, COMO Cocoa Island, Four Seasons Resorts, Six Senses Laamu, or Huvafen Fushi. Unwind with prenatal massages, pregnancy yoga, healthy meals, starlit cinema, sandbank picnics, and safe snorkeling in the turquoise lagoons. Plant a coral frame and receive updates as it grows alongside your baby. The resort offers plenty of activities for partners too. Conclude this idyllic babymoon with a departure from the Maldives, taking home cherished memories. itineraries.summary: This exclusive itinerary offers complete privacy and luxury on a private Maldivian island, featuring personalized services, untouched beaches, and bespoke experiences in an intimate tropical paradise setting.
itineraries.detail_title: 08 Days Private Island Holiday In Maldives
itineraries.detail_text: 08 Days Private Island Holiday In Maldives Explore More Tailormade This Tours Indulge in the epitome of luxury travel by escaping to your own private island paradise for 8 blissful days in the Maldives. Choose from exclusive resorts like Cheval Blanc Randheli, Kudadoo Maldives Private Island, Velaa Private Island, Niyama Private Islands, or Naladhu Private Island across different atolls. Upon arrival on Day 1, you’ll be privately transferred to your tropical hideaway to begin your all-inclusive island experience. Spend Days 2-7 embracing the secluded island life – unwind on pristine beaches, rejuvenate at the spa, play tennis or engage in various sports, attend yoga sessions, and let the little ones revel in kids’ clubs and water parks. Explore deserted islands on a picnic excursion, try unlimited watersports from scuba diving to jet-skiing, go snorkeling with marine experts to spot turtles and rays, learn Maldivian cuisine, and fish at sunset. Elevate your stay with a luxury yacht cruise or romantic starlight dining. On Day 8, you’ll be privately transferred back to the airport, leaving with memories of your exclusive Maldivian private island holiday. itineraries.summary: This vibrant Maldives itinerary offers daily entertainment and activities at Kurumba Resort, featuring live music, cultural performances, themed nights, and diverse recreational options alongside luxury beachfront accommodation.
itineraries.detail_title: 08 Days – Nightly Entertainment At Kurumba Maldives
itineraries.detail_text: 08 Days – Nightly Entertainment At Kurumba Maldives Explore More Tailormade This Tours Spend 8 days immersed in the Maldivian island life at the iconic Kurumba Maldives Resort on Vihamanaafushi Island in the North Malé Atoll. Your journey begins on Day 1 with an airport transfer to the resort. The following days offer a delightful blend of cultural experiences and relaxation. Witness the vibrant Bodu Beru dance and drums performance on Day 2, followed by chilled DJ evenings. Day 3 takes you on a Turtle Safari to spot different species, complemented by live music at night. Experience true Maldivian life on Day 4 with a visit to a local island, snorkeling, and souvenir shopping, before a DJ night. Leisure days are sprinkled throughout, allowing you to unwind at the resort, with sunset lounge music, cocktail tastings in the lagoon, and live music evenings. The highlight is the Sunset & Dolphin Discovery Cruise on Day 6, where you can spot friendly dolphins frolicking in the ocean. Your Maldivian adventure concludes on Day 8 with a transfer back to the airport, leaving you with lasting memories. When is the best time visit Maldives? Between November to April. Q. What are the payment options available at the resort? Cash less payments have been more common, but cash will be accepted. Q.What medical facilities does the resort has? Kurumba has an in-house resident doctor and nurse. They are experienced professionals and certified to comply with the latest guidelines by the Maldives Health Protection Agency. Q. What to wear in Maldives? Lightweight cottons and linen clothes are recommended. Items for Ladies would be sundresses, kaftans, light shirts, sarongs, shirts & skirts. If you plan to visit a local Island or Male’, it is advisable to have your thighs and shoulders covered if visiting the inhabited local islands. For the men, light cotton shirts, shorts, pants, board shorts and some light and comfortable smart casual for evening wear.

itineraries.title: 04 Days Short Break in Maldives
itineraries.summary: This compact Maldives escape offers quick tropical rejuvenation with convenient resort stays near Male, featuring quick access to beaches, snorkeling, and relaxation in a paradise island setting.
itineraries.detail_title: 04 Days – Short Break In Maldives
itineraries.detail_text: 04 Days – Short Break In Maldives Explore More Tailormade This Tours This 4-day getaway offers a choice of staying at one of three luxury resorts – Dhigufaru Island Resort, Anantara Kihavah Maldives Villas, or Dusit Thani Maldives, all located in the scenic Baa Atoll. Airport transfers by domestic flight or speedboat are included. Day 1 is for arrival and relaxation at your chosen resort. Day 2 allows you to pick from various activities like fishing, scuba diving, snorkeling, dolphin watching, parasailing, or jet-skiing. Day 3 focuses on rejuvenation with ayurvedic massage, yoga, and resort amenities, followed by a cinema experience under the stars. The trip concludes on Day 4 with a transfer back to the airport for your departure flight. Contact for pricing details. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations FAQs & Tips Day 1 Maldives Arrival in Maldives Meet & Greet at the Airport by Representative Domestic Flight or Speed Boat Transfer to the Resort Rest of the Day Relaxing at the Resort Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 2 Maldives You May Engage with the Acitivites of Your Choice: Fishing, Discover Scuba Diving, Snorkeling Adventure, Dolphin Discovery, Parasailing, Jet Ski Relaxing After a Busy Funfilled Day Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 3 Maldives Recharge Your Body & Mind with Ayurvedic Massage or Yoga Enjoy Resort Amenities In the Evening Cinema Experience Under the Stars Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 4 Maldives Domestic Flight or Speed Boat Transfer to Airport Fly to Home Country FAQ Q. Which basis includes in this package? All Inclusive Basis. Q. Do I need a visa to visit Maldives? Visa is free on arrival for all countries. Q. Is there any vegetarian food available at resorts? Yes, vegetarian food is available. Q. Are these resorts adults only? No, All these resorts are family friendly.

itineraries.title: 04 Days Luxury Mini Break in COMO Cocoa Island
itineraries.summary: This exclusive Maldives retreat offers sophisticated luxury at COMO Cocoa Island, featuring overwater suites, wellness treatments, gourmet dining, and pristine lagoon access in an intimate private island setting.
itineraries.detail_title: 04 Days – Luxury Mini Break In Como Cocoa Island
itineraries.detail_text: 04 Days – Luxury Mini Break In Como Cocoa Island Explore More Tailormade This Tours This 4-day trip offers a luxurious escape to COMO Cocoa Island, a private island resort located in the Makunufushi, South Male Atoll of the Maldives. The package includes roundtrip speedboat transfers between the airport and the resort, as well as various activities and experiences. On the first day, you’ll be greeted at the airport and whisked away to the resort by speedboat. After some relaxation, you’ll enjoy an evening sunset fishing experience. The second day includes a 2-hour catamaran snorkeling trip and a cooking class to learn how to prepare a dish from the resort’s menu or COMO Shambhala Cuisine. The highlight of the third day is a romantic sandbank dinner, where you’ll dine at a private table set up on the eastern sandbank of the island, accompanied by a personal butler and a bottle of chilled champagne. The trip concludes on the fourth day with a speedboat transfer back to the airport for your departure flight. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations FAQs & Tips Day 1 Maldives Arrival in Maldives Meet & Greet at the Airport by Representative Speed Boat Transfer to the Resort Having Some Relaxation at the Resort Evening Sunset Fishing Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 2 Maldives 02 Hours Catamaran Snorkeling Trip Learn to Make a Dish from the Restaurant Menus or Healthy COMO Shambhala Cuisine Lesiure at the Resort Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 3 Maldives Lesiure at the Resort Enjoying Resort Amenities Sandbank Dinner Overnight Stay in Maldives Day 4 Maldives Speed Boat Transfer to Airport Fly to Home Country FAQ Q. How long does it take to travel from Male airport to the Resort? It does take approximately 40 minutes by speedboat. Q. Can I use my drone there? Please note that for privacy and safety reasons, drones and other unmanned aerial equipment are not permitted to be flown anywhere at the resort. Q. What is the currency used in Maldives? The Maldives uses Rufiyaa (MVR) as the national currency. Q. Can I smoke in Maldives? Yes. There is no policy it varies from island to island it generally favours smokers. But some will have restricted areas (restaurants, rooms in particular).

itineraries.title: Wildlife Photography Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: This specialized photography itinerary offers exceptional wildlife viewing opportunities in Sri Lanka’s national parks, providing professional guidance for capturing leopards, elephants, and diverse endemic species.
itineraries.detail_title: Wildlife Photography Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Wildlife Photography Tour Encounters with wild animals, camping experiences amidst the wild, whales in the Indian Ocean and stunning coastal panoramas are what you can expect on this tour. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Calling out to all those photographers who are also fanatics of the wild! This specially designed wildlife photography tour in Sri Lanka gives an opportunity for photographers to expand their portfolio with real-life images of surroundings that they are also keen on exploring themselves. Encounters with wild animals, camping experiences amidst the wild, whales in the Indian Ocean and stunning coastal panoramas are what you can expect on this tour. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Colombo Upon arrival at the Bandaranaike International Airport, a Blue Lanka representative will greet you there and guide you to a private luxury vehicle which will be your mode of transport for this wildlife photography tour in Sri Lanka. Your first stop will be in Colombo, at Galle Face Hotel. The hotel is located in close proximity to malls and other attractions in Colombo, so feel free to set out and explore. Day2 Udawalawa On day 02, you will transfer to Udawalawe and reach the Elephant Transit Home by noon, which is the milk feeding time for elephants. After lunch, we have scheduled an afternoon jeep safari at the Udawalawe National Park so you can get maximum possible photographs of elephants in their natural surroundings. Your accommodation for the night will be at Grand Udawalawe Safari Resort. Day 3 & 4 Yala Early the next morning, you have another encounter with Udawalawe elephants on a game drive beginning at 6.00 a.m. Next, you will head towards Yala, to make it in time for the afternoon jeep safari (3.00 p.m.) at the Yala National Park. Expect to see deers, leopards, sloth bears, water buffalos and more here – and of course, have your camera ready to capture! Leopard Nest is where you will be camping in Yala. Prepare for an early morning game drive on day 04 at Yala and then an afternoon jeep safari at Bundala National Park, and again, an early morning safari at Yala National Park before you transfer to Mirissa on day 05. Day 5 & 6 Habarana to Anuradhapura Reach Mirissa by afternoon and check in to Sri Sharavi Beach Villas and relax the rest of the evening. Another early morning activity is lined up for you on day 06 of this wildlife photography tour in Sri Lanka, but this time, it’s with the whales in the ocean! So gear up for a challenge to your photography skills while you do some whale watching in Mirissa. The rest of the day is left for relaxation and some unique captures of the gorgeous sunset along the southern coast of Sri Lanka. Day 7 Habarana The next morning, before transferring to Negombo, you will stop and visit: Galle Dutch Fort. Kosgoda Turtle Hatchery. Upon reaching Negombo, we have scheduled an evening boat safari at the Muthurajawela Marsh, after which you will check in to Jetwing Blue for the night. Day 8 Departure On the last day, if time permits you can tour Negombo town and then head towards the airport. The wildlife photography tour of Sri Lanka now comes to an end. We have scheduled this as an eight-day tour but if you have lesser time or would like to spend more time on this tour, or if you would like to cover different attractions, do let us know. Our team of consultants is waiting to serve you and prepare a customized itinerary according to your requirements. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 8 Days Milk Feeding At Elephant Transit Home. Interesting Big Cats In Jungle Of Yala Catching Their Prey. Flocks Of Migrant Flamingos And Aquatic Birds In Bundala. Meet Great Whales And Lovable Dolphins In Mirissa. Places you will stay See available hotels Grand Udawalawe Safari Resort Udawalawe Sri Sharavi Beach Villas Kamburugamuwa Galle Face Hotel Colombo See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Sunscreen, hat, binoculars are a must when you’re in a Safari. Bring comfy cotton clothes to beat the heat. Stay relaxed!! That’s the best tip for the tour. FAQ What is the less crowded entrance in Yala National Park? Katagamuwa Entrance Are the jeep safaris shared or private basis? Jeep safaris will be on private basis. Can my chauffeur guide to take part with me in the safaris? Of course Yes! Your chauffeur will be there to assist you. How long does it take for whales watching? It will take about 04-05 hours.

itineraries.title: Explore Northwest East Central Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: This comprehensive Sri Lanka itinerary offers extensive cultural and natural exploration across multiple regions, featuring ancient cities, wildlife parks, hill country, and coastal experiences throughout the island.
itineraries.detail_title: Explore Northwest, East & Central Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Explore Northwest, East & Central Tour in Sri Lanka Explore Northwest, East and Central Tour in Sri Lanka is a combination of the best of wildlife, history and culture specifically focusing on covering the Northwest, East and the cultural triangle. Explore More Tailormade This Tours The North East of Sri Lanka is a world distinct from the rest of the island. The community has kept its distinct character while being closer to India than Colombo. Because Tamil migrants from southern India frequently invaded and colonized the Northeast, the culture of the region owes as much to Hindu India as it does to Buddhist Sri Lanka. In this Explore Northwest, East and Central Tour by Blue Lanka Tours, you are not only going to explore the culture and get to know the stories of the North and East, but also you will explore the North West with Kalpitiya and then discover the wildlife in Wilpattu National Park. With a little bit of everything, this itinerary is sure to cover the main highlights of the Northwest, Northeast, and the NorthCentral parts of Sri Lanka. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Kalpitiya Airport Pickup and transfer to Kalpitiya. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Overnight stay in Kalpitiya. Day2 Kalpitiya to Wilpattu Transfer to Fort Railway Station. Take a Scenic Train Ride to Jaffna from Colombo. Check-Into the hotel and Relax Overnight stay in Jaffna Day 3 Jaffna Morning Pay a Visit to Nallur Kovil. Explore Jaffna Museum. Taste Ice- Cream at Famous Rio Ice Cream Bar. Visit Jaffna Public Library. Visit Jaffna Fort. Overnight Stay in Jaffna. Day 4 Jaffna Take a ferry transfer to Nainativu Island. Pay a Visit to Nagapooshani Amman Kovil . Later Take Blessings from Nagadeepa Ancient Temple. Spend the evening at leisure. Overnight Stay in Jaffna Day 5 Jaffna Early Morning Leave for Kurikadduvan Jetty and Catch the ferry to Delft Island Explore Main Attractions on the Island Including the Following – Portuguese/Dutch Fort | Delft Island baobab tree | Old Dutch Hospital | Pigeon Nest | Dutch Horse Stables | Hanuman footprint Overnight Stay in Jaffna Day 6 Jaffna to Nilaveli Morning explore the local markets in Jaffna city and you may buy some snacks or food, which are authentic to the area. Transfer to Nilaveli Spend the rest of the day at leisure Overnight Stay in Nilaveli Day7 Nilaveli Full-Day Relax By The Beach Alternatively, You Can Enjoy Watersports Such as Snorkeling; Diving; Etc. Overnight Stay in Nilaveli Day8 Nilaveli Day at Leisure to Chill Out by The East Coast Beaches Late Evening Head to Uppuveli Beach for Drinks by the Water Overnight Stay in Nilaveli Day9 Nilaveli to Sigiriya Transfer to Sigiriya En Route Explore The Landmarks in Trincomalee Koneswaram Kovil | Fort Fedrick | Kanniya Hot Water Springs Evening Visit to Dambulla Cave Temple Overnight Stay in Sigiriya Day10 Sigiriya to Polonnaruwa Morning Experience a Sri Lankan Village Tour Enjoy a Catamaran Ride | Bullock-Cart Ride | Paddy Fields Walk Cooking and Tasting Authentic Rice and Curry Lunch at a Village House Transfer to Polonnaruwa Explore the Ancient Kingdom of Anuradhapura by Bicycles Overnight Stay in Sigiriya Day10 Sigiriya to Negombo Morning Climb to Sigiriya Rock Fortress Explore Murals | Royal Garden | Sigiriya Museum |Water Gardens | The Frescoes | Mirror Wall |Lion Paw Staircase | Sigiriya Summit Transfer to Negombo Check-Into The Hotel and Relax Overnight Stay in Negombo Day 14 Negombo to BIA Airport Transfer Journey Highlights Trip Length: 12 Days Explore Negombo’s mash-up of colonial and local architecture Visit the resplendent ruins of Polonnaruwa Climb Lion’s Rock – a proposed 8th wonder of the world Exploring Jaffna, walking through the colourful blocks and markets Island hopping in Jaffna ElephantRio Ice cream when in Jaffna ElephantSnorkelling in Tricomalee ElephantThe Game Drive in Wilpattu Places you will stay See available hotels Cinnamon Lodge Habarana C Beyond Nilaveli Kumpurupiddi Villa Hundira Negombo Leopard Trails Wilpattu Fox Resorts Jaffna Jaffna Dolphin Beach Ilanthadiya See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Explore Negombo’s mash-up of colonial and local architecture Visit the resplendent ruins of Polonnaruwa Climb Lion’s Rock – a proposed 8th wonder of the world Exploring Jaffna, walking through the colourful blocks and markets FAQ Is Dolphin watching available in Kalpitiya? – Yes, Kalpitiya is famous for Dolphin watching and also whale watching. How is the weather in Trincomalee? – Trincomalee is quite warm compared to Colombo. How long does the full day safari at Wilpattu take? – Approximately 10-12 hours. How long does it take to climb Sigiriya? – Depends on your fitness. In general, climbing up and down will take around 2.5 hours.

itineraries.title: Family Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Take your family on an exciting holiday by opting for this exclusively designed family tour in Sri Lanka. While the young and the fit…
itineraries.detail_title: Family Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Family Tour in Sri Lanka Take your family on an exciting holiday by opting for this exclusively designed family tour in Sri Lanka. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Take your family on an exciting holiday by opting for this exclusively designed family tour in Sri Lanka. While the young and the fit are away climbing the “Mini Adam’s Peak”, the old can sit back, relax and enjoy the views from their hotel, or everyone can enjoy a game drive day out at the Yala National Park. Every destination on this itinerary has something special to offer for the young and the old! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day2, 3 Sigiriya The next morning you will head off to Sigiriya and en route you have a chance to visit the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage and mingle with the elephants there. Your overnight stay on day two will be at Heritance Kandalama in Sigiriya. On the morning of day three, you will be taken to the Ancient City of Polonnaruwa where you can explore the ruins left by the Cholas Dynasty. Afterwards, you can experience an amazing Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Experience in the locality of Priyamali House. You will end the day by experiencing an amazing sunset atop the Sigiriya Lions Rock. Day4 Kandy On day four of this family trip in Sri Lanka will visit the Dambulla Cave Temple in the nearby city of Dambulla and then drive to the city of Kandy. Once there, you will undertake an amazing sightseeing experience around the Kandy Lake and towards the evening you will be taken to enjoy a cultural dance show. Your night stay will be at The Golden Crown hotel. Day5, 6 Nuwara Eliya In the morning, you will partake in the ceremonial event of the Temple of the Tooth. Afterwards, you will begin your journey to the beautiful town of Nuwara Eliya. En route, you stop for a visit at the lovely Ramboda Water Falls. In Nuwara Eliya, your night stay will be at Grand Hotel. On day six, we take you to explore the stunning Horton Plains and the trail to the “World’s End” point. Also on the schedule is a visit to the lavish tea plantations, Pedro Tea Factory and a boat safari on Gregory Lake. At the end of the day, you return to Grand Hotel for the night. Day7 Ella The following morning we have a beautiful train journey lined up for you from Nanu Oya to the breathtaking town of Ella. Upon reaching, you can visit the “Little Adams Peak” and the Rawana Water Falls. In Ella you also have the unique opportunity of taking part in a cookery class that teaches you how to make the authentic Sri Lankan Rice and Curry. Your overnight stay will be at 98 Acres Resort and Spa. Day8 Yala On day eight you will head towards the South of the island. On the way to the city of Yala, you will visit the Buduruwagala Buddha Statue. Once in Yala, you will be taken on an exciting Jeep Safari at the Yala National Park. Your night stay will be at Cinnamon Wild, Yala. Day9 Mirissa The next day you will be driven to the neighbouring city of Mirissa. En route, you can visit the fish market as well as observe the old and mind boggling tradition of stilt fishing. Once you check in to Sri Sharavi Beach Villas in Mirissa, you can relax on the beach for the rest of the day. Day10, 11, 12 Bentota Early next morning we will take you on a whale watching tour in Mirissa and then drive you to Galle, where you will explore the Galle Dutch Fort. Your next stop will be in Bentota, which is also on the southern coastline. Vivanta by Taj is the hotel you will be staying over for the night. As you approach the end of this Sri Lanka family tour, days eleven and twelve are completely dedicated towards relaxing at the beach, giving yourself a nice sun tan and spending quality time with your family. Day13, 14 Colombo On day thirteen, you will begin your journey to Colombo. On the way, we have lined up a visit to the turtle Hatchery and a boat ride on the Madu Lake. Once in Colombo, your night stay will be at Galle Face Hotel. Day fourteen is assigned for exploring the city and doing some shopping, provided you have sufficient time before the departure of your flight. You are now at the end of the wonderful and luxurious tour with Blue Lanka Tours. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 12 Days Giving yourself a lovely sun tan on the southern coast beaches of Sri Lanka. Absorb stunning views during your train journey across the hill country. Enjoy a family friendly jeep safari at the Yala National Park while looking out for unique animal species throughout the drive. Discover Captivating Cultures In Cultural Triangle As A Family Family mealPartaking In Cooking And Share A Delicious Lunch With A Local Family In A Village Train rideTake A Rocking And Rolling Train Journey To Hill Country Of Sri Lanka Wildlife tourWonder At Exotic Wildlife With A Safari In Yala National Park Places you will stay See available hotels The Golden Crown Kandy Sri Sharavi Beach Villas Kamburugamuwa Vivanta by Taj Negombo Heritance Kandalama Dambulla Cinnamon Wild Yala Heritance Tea Factory Nuwara Eliya Galle Face Hotel Colombo 98 Acres Resort Ella See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Families with teens, toddlers and elderly parents are welcome! You can visit Sri Lanka all-year-round, may be during summer break; gorgeous Christmasseason or in May half term just before the southern monsoon begin. Bike around Polonnaruwa to admire the temples and further let your kids coil of menacing but de-venomed snakes around their necks to make your excursion memorable Partake in evening pooja ceremony at Kandy temple and make an offering to the sacred tooth relic of Lord Buddha which is a humbling and captivating ritual that kids would have never seen before. Try a city tour in Kandy by Tuk-Tuk to get close to locals and taste some of the ‘must try’ street food delicacies along the ride with kids. Remember to request to reserve seats in second class the windows open up, to get best opportunity to soak in the surrounds and take a daring happy snap. Be sure to take plenty of water and snacks with you during trekking times, to keep energy levels of kids up. You are free to choose an afternoon jeep safari, as kids will not prefer to early dawn start safaris. Suggest taking seasick pills in the morning before embarking for whale watching, to minimize motion sickness. Make sure to bring your sunscreen with you. Please let us know if adults and kids have any dietary preferences or food allergies we should be informed the hotels of in the arrangements of your meals. FAQ Q. How many steps are there to climb Sigiriya Lions Rock? It has about 1200 steps to climb. Q. Is there any trek to Ramboda Water Falls? We offer only a sightseeing tour from it’s view point. Q. Do the hotels in Sri Lanka have interconnecting rooms? Yes, some hotels have interconnecting rooms. Q. Are baby seats available if needed? Yes, we will provide baby seats.

itineraries.title: Hill Country Photography Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: This hill country photography tour in Sri Lanka will give you just that! Taking you to viewpoints in Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Ella gives you the chance to capture…
itineraries.detail_title: Hill Country Photography Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Hill Country Photography Tour in Sri Lanka Embark on an unforgettable journey through Sri Lanka’s picturesque Hill Country with Blue Lanka Tours’ Photography Tour. Capture the breathtaking landscapes, lush tea plantations, cascading waterfalls, and charming colonial architecture that define this region. Led by expert guides, this tour offers unique opportunities to hone your photography skills while immersing yourself in the rich culture and natural beauty of the Hill Country. Whether you are a seasoned photographer or a novice enthusiast, this tour promises to inspire and delight as you explore the enchanting vistas and hidden gems of Sri Lanka’s highlands. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Looking to explore nature at its best with your camera? This hill country photography tour in Sri Lanka will give you just that! Taking you to viewpoints in Kandy, Nuwara Eliya and Ella gives you the chance to capture drop-dead gorgeous photographs of Sri Lanka’s natural landscapes of lush greenery, tea plantations and cascading waterfalls. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day 1 & 2 Kandy Upon your arrival at Bandaranaike International Airport, you’ll be greeted by a Blue Lanka chauffeur cum guide who will escort you to a private luxury vehicle. Your journey begins in Kandy, where you’ll visit the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic and capture the essence of the city from a popular viewpoint. Check into Polwatte Eco Lodge for a two-night stay. The following day, immerse yourself in an adventurous hiking tour amidst the Knuckles Mountain Range, offering unique photography opportunities. Day 3, 4 Nuwara Eliya On day 3, journey to Nuwara Eliya, stopping by the enchanting Ramboda waterfalls, Labookellie Tea Factory, and tea plantations along the way. Take a leisurely stroll around Gregory Lake, capturing the scenic beauty it offers. Spend two nights at Heritance Tea Factory. Early morning on day 4, embark on a nature trail in Horton Plains National Park, leading to the breathtaking World’s End. Visit Seetha Amman Temple and Hakgala Botanical Gardens later in the day to capture the beauty of flora. Day 5, 6 Ella The following morning, enjoy a mesmerizing train journey from Nanu Oya to Ella, renowned for its stunning landscapes. Check into the luxurious 98 Acres Resort and Spa for a two-night stay. Day 6 is dedicated to sightseeing, with opportunities to photograph iconic landmarks such as Lipton’s Seat, Nine Arches Bridge, and Little Adam’s Peak at sunset. Day 7 Colombo On day 7, visit Ravana, Diyaluma, and Bambarakanda waterfalls before heading to Marino Beach in Colombo for an overnight stay. Day 8 Colombo to BIA Explore camera-worthy attractions in Colombo before departing for Bandaranaike International Airport, depending on your flight schedule. We hope this hill country photography tour has sparked your passion for photography. Feel free to reach out to us for any customization or further assistance. The Blue Lanka team is dedicated to ensuring your satisfaction. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 8 Days Marvel at breathtaking panoramas from the World’s End viewpoint. Experience the majestic beauty of the Knuckles Range while trekking amidst stunning scenery. Indulge in a renowned cloud train journey to the charming town of Ella. Witness the fascinating journey of Ceylon Tea from bush to cup. Gain insight into the life of a tea picker. Explore Horton Plains on foot until you reach the iconic World’s End viewpoint. Insightful Tips for Adventure Seekers Tailored for photographers of all kinds! Both men and women are required to wear conservative attire covering their shoulders, chest, and knees before entering temple premises, as a sign of respect for the culture. Ensure you refrain from photographing with your back towards statues of Lord Buddha. Don’t miss the opportunity to capture the vibrant hustle and bustle of Kandy city, especially during sunset at picturesque spots like Kandy Lake. Opt for lightweight, breathable pants or shorts, along with sturdy trainers or hiking boots. Remember to pack essential hiking gear such as a hat, sunscreen, bug spray, a basic first aid kit, raincoat, and ample water. Head out in the morning to capture the scenic beauty of tea pickers at work in Nuwara Eliya for a truly memorable shot Always seek permission before photographing locals during train journeys, and ensure you use a fast shutter speed to avoid blurry images. Know which shutter speed is ideal for capturing stunning waterfall shots. Don’t miss out on photographing iconic locations like Little Adam’s Peak and the Nine Arch Bridge, adding a creative touch to your travel diary! FAQ Q. When is the best time to visit hill country? December to April is the best. Q. Can I bring a drone to Sri Lanka? A. If you wish to bring your drone with you, you will have to get a permit from the Civil Aviation Authority of Sri Lanka. https://www.caa.lk/en/drones#apply-for-drone-flying-permission Q. Do the hotels provide extra beds in the room? Yes, they do provide an extra bed for an additional cost. Q. Do you provide driver accommodation? Most of the hotels provide driver accommodation. If not they will find nearby. Places you will stay See available hotels Heritance Tea Factory Nuwara Eliya 98 Acres Resort Ella Madulkelle Tea and Eco Lodge Kandy Nine Skies Ella W15 Lake Gregory Nuwara Eliya Jetwing Kaduruketha Wellawaya See available Hotels View All

itineraries.title: Gem Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Sri Lanka is one of the largest gem producers of the world and has been known for its specialty gem stones for many centuries now…
itineraries.detail_title: Gem Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Gem Tour in Sri Lanka Sri Lanka is one of the largest gem producers of the world and has been known for its specialty gem stones for many centuries now… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka is one of the largest gem producers of the world and has been known for its specialty gem stones for many centuries now. A gems tour of Sri Lanka will give you an in-depth analysis of the gem field – what kind of gems are available, how it’s made, best places to purchase from and so on. So here’s presenting you the best possible opportunity to get your hands on those lovely colorful semi-precious Ceylon stones that you’ve been eyeing! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Beruwala Upon arrival at the Bandaranaike International Airport, a Blue Lanka representative will greet you and escort you to your private luxury vehicle. This representative will be your chauffeur cum guide for the rest of your Gem Tour in Sri Lanka. Your first stop will be a hotel check in after a 3 hour drive to the southern coast – Cinnamon Bey Beruwala. If time permits, an evening boat ride on the Madu Lake is possible. Day2 Galle On day 2, after breakfast, you will begin your excursion to Galle – which is an hour’s drive from Beruwala. Galle is rich in history with several colonial influences – the Portuguese being the most prominent. The remnant of the Dutch Fort (built in the 17th century) is a remarkable site to visit in Galle. The neighboring area is a peaceful and hospitable colony with a lot of restaurants, cafes, souvenir boutiques, ice cream shops and churches to explore. Once you are done exploring the city of Galle, on your return to Beruwala, you could stop at Ambalangoda to have a look at the famous traditional masks in Sri Lanka or else include a visit to the Kosgoda turtle hatchery. Day3, 4 Ratnapura The next morning, you will visit the Fairgems School in Beruwala. Aurelie, the director of the school will welcome you and guide you around. You can interact with the students and learn about their lives. You will then be given a lesson on gemstones. This workshop will include a theoretical introduction to the industry along with an opportunity to practice the preforming on a rough stone. After lunch, you will begin a 3 hour journey to Ratnapura – the heart of the gem industry. Lake Serenity Boutique Hotel is where you will be staying in Ratnapura. On Day 4 of your Sri Lanka gems tour, we will take you to a precious stone mine in Ratnapura, then to a gem market and also to visit the gemological museum. Day5 Udawalawe The next day, you will drive two hours to the Centauria Wild for an exciting stay amidst the jungle. After checking in and relaxing in the hotel, a jeep will be waiting for you at approximately 3.00pm for a safari ride through the Udawalawe National Park. Renowned for its elephant and bird population, you will also see crocodile, bear, sambar, and leopards – if you are lucky! Day6 Buttala The next morning you can visit the Elehant Transit Home to interact with baby elephants. This is a center where the baby elephants are cared for before they are released into the Udawalawe National Park. Two hours later, you will reach Buttala. Visit the Buduruwagala Temple and be amazed at the lovely Buddha carvings on the rock. Then take a pleasant walk into the forest to reach Tree Tops Jungle Lodge where you will be staying for the night. The lodge is located alongside Menik River, which means ‘river of gems’ in Sinhalese. Day7, 8 Ella On day 7 you will visit the Okkampitiya gemstone mine. Here you can discover stone extraction methods such as sieving and separating the stone gravel from earthy sands. After the mine visit, an hour’s drive away is the beautiful town of Ella in Sri Lanka’s hill country. Admire the lovely cascading Ravana Falls on the way. If you manage to reach Ella by early evening, it is a good idea to climb Little Adam’s Peak for a breathtaking view of the sunset from the summit. The hike takes about two hours up and down. On day 8, a tuk-tuk will be arranged for you in the neighboring town of Bandarawela. Here you will be taken through a ride amidst the gorgeous landscapes and luscious tea plantations. Stop at Lipton’s Seat for a snack with a stunning view. Sir Thomas Lipton is the genius of tea in Sri Lanka and this is where he liked to sit and sip on his tea. Day9, 10 Kandy (via train ride to Nanu Oya) The next morning, head on to the Ella Railway Station for an absolutely breathtaking train ride amidst beautiful mountains, valleys, tea plantations and villages – to the small town of Nanu Oya. Your Blue Lanka representative will meet you at the Nanu Oya Station and drive you to Kandy. On the way, you can stop at the scenic view of the Kotmale reservoir and also to see the Ramboda Waterfall. You will reach Kandy by evening so you have time for a visit to the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic before you check in to Kandy House for the night. On day 10, you can tour the Royal Botanical Garden in Peradeniya. Located alongside the Mahaweli River, this massive garden includes flora such as orchids, cannonball trees, royal palms and lovely colorful flower beds. Spare about 2 hours to explore this garden and then visit the stone museum in Kandy. Later that day, you can drive to the neighboring Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage for another interactive evening with the elephants. A Kandyan cultural dance show takes place every evening at 5.00pm. If time permits, you can enjoy this show before you return to your hotel for the night. Day11, 12 Sigiriya On the way to Sigiriya on day 11, stop at Matale to explore a batik factory and learn the art of traditional batik printing. Or another option is to experience the amazing Ayurvedic Herbal Gardens and discover traditional plants, fruits and the renowned spices of Sri Lanka. Here you can also learn about cultivation methods, harvesting, drying and how these plants/spices are used in traditional cooking and body care. Later that day, you can visit the Dambulla Cave Temple Complex which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Five Buddhist caves are perched on rocky hills and inside these caves are exclusive wall paintings, Buddha Statues and Stupas. Water Garden Hotel in the neighboring town of Sigiriya is where you will check in for the next few days of your Gem Trip in Sri Lanka. Day 12 is an excursion to the ancient city of Polonnaruwa. Touring on a bicycle is recommended for a different experience but if you wish, our chauffeur can take you there. During the ancient era, Polonnaruwa was the capital city of Sri Lanka for centuries. You will find ruins of Temples, Palaces, Buddhist Stupas and Statues. The Parakrama Samudra Lake is a nice place to cool off before you head back to your hotel in Sigiriya. Day13, 14 Colombo Early next morning on Day 13, head towards the Lion’s Rock in Sigiriya. Our guide will help you with the climb and take you to the right places where you can discover beautiful paintings and frescoes engraved into the rock. At the top of the rock are remnants of a Fortress built by King Kashyapa during the 10th century. Explore these at the summit and enjoy a tremendous view of the cultural triangle of Sri Lanka. Also classified as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, Lion’s Rock is one of the most popular tour attractions in Sri Lanka. After this activity, begin a five hour journey to the city of Colombo and check in to the exclusive Shangri La Hotel for the night where you can relax and unwind after a long and tiring day. On the last day of your Gem Tour Sri Lanka, you could choose to take a Tuk-Tuk City Tour accompanied with a guide to explore the trading hub, popular markets, important temples and some colonial buildings. Another option is to explore the city on your own and spend your time shopping for souvenirs and so on before it is time for you to head to the airport. One hour from Colombo is the Bandaranaike International Airport. This is where your Gem Tour in Sri Lanka comes to an end. Sincerely hoping that you found this itinerary attractive; we are waiting to hear from you. Please note that we are very flexible with changes to the itinerary so if you have any special requirements, kindly get in touch with us. History of Gems in Sri Lanka The history of the gems industry dates back to about 3000 years ago. According to the great Sri Lankan chronicle Mahavamsa, Ceylon was known as ‘Ratna Deepa’ in Sanskrit, meaning ‘the island of jewels’. Its soils, rich in minerals, have been yielding an amazingly continuous supply of premium quality precious and semi-precious gem stones. Ratnapura (translating to ‘city of gems’ in Sinhalese) owns a significantly wide range of gemstones, many of which are outstanding in clarity when compared to stones from other areas.Apart from producing these precious gems, the Sri Lankan gem industry has also been specializing in the process of mining, cutting and polishing for many centuries. Because Sri Lankans have an extensive knowledge on the types of gems and how they should be cut, each stone that is found and prepared for the foreign market is finely cut and polished before exported. Ceylon Moors, descendants of the Arabians are those who are largely responsible for the gem industry in Sri Lanka. This is because the Arabs undertook rich trading – the ability to speak a variety of languages and the wealth to travel across the globe in search of commercially valuable items. After the end of the civil war nearly a decade ago, Sri Lanka has been showing excellent growth in the service, industrial and agricultural sectors. With regard to the gem industry, regulations pertaining to mining and exports of gems have relaxed, thereby resulting in growth of the industry itself. Gem varieties available in Sri Lanka Since the early times of gem production, some off the common gemstones produced in Ceylon are: SapphiresRubyCat’s eyeAlexandriteGarnetStar sapphireZirconTourmalineSpinalTopazBerylQuartzMoonstoneSerendibiteEkaniteThe Blue Sapphire is one of the most supreme gems that Sri Lanka is renowned for. Some of the finest blue sapphires in the world are found here, one of which was gifted to Princess Diana by Prince Charles! Being second to diamond in hardness, this premium stone is amongst the highly prized of all gems. Did you know? The world’s largest sapphire – weighing 42 pounds, was found in the gravels of Sri Lanka.A British millionaire purchased from Sri Lanka the following:Blue Giant, weighing 500 caratsBlue Belle of Asia, weighing 400 carats Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days Practicing how to prepare a rough stone at the Fairgems school in Beruwala. Shopping for all your gem needs at the gem market in Ratnapura. Enjoying a breathtaking train ride in the hills of Sri Lanka – from Kandy to Nanu Oya. Galle FortWander Around The Majestic Galle Fort And Its Charming Cobblestone Streets Discover Stone Extraction Methods At A Gemstone Mine In Okkampitiya Go On A Safari At Udawalawe National Park To Spot Elephants In Their Natural Habitat Get Your Hands On Sparkling Colorful Ceylon Stones And Learn The Art Of Forming It Places you will stay See available hotels Nine Skies Ella Tree Tops Jungle Lodge Yala Water Garden Sigiriya Sigiriya Shangri-La Hotel – Colombo Colombo The Kandy House Kandy See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Trekking at the Knuckles Mountain Range Water Rafting Hike Little Adam’s Peak See the Beautiful Coral Reefs FAQ

itineraries.summary: Last Minute Tour in Sri Lanka is dedicated to last-minute travelers. Don’t worry about any last-minute decisions, our experts are ready for you at any given moment…
itineraries.detail_title: Last Minute Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Last Minute Tour in Sri Lanka Last Minute Tour in Sri Lanka is dedicated to last-minute travelers. Don’t worry about any last-minute decisions, our experts are ready for you at any given moment to accommodate all your last-minute requests and a well-planned tour. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka a destination for kicking back and enjoying the good life, but there is also a dynamic choice of Sri Lanka tours to pick from. Travelers will be in for a treat if they take advantage of Sri Lanka’s physical, cultural, and ethnic variety, therefore even if you decide on a last-minute tour to Sri Lanka, this itinerary will cover many highlights as possible for you. Covering the cultural sites, central city, up country, and down South, the Last Minute Tour in Sri Lanka itinerary is ideal for anyone who will be planning your tour just days or hours away from landing on the paradise island of Sri Lanka. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Negombo Arrive at Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) and receive airport assistance. Transfer to Negombo and check into a beautiful hotel in Negombo for a relaxing stay to overcome any jet lag. Enjoy the serene beaches of Negombo. Overnight stay in Coastal Negombo. Day2 Negombo to Wilpattu to Anuradhapura Transfer to Wilpattu and savor a traditional Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Lunch under the shady trees. Embark on an exhilarating Jeep Safari in Wilpattu National Park. Transfer to Anuradhapura for an overnight stay. Day 3 Anuradhapura to Sigiriya Explore the ancient city of Anuradhapura in the morning. Transfer to Sigiriya and check into your hotel for refreshments. Climb the iconic Sigiriya Rock Fortress in the evening to witness breathtaking sunsets. Overnight stay in Majestic Sigiriya. Day 4 Sigiriya to Kandy Journey to Kandy with stops at Dambulla Cave Temples and Nalanda Gedige. Visit the sacred Temple of the Tooth in the evening. Overnight stay in Pleasant Kandy. Day 5 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Visit Peradeniya Botanical Garden in the morning. Experience a scenic train ride to Nuwara Eliya, passing through tea plantations and picturesque landscapes. Indulge in a high tea experience at the hotel lawn in the evening. Overnight stay in Misty Nuwara Eliya. Day 6 Nuwara Eliya to Galle Explore a tea plantation and factory in the morning. Transfer to Galle with stops at Little England, Ella, and Wellawaya along the way. Overnight stay in Legendary Galle. Day 7 Galle Relax by the beach or enjoy hotel facilities in the morning. Take a leisurely walk on the ramparts of Galle Fort in the evening. Experience traditional street food within the fort. Overnight stay in Legendary Galle Day 8 Galle to BIA Transfer to Bandaranaike International Airport (BIA) for departure. Optional stop in Colombo to experience the city before departing. Journey Highlight Length of Trip: 08 days Embark on a thrilling game drive at Wilpattu National Park. Explore the ancient wonders of the Anuradhapura Kingdom. Discover the majestic waterfall in Ella. Witness the sacred Theva Pooja time at Kandy’s Temple of the Tooth. Indulge in delicious ice cream at Peddlers Ice Cream in Galle Fort. Places you will stay See available hotels Rajarata Hotel Anuradhapura Kumbukgaha Villa Sigiriya The Hill Club Nuwara Eliya Merchant Galle Fort Galle Regal Réseau Resort & Spa Negombo Grand Serendib Hotel Kandy See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips for Adventure Seekers Always apply sunscreen when in Sigiriya to protect your skin. Opt for breathable cotton attire to stay cool, ensuring shoulders and knees are covered when visiting temples.. Don’t forget your binoculars for spotting wildlife in Wilpattu. Essential beach gear: flip flops and sun-kissed tan lines. FAQ Q. How long does it take to explore the Anuradhapura Kingdom? Approximately 3-4 hours. Q. What is the Theva Pooja in the Temple of the Tooth? It’s a Pooja done daily for the Tooth Relic of Lord Buddha. Q. Can I go in a big cart around Peradeniya Botanical Gardens? Yes you definitely can and it cost around LKR 2000-3000 Q. Are the seafood restaurants in Galle? In Galle Fort, you will find plenty of seafood restaurants.

itineraries.title: Ramayana Tour
itineraries.summary: Many generations have been fascinated by the story of Ramayana and as a result, there has been a significant impact on art and culture throughout India and Southeast Asia…
itineraries.detail_title: Ramayana Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Ramayana Tour Many generations have been fascinated by the story of Ramayana and as a result, there has been a significant impact on art and culture throughout India and Southeast Asia… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Many generations have been fascinated by the story of Ramayana and as a result, there has been a significant impact on art and culture throughout India and Southeast Asia. This ancient poem is closely associated with Sri Lanka because several sites that prove a connection to the events in the story have been found in Sri Lanka. The Ramayana Tour in Sri Lanka is designed to explore only these particular sites and connections. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Arrival in Negombo Upon your arrival at Bandaranaike International Airport, you will be warmly welcomed by a representative from Blue Lanka, who will also be your guide for the Ramayana Tour. Your first stop will be the charming coastal town of Negombo, where you will spend your first night. If time allows, you can explore Negombo, including its vibrant fish market and the picturesque Dutch Canal. Day2 Journey to Kandy via Pinnawala After breakfast, check out from your hotel and embark on a 90-minute drive to Chilaw to visit the sacred Munneswaram Temple, where Lord Rama sought solace and blessings from God Shiva. Next, proceed to the Mannavari Temple, the first lingam established and worshipped by Lord Rama. En route to Kandy, visit the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage to witness these majestic creatures in their natural habitat. Day3 Explore Nuwara Eliya Today, you will be transferred to Nuwara Eliya for a one-night stay. Along the way, visit the Sri Bhaktha Hanuman Temple in Ramboda, constructed by the Chinmaya Mission, and known for its special Pooja ceremonies. In the evening, visit the Gayathri Peedam, a site believed to be blessed by Lord Shiva for King Ravana’s son. Day4 Nuwara Eliya Exploration Check out from your hotel and visit the Seetha Amman Temple, situated by a stream where Sita Devi is said to have bathed. Then, proceed to Divurumpola, where Sita Devi underwent a trial to prove her purity. Afterward, check in to your hotel in Bandarawela and enjoy the rest of the day at leisure. Day5 Journey to Kataragama via Ella After breakfast, head to Ella to visit the awe-inspiring Ravana Falls and the legendary Ravana Cave, where King Ravana is believed to have resided. These caves showcase the architectural brilliance of King Ravana and served as secret passages through the hills. Continue your journey to Kataragama for an overnight stay. Day6 Discover Colombo’s Ramayana Sites In the morning, visit the Kataragama shrine dedicated to God Skanda, followed by a visit to Ussangoda, a site believed to be one of King Ravana’s torched areas and an ancient airport. Proceed to Galle to visit Rumassala, where parts of the Himalayan herbs brought by Lord Hanuman are said to have fallen. Visit Seenigama Temple in Hikkaduwa, associated with the legendary battle between the Monkey King and King Ravana’s forces. Conclude the day with a visit to Panchamuga Anjaneyar Temple in Colombo, which features a unique Anjaneyar chariot. Day7 Departure Depending on your flight schedule, you may have the morning to explore Colombo before your chauffeur transfers you to the airport for your departure. We hope this Ramayana Tour has fulfilled your desire to explore the epic story in Sri Lanka. If you wish to extend your stay and explore more attractions, we can customize the itinerary according to your preferences. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 7 Days Witnessing the popular Poya day Pooja sessions at Sri Baktha Hanuman Temple in Ramboda. Taking a faithful oath at Divurumpola – where Sita Devi proved her chastity.River. Exploring the brilliance of King Ravana through the cave tunnels in Ella. Captivity Places Of Sita Devi – Ramboda And Seetha Amman Kovil The Pilgrimage Site Where Princess Sita Underwent To ‘Agni Pariksha’ Hidden Place Of King Ravana – Ravana Cave Pay Respect To God Kataragama Participating A Traditional Pooja Ceremony Places you will stay See available hotels The Golden Crown Kandy Galle Face Hotel Colombo Jetwing St. Andrews Nuwara Eliya Jetwing Beach Negombo See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Pack Wisely: Pack lightweight, breathable clothing, comfortable shoes, sunscreen, and insect repellent. Bring a hat and umbrella for sun protection. Respect Local Culture: Follow local customs like removing shoes before entering temples and dressing modestly to respect Sri Lanka’s rich cultural heritage. Be Flexible: Stay open to unexpected experiences and changes in the itinerary. Some of the best memories can come from deviating from the planned route. Capture Moments: Bring a camera to capture the beauty of the sites you visit, but be respectful of photography restrictions, especially in religious areas. Sample Local Cuisine: Try Sri Lankan dishes to fully experience the country’s diverse and flavorful culinary traditions. FAQ Is this Ramayana Tour a group tour or a private tour? We do arrange only private tours so this will be arranged exclusively for you. Can I visit Ramayana Sites such as Kovils/Temples at Pooja time? Yes, it is possible. We have to check pooja times in each kovil/temple to accommodate this. Can I visit Yala National Park during this tour? Yes, we can incorporate Yala into this itinerary but you have to wake up early in the morning to do the early morning safari in Yala National Park. Can I get a Tamil-speaking chauffeur guide? Normally we do provide only English-speaking chauffeur guides. But we can consider this request and will do the needful.

itineraries.title: Relax and Unwind in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Looking for a break from your fast paced routine? Escape to the pearl of the Indian Ocean for an absolute relax and unwind tour in Sri Lanka.
itineraries.detail_title: Relax and Unwind in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Relax and Unwind in Sri Lanka Looking for a break from your fast paced routine? Escape to the pearl of the Indian Ocean for an absolute relax and unwind tour in Sri Lanka. Specifically designed for bonding with Mother Nature… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Looking for a break from your fast paced routine? Escape to the pearl of the Indian Ocean for an absolute relax and unwind tour in Sri Lanka. Specifically designed for bonding with Mother Nature, this tour focuses on high end accommodation with beautiful natural surroundings. Whether you are with your partner, close friends or family, absolute unwinding of mind, body and soul is possible on this Sri Lanka tour. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Colombo At the Bandaranaike International Airport, a Blue Lanka representative will greet you to begin this exclusive relax and unwind tour in Sri Lanka. He will drive you to Colombo for your first night at The Residence by Uga Escapes on Bed and Breakfast basis. Day2, 3 Sigiriya During the morning of Day 2, your driver cum guide will pick you up at The Residence Hotel and drive you to the amazing city of Sigiriya. Here, you will get the chance of experiencing one of the most marvelous pieces of history that Sri Lanka would have to offer, which is the Sigiriya Lions Rock. After exploring this popular attraction, you will check in to the beautiful Jetwing Vil Uyana for the next two nights on Bed and Breakfast Basis. Day 3 is completely left out for you to enjoy the wonders that Jetwing Vil Uyana would have to offer you. Day 4, 5, 6 Kandy On Day 4, you will visit the Dambulla Cave Temple and then head towards Kandy for the next few nights. In Kandy, you will stay at Santani Wellness Resort & Spa – exclusively catered towards providing you with a relaxed holiday. On Day 5, you may leave the hotel for a tour of Kandy including sites such as the sacred Temple of the Tooth and the Kandy Lake. Apart from that, you have 3 days to enjoy, relax and unwind amidst breathtaking greenery at Santani Resort on Bed and Breakfast basis with an All Inclusive basis option available on request. Day7, 8 Haputale On day 7 of this Sri Lankan Relax and Unwind Tour, you will have a chance to explore even more of the panoramic natural beauty in the hill country when you visit the small town of Ella. Here you have two days at Thotalagala Bungalow on a valuable all-inclusive basis. Make the most of the quality time you have to spend with your loved ones amidst stunning views and cozy weather. Day9, 10 Yala By noon on Day 9 you will check in to Wild Coast Tented Lodge for a two night unique, nature friendly, yet luxurious accommodation experience on an all inclusive basis. The same afternoon, we have scheduled an amazing jeep safari for you at the Yala National Park. On Day 10, you can further explore the wildlife in Sri Lanka by going on a full day game drive while you are in Yala. Day11, 12, 13 Galle After another early morning jeep safari in Yala on day 11, you will be driven to the southern coast of Galle for the next three nights at Aditya Resort and Spa on bed and breakfast basis. You have 3 days to unwind and explore the exotic beach life in Sri Lanka. While in Galle, you can also tour the city and visit the Dutch Fort. Day14 Airport Day 14 brings you to the end of this Relax and Unwind Trip in Sri Lanka, where you will be driven to the International Airport depending on the time of your flight. We hope that this exclusively designed relaxation itinerary is just what you are looking for in a holiday. We focus on providing tailor made holiday experiences to our clients. Hence please note that any part of this itinerary can be customized to your preferences.
