Pampering yourself with a luxurious Ayurvedic spa treatment at any of the holiday resorts in Sri Lanka. Enjoying the luxury, nature friendly accommodation amidst the wild at Wild Coast Tented Lodge – Yala. Explore King Kashyapa’s Miracle Palace Of Sigiriya And Its Colorful Frescoes Get Rejuvenated With Holistic Wellness At Santani Sanctuary Walk Through Mountainous Landscapes And Lush Green Tea Plantations Stroll In The Laidback Streets Of The Galle Fort Places you will stay See available hotels Residence by Uga Escapes Colombo Jetwing Vil Uyana Sigiriya Aditya Resort Rathgama See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips An evening cultural dance show in Kandy. FAQ How far is the Santani Wellness Resort & Spa from Kandy Town? Approximately around 01 & ½ hours drive time and about 35km away from Kandy Town. Does Thotalagala Bungalow accept kids? They do not accept children under 8 years of age. What type of jeep model will be used for the game drives? Toyota Hilux (Open Safari Jeep) How many days before should I apply for the visa? You can apply for an online visa closer to your arrival date.

itineraries.title: Shopping Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Shopping in Sri Lanka is a treat, as anybody who has visited this beautiful island nation will attest. There’s a lot to take home, from wonderfully created jewelry to batiks…
itineraries.detail_title: Shopping Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Shopping Tour in Sri Lanka Shopping in Sri Lanka is a treat, as anybody who has visited this beautiful island nation will attest. There’s a lot to take home, from wonderfully created jewelry to batiks and more, at costs you’d cheerfully pay. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Shopping in Sri Lanka is a treat, as anybody who has visited this beautiful island nation will attest. There’s a lot to take home, from wonderfully created jewelry to batiks and more, at costs you’d cheerfully pay. Not only that but major towns like Colombo and Kandy sell everything from typical street to high-end stuff, leaving one spoiled for choice. From gorgeous stone artworks to glittering jewels and bright saris, shopping in Sri Lanka will make you appreciate everything that this wonderful nation has to offer its visitors. If you’re planning a trip to Sri Lanka, make sure you go on a shopping frenzy like the natives! But first, remember, Sri Lanka is much more than just shopping. What you get in Sri Lanka as a shopping tour is not what you get in other countries. What you get is the authenticity of traditions with Handicrafts, gems, spices, silks, batiks, and many of those you wouldn’t typically get in the streets of New York. Yes, Sri Lanka does have fancy brands, but it is not for the luxury branded spoils. The shopping tour is a combination of culture, heritage, and history. Whilst a typical shopping tour may take you more of the south, central and western provinces in Sri Lanka, Blue Lanka Tours’ shopping tour takes a little detour up North to Jaffna. Why Jaffna? Jaffna is the northern most point in Sri Lanka, closer to India. The atmosphere, the streets, the culture, its all a mini India. Streets are full of colourful delights of many local sweets and drinks. You also get the Indian Saari culture in Jaffna which you may not be able to find in another place more so authentic than in Jaffna streets. This shopping tour also combines the attractions of UNESCO Sites, Village tours, City Tours and many more of everything that you need to see when in Sri Lanka in the middle of your shopping spree. Blue Lanka Tours’ Shopping Tour is always a top choice of our beloved clients. Whilst, the itinerary is a guiding light, clients are free to tweak it as they wish as we tailor make all tours to fit your dates, preferences and selections. Read through the Shopping Tour Itinerary and hit us up on our chat box to get more details. Happy Reading !!! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day 1 BIA to Chilaw Airport Pickup and Transfer to Chilaw. Overnight stay in Chilaw. Day2 Chilaw to Jaffna Morning Visit to a Local Fish Market – ‘Lellama’. Transfer to Jaffna. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Overnight stay in Jaffna. Day3 Jaffna Visit to Nallur Kovil. Explore Jaffna Museum. Taste Ice- Cream at Famous Rio Ice Cream Bar. Visit to Jaffna Public Library. Visit to Jaffna Fort. Overnight Stay in Jaffna. Day4 Jaffna to Dambulla Morning Enjoy a Market Tour in Jaffna City. Transfer to Dambulla. Relax and Enjoy Hotel Facilities. Overnight stay in Dambulla. Day5 Dambulla Morning Visit to Dambulla Local Market. Experience Sri Lankan Village Tour. Evening Exploration of Sigiriya Rock Fortress Overnight stay in Dambulla Day6 Dambulla to Kandy Transfer to Kandy. En Route Visit to a Spice Garden Where You Can Buy Edible Souvenirs That You Can Take Back Home. Stop at a Brassware Village to See the Process of Making Brassware Items. Blessings at Kandy Tooth Relic Temple During Theva Time. Overnight stay in Kandy Day7 Kandy Sightseeing Around Kandy City and Lake by Tuk-Tuks. Visit to a Handloom and Batik Making Factory. Visit to a Gem Lapidary Where You Can Witness Gem Making Process and Buy if Needed. Evening Witness Cultural Dance Show. Overnight stay in Kandy Day8 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Transfer to Nuwara Eliya. En Route Witness Ramboda Waterfall. Visit to a Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory. Experrience Tea Plucking and Tea Tasting. Evening Drive Through Little England. Overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya Day9 Nuwara Eliya to Ella to Yala Take a Scenic Train Ride to Ella from Nanu Oya. Transfer to Yala. En Route Witness Ravana Waterfall. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Overnight stay in Yala. Day10 Yala Morning Exploration of Countryside of Yala. Witness Buffaloa Curd Making Process | Pottery Making | Palmyrah Weaving. Afternoon Jeep Safari in Yala National Park. Overnight stay in Yala. Day11 Yala to Galle Transfer to Galle. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Evening Exploration of Galle Fort. Overnight stay in Yala. Day12 Galle Morning Learn to Carve and Paint a Traditional Sri Lankan Mask with The Help of a Celebrated Mask Carving Artist. Later Visit to a Mask Museum. Afternoon Take a Stroll in the Streets of Galle City and Fort. Overnight stay in Galle. Day13 Galle to Colombo Transfer to Colombo. En Route Visit to a Sea Turtle Conservation Center. Visit to Witness Coconut Husk Products. Overnight stay in Colombo Day14 Colombo Morning Exploration of Colombo City. Enjoy Shopping in Colombo. Relax at The Hotel. Overnight stay in Colombo Day15 Colombo to BIA Airport Transfer Journey Highlights Trip length: 15 Days Handicrafts and the making of them are a delight to your eyes. Experience the Sri Lankan Batik culture. The importance of gems in Sri Lanka. Little shops that have souvenirs for friends and family. Enjoy local food joints. Places you will stay See available hotels Ananthaya Resort & Spa Chillaw Fox Resorts Jaffna Jaffna Saraii Village Sigiriya The Radh Kandy Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya Cinnamon Grand Colombo The Galle Fort Hotel Galle See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Always take clothes that are very comfortable and easy. Be prepared for more stops along the way for your shopping tour. Always remember to carry LKR with you when you go to small shops. Always relax, enjoy, and indulge in everything throughout the tour. FAQ What I can shop for in Sri Lanka? Gems & Jewelry, Ceylon Tea, Wooden Masks, Antiques, Sri Lankan Sarees, Handicrafts, Souvenirs, Spices. What is the best time to visit Jaffna? Anytime between January to March would be best. Does Sri Lanka exports gems? Gems purchased in Sri Lanka can be exported either by air freight or by hand by the owner. Ratna gems are submitted to the National Gems and Jewelry Authority along with the Common Customs Declaration. Can I pay with my debit/credit card? You can, but not everywhere. Major stores accept Visa and MasterCard, but you won’t find this option in local shops, markets, and restaurants. So the best way is to always have some cash with you.

itineraries.title: Short Break Tour In Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Looking for a short and fun-filled break in Sri Lanka? Then look no further and embrace this 3-day holiday package we carefully designed to offer you the maximum benefit…
itineraries.detail_title: Short Break Tour In Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Short Break Tour In Sri Lanka Looking for a short and fun-filled break in Sri Lanka? Then look no further and embrace this 3-day holiday package we carefully designed to offer you the maximum benefit … Explore More Tailormade This Tours Looking for a short and fun-filled break in Sri Lanka? Then look no further and embrace this 3-day holiday package we carefully designed to offer you the maximum benefit of your momentary stay inthis magical island. Enjoy a cultural feast packed 3 days including visits to impressive ancient rock fortress Sigiriya, Dambulla cave temples, beautiful Kandy city and most favourit Pinnawala elephant orphanage! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Kandy Arrival in Sri Lanka in the style you deserve and meet with Blue Lanka Tours representative and the chauffer guide at the Airport arrival lounge. We always recommend an early arrival to enjoy this tour to the fullest! To begin your Sri Lanka holiday, journey to Kandy – the last Sinhalese kingdom. Along the way, stop at Pinnawala elephant orphanage to show some love and care to abandoned, injured Asian elephants including baby elephants. Treat your eyes with playful activities of these adorable creatures like bathing, feeding, engaging in mischief and more. In the evening visit Dalada Maligawa or the Temple of the Tooth, which houses the country’s most sacred relic: a tooth said to belong to the Gautama Buddha. Wander in the busy streets of Kandy and around the lake before checking-into your hotel. Day2 Sigiriya Rise early and enjoy a tasteful breakfast at the hotel. Leave to Sigiriya enthusiastically as you are going to discover two of the UNESCO World Heritage Sites in Sri Lanka on this day. Step into cave temple complex in Dambulla on your way, spreads through five caves that features thousands of metres of painted walls containing more than 150 images of Lord Buddha, as well as other deities and Sri Lankan kings. It is also stated that, once this place has been served as dwellings for prehistoric Sri Lankans before the arrival of Buddhism. Continue your ride to Sigiriya. Upon reaching, get ready to climb the 5th century rock fortress and explore the ancient citadel built on top of this 200 meters tall rock by king Kashyapa. Once you get on the top, enjoy breathtaking views over the surrounding landscape. Spend overnight at a hotel in Sigiriya and have restful sleep prior to departure. Day3 Airport As you short stay in Sri Lanka comes to and end today, pack-up and check-out from Sigiriya hotel to leave to Airport. In any case your flight departs at night, head to the bustling South Asian City of Colombo to explore iconic attractions prior to departure. Feel free to contact our friendly travel consultants to book this package or tailor it according to your liking. Remember, there are plenty of things to see and experience within our shores! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 3 Days Discovering Dambulla’s maze of impressive cave temples Gazing at the Sacred Temple of the Tooth Relic in Kandy Basking in the panoramic views from the top of the Lion Rock Be A Part Of The Tremendous Efforts Taken For The Welfare And Protection Of Orphaned Elephants In Sri Lanka With A Visit To Pinnawala Take A Twilight Guided Tour Through The Incense Scented Grounds Of Kandy Temple To Make Your Offerin Climb The BIG Rock Of Sigiriya And Learn About Its Backstories Explore The True Sri Lankan City Of Dambulla And Its Treasure Temples Places you will stay See available hotels Grand Kandyan Hotel Kandy Sigiriya King’s Resort Sigiriya Heritance Kandalama Dambulla See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Trekking at the Knuckles Mountain Range Water Rafting Hike Little Adam’s Peak See the Beautiful Coral Reefs FAQ How far is the Kandy from air port? Kandy is about 115km away from the airport via Central Expressway. What is the transportation method? Transportation will be provided by an air-conditioned private vehicle. Do Halal Food available at hotels? Most of the hotels provide halal food. Can I do hot air ballooning in Sigiriya? Yes, it can be done early in the morning when you are in Sigiriya and depends on the weather condition.

itineraries.detail_title: Short Break Tour in Sri Lanka 1
itineraries.detail_text: Short Break Tour in Sri Lanka 1 Blue Lanka’s Short Break Adventures are sure to tick all the boxes, whether you’re looking for the ultimate holiday add-on or a small adventure. They are full of value, take the effort out of planning, and help you get the most out of your next vacation. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Short breaks are an excellent addition to a larger vacation/part vacation – or a short adventure in their own right. The last 2-8 days range from beach breaks to guided trips or are centered on an activity such as exploring the UNESCO Highlights in Sri Lanka, exploring the coastal areas with a bit of city, or a combination of highlights that you can see during a very short period. Departures are common and almost always assured. Short Breaks are slightly different from our round tours in that you preserve some independence during downtime, such as a more relaxing and more night. Besides, Blue Lanka Tours’ short break tour of 04 Days is also ideal for traveling with friends, family, or a group of other enthusiasts, and we can design a custom journey just for you. It’s never been simpler to fit in a quick getaway to see the beaches, the UNESCO sites, or take a Safari to the Yala Jungles – all in just a few days in Sri Lanka but you’ll be surprised at how much you can get done for you, and cater to your requests. Consider this 04 Days Short Break Tour as a started guide and hit us up when you need a similar one done. The team awaits your inquiry. Happy Browsing through Blue Lanka! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day 1 BIA to Sigiriya Airport Pickup & Transfer to Sigiriya En route visit Dambulla Caves Temple Overnight stay in Sigiriya Your first day is a direct transfer from your arrival at the airport to Sigiriya. A highlight of Sri Lanka and would allow you to cover at least two UNESCO World Heritage Sites. You will explore UNESCO site no.1 as you head to Sigiriya, in Dambulla. You will explore the Dambulla Cave Temples. This cave monastery, with its five sanctuaries, has been a revered pilgrimage site for 22 centuries and is Sri Lanka’s biggest and best-preserved cave-temple complex. The Buddhist mural paintings (covering an area of 2,100 m2), as well as the 157 sculptures, are particularly significant. So this is a must-do en route and one of the highlights of this Short Break Tour. Day2 Sigiriya to Kandy Morning explore Sigiriya Lions Rock Transfer to Kandy Visit Matale Alu Viharaya Temple Evening Enjoy Cultural Dance Show at 05.00 pm Overnight stay in Kandy Day 02 is rather long so brace yourselves. You will start the morning by climbing Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Return and you will check out to head to Kandy. En route you may pay a visit to Matale Alu Viharaya. You will then be taken directly to watch the Cultural Dance Show. The Kandy Lake Club Cultural Dance Show is an enthralling fusion of Sri Lankan arts, dance, and cultural heritage that is a must-see. Take advantage of this chance to learn more about cultural ethics via the Kandyan dance and Sri Lanka’s iconic national dance, the Kohomba Kapkariya. You will then check in to the hotel to relax and enjoy a restful night in Kandy. Day3 Kandy to Colombo Morning Blessings at Kandy Temple of Tooth Explore Kandy City Tour Transfer to Colombo En-route visit Peradeniya Royal Botanical Gardens Overnight stay in Colombo Your Day 03 will start with the Theva Pooja in the morning seeking blessings at Kandy Temple of the Tooth. You will then explore Kandy city as you walk through the lake round, the compact colonial city, witnessing the main highlights of Kandy City. Post City Tour you will proceed to visit the infamous Botanical Gardens in Peradeniya after which you will be directly transferred to Colombo. Day4 Colombo to BIA Explore Colombo City Tour Airport Transfer Your morning will start with a Cityscape Colombo Tour. All highlights will be capturedallowing you to witness the busy city of Colombo and all that comes with it. Your tour will conclude in Colombo. Journey Highlights Trip length: 4 Days At the top of Sigiriya Rock Fortress Walking around the Lake in Kandy Lost in the history of Dambulla Caves Exploring Colombo cityscapes The monkeys and the elephants you may come across while in Sigiriya Vistas from the top of Sigiriya Rock Taking a stroll in Pettah when in Colombo The Pooja in Kandy Temple of The Tooth Places you will stay See available hotels The Radh Kandy Cinnamon Lakeside Colombo See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Be mindful about your attire when you visit Temples Carry your Sun Screen with you wherever you go Carry your Sun Screen with you wherever you go Relax & Enjoy while you explore our UNESCO World Heritage Sites FAQ What is the best time of the day to visit Sigiriya Lions Rock? It’s always good to go in the morning hours. What is the biggest botanical garden in Sri Lanka? Royal Botanical Garden in Peradeniya which is already included to this short break tour 01 itinerary. Is it possible to obtain a ayurvedic treatment and from where? Yes, it is possible. We suggest you Athreya Ayurveda Ashram in Habarana to relax with an herbal massage. Is it safe to drink the tap water? It is strongly advised not to drink tap water in Sri Lank It’s best to stick to treated and filtered water.

Explore More Tailormade This Tours The Blue Lanka Tours short break tour is also ideal for traveling with friends, family, or a group of other enthusiasts, and we can design a custom journey just for you. It’s never been simpler to fit in a quick getaway to see the beaches, the UNESCO sites or take a Safari in to the Jungles – all in just a few days in Sri Lanka but you’ll be surprised at how much you can get done for you, and cater to your requests. This Short Break Tour Option is a combination of City, Beach and a History. Consider this Short Break Tour as a starting guide and hit us up when you need a similar one done. Explore Colombo City Tour. Overnight stay in Colombo. Day2 Colombo to Udawalawa Transfer to Udawalawa. Visit Elephant Transit Home. Afternoon Jeep Safari in Udawalawa National Park. Overnight stay in Udawalawa Day 3 Udawalawa to Galle Transfer to Galle. En route witness Stilt Fishermen (Photo Purpose Only and Depending on your interest). Explore Galle Old Dutch Fort. Places you will stay See available hotels Cinnamon Lakeside Colombo Grand Udawalawe Safari Resort Udawalawe The Galle Fort Hotel Galle See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Carry your Sun Screen with you wherever you go. Slip into Cotton Clothes to be as comfortable as possible. Relax & Enjoy while you are on this Short Break. Flip Flops are a must. FAQ Q. How long does it take for the jeep safari in Udawalawa? It does take about 03 hours to complete. From 03.00pm to 06.00pm. Q. What kind of weather can I expect in Galle? Galle is on the coast and has temperatures ranging between 23℃ (74℉) to a maximum of 31℃ (87.8℉). Q. What languages are spoken in Sri Lanka? Sinhala and Tamil are spoken along with English. Q. Is Sri Lanka a suitable destination for children? Yes, Sri Lanka is a very child-friendly country. Hotels and guest houses often have family rooms. Children are well-catered for in restaurants. Baby food and nappies are available in supermarkets. There are many child-friendly attractions in Sri Lanka such as Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage,Dehiwala Zoo, National Parks, Turtles, Train journey in the Hill Country etc.

itineraries.title: Solo Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: Blue Lanka Tours are one of the leading luxury travel companies also catering to the niche of solo wanderers, by taking extra care to ensure the safety of female/male solo travellers…
itineraries.detail_title: Solo Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: Solo Tour in Sri Lanka Blue Lanka Tours are one of the leading luxury travel companies also catering to the niche of solo wanderers, by taking extra care to ensure the safety of female/male solo travellers, specialising in designing luxury solo trips in Sri Lanka to the satisfaction of our valuable clients. Explore More Tailormade This Tours Sri Lanka is a wonderful destination to visit, particularly for people interested in culture and nature. Our itinerary is for all single female/male travellers around in Sri Lanka, including where to stay, which excursions and activities to choose, and how to get around, is provided below. Yes, Sri Lanka is a defe destination for female solo travellers. No question there. Most importantly, Blue Lanka Tours has always been accommodating many solo travellers around the world and our Blue Lanka Guides are the best of best who will give you a safe, secure and super duper solo travel of your life. With Blue Lanka Solo escorted trips –you may get lost in the nature or find your spirit among the stunning beaches and lush green mountains of Sri Lanka, the tropical paradise island. This Itinerary captures the best of Sri Lanka for the discerning Solo traveler with Blue Lanka Tours. So here’s a Ching Ching to the beginning of planning your solo trip in Sri Lanka, with Blue Lanka Tours. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Negombo Airport Pickup and Transfer to Negombo Overnight stay in Negombo Day2 Negombo to Anuradhapura Optional – Visit to a Local Fish Market – ‘Lellama’ Transfer to Anuradhapura. Exploration of Anuradhapura Ancient City. Overnight stay in Anuradhapura. Day 3 Anuradhapura to Trincomalee Transfer to Trincomalee. En Route Climb to Mihintale. Relax By The Beach. Overnight stay in Trincomalee. Day 4 Trincomalee Morning Visit to Koneswaram Kovil. Visit to Fort Fredrick – Dutch remains of the country. Have a hot water bath at natural hot spring Kanniya. Overnight stay in Trincomalee. Day 5 Trincomalee to Habarana Transfer to Habarana. Experience Sri Lankan Village Tour. Evening Exploration of Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Overnight stay in Habarana. Day 6 Habarana to Polonnaruwa Morning Hike to Pidurangala Rock. Transfer to Polonnaruwa. Exploration of Polonnaruwa Ancient City by Bicycle. Overnight stay in Habarana. Day7 Kandy Transfer to Kandy. En Route Visit to Dambulla Cave Temple. Visit a Nalanda Gedige. Witness Cultural Dance Show. Blessings at Kandy Tooth Relic Temple. Overnight stay in Kandy. Day8 Kandy to Nuwara Eliya Morning Take a Stroll Along the Botanical Garden of Peradeniya. Transfer to Nuwara Eliya. En Route Enjoy a Sri Lankan Rice and Curry Lunch Viewing the Stunning Ramboda Waterfall. Check-Into The Hotel and Relax. Overnight Stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day9 Nuwara Eliya Visit to a Working Tea Plantation and a Tea Factory. Drive Through Little England Exploring The Grand Hotel, Old Post Office and other British remains. Relax at The Hotel. Overnight Stay in Nuwara Eliya Day10 Nuwara Eliya to Ella Morning Drive Through Little England. Take a Scenic Train Ride to Ella from Nanu Oya. Visit to Nine Arch Bridge. Experience Cooking and Dining at a Tyical Local Place. Overnight Stay in Ella. Day11 Ella to Yala Morning Hike to Little Adam’s Peak. Transfer to Yala. En Route Witness Ravana Waterfall. Pay a Visit to Buduruwagala Temple. Overnight Stay in Yala. Day12 Yala to Tangalle Morning Jeep Safari in Yala National Park. Transfer to Tangalle. Relax By The Beach. Overnight Stay in Tangalle. Day13 Tangalle Relax By The Beach. Overnight stay in Tangalle. Day 14 Tangalle to Colombo Transfer to Colombo. En Route Exploration of Galle Fort and City. Visit to a Sea Turtle Conservation Center. Exploration of Colombo City. Overnight stay in Colombo. Day 15 Colombo to BIA Airport Transfer. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 15 Days Taking the most scenic train ride from Nanuoya to Ella. Spotting Leopards at Yala. Village Tour & Cookery Demo. Enjoying the Cultural Heritage sites. Explore Galle Fort. Places you will stay See available hotels Heritance Negombo Negombo Trinco Blu by Cinnamon Trincomalee Cinnamon Wild Yala The Golden Crown Kandy Jetwing St. Andrews Nuwara Eliya 98 Acres Resort Ella Anantara Peace Haven Tangalle Galle Face Hotel Colombo See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Sunscreen, hat, binoculars are a must when you’re in a Safari. Bring comfy cotton clothes to beat the heat. For Hill Country, bring your sweaters. Always carry your camera around or keep your phone charged for beautiful pictures. Stay relaxed!! That’s the best tip for the tour. FAQ Is it safe to travel to Sri Lanka alone? Yes of course, Sri Lanka is a safe country and best place for solo traveling. What are the not to miss in Sri Lanka? You must visit cultural triangle, central highlands, the most beautiful train journey to Ella, Wild National Parks and don’t forget to spend some time on the beach. Will the chauffeur be there to explore sites with me? Yes, your chauffeur will accompany you throughout the tour and he will explain and share his knowledge with you every pass by. Can I obtain on arrival visa? You have to apply e-visa through our online portal https://eta.gov.lk/slvisa/

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour
itineraries.summary: Grab this exclusive offer of Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour where you have the opportunity to explore Sri Lanka’s vast variety of tourism as well as the pristine beaches of Maldives…
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour Grab this exclusive offer of Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour where you have the opportunity to explore Sri Lanka’s vast variety of tourism as well as the pristine beaches of Maldives… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Grab this exclusive offer of Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour where you have the opportunity to explore Sri Lanka’s vast variety of tourism as well as the pristine beaches of Maldives. Most of Sri Lanka’s cultural and historical attractions of Sigiriya and Kandy are highlighted here along with a few days in the beautiful hill country of Nuwara Eliya, after which you will transfer to the Maldives for absolute unwinding with sun, sand, and water. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 – 3 Sigiriya At the Bandaranaike International Airport, you will meet our Blue Lanka representative who will assist you with your luggage and take you to an ultra-comfortable and luxurious vehicle which will be your ride throughout the tour in Sri Lanka. This representative will be your chauffeur cum guide and will be more than happy to help you with anything you need in Sri Lanka. Your first stop on this Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour will be the popular town of Sigiriya, which is about four and a half hour’s drive from the airport. After the tiring flight and onward road journey, you will check in to the epitome of luxury at Heritance Kandalama Hotel and relax for the rest of the day. After breakfast the next morning, your guide will drive you to the ancient city of Polonnaruwa (one and a half hours away), where you can explore the historical ruins and then stop for an authentic rice and curry lunch treat at the famous Priyamali Gedara. In the afternoon, you have an exciting jeep safari scheduled at the Minneriya National Park. After the one-of-a-kind exploration of Sri Lanka’s wildlife, you will be taken back to Heritance Kandalama for the night. On day 3, finish your breakfast at the hotel and head towards the Lion’s Rock in Sigiriya. Getting to the top involves a climb of about 1200 steps and is quite easy for most people. Don’t forget to observe the engraved frescoes and beautiful paintings inside the rock. At the top are remnants of a rock fortress built by one of the ancient Kings of Sri Lanka. The rest of the day is left at leisure at Heritance Kandalama. Day4 Kandy From Sigiriya to Kandy is a two and a half hour drive but it will take longer to reach because en-route this trip we have scheduled a visit to: Dambulla Rock Cave Temples (about 300-400 steps to climb) Ayurvedic Herbal Gardens Royal Botanical Gardens It will most likely be early evening by the time you reach Kandy so you will check in to The Golden Crown Hotel to relax for the rest of the day. Day5, 6 Nuwara Eliya After breakfast the next morning, you will visit the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic in Kandy and obtain early morning blessings from Buddhist Monks at the Temple. Two and a half hours later, you will reach the beautiful town of Nuwara Eliya, also known as “Little England”. En route, you will visit the beautiful Ramboda waterfalls and also enjoy a unique tea plucking experience along with a visit to a tea factory. At the end of the day, you will check in to The Grand Hotel in Nuwara Eliya. Scheduled for Day 6 of this tour is an early morning hike at Horton Plains (9.5kms). Your breakfast will be packed to go by the hotel staff. After the hike and satisfaction of stunning views at World’s End Point, you can relax for the rest of the morning. Later that day, our chauffeur will take you on a tour of Nuwara Eliya town and bring you back to the hotel for the night. Day7 Colombo The drive to Colombo from Nuwara Eliya is about six hours. So depending on the time you arrive, you could either opt for relaxation at Marino Beach hotel or take a city tour of Colombo, thereby ending the tour of Sri Lanka. Day8 Transfer to airport Your flight to the Maldives will be scheduled on Day 08. Upon landing at Male Airport, you will be transferred to Kurumba Beach Resort where you will stay in a beachfront deluxe room on an all-inclusive dining basis. Day9 – 12 Maldives The next four days of this Sri Lanka and Maldives tour is left for ultimate relaxing, unwinding and rejuvenating at the gorgeous beach resort in the Maldives. Enjoy the beautiful sunshine, calm waters, white sands and peace and tranquil. Indulge in some snorkeling to explore the stunning marine life and opt for other water sports activities such as jet skiing, deep sea diving, surfing, and anything else that interests you. Day13 Transfer to airport This brings you to the end of a combined Sri Lanka and Maldives Tour. We hope this itinerary was attractive to you. Exploring two destinations in one holiday is a tempting offer indeed. Please note that this itinerary can be customized in any way required. The Blue Lanka team looks forward to hearing from you. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 13 Days Exploring historical ruins, Stupas, and Pagodas in the ancient city of Polonnaruwa Indulging in breathtaking views at the World’s End Point at Horton Plains National Park Bathing in sun, sand, and water on the exotic beaches of Maldives. Check Out The Legendary Archaeological Museum In Polonnaruwa Experience The Thrills Of Elephant Safari At Minneriya National Park Explore The Splendor Of The Horton Plains With A Leisurely Trek Indulge In Snorkeling In The Crystal-Clear Waters Of Maldives Places you will stay See available hotels Kurumba Beach Resort Bentota The Golden Crown Kandy Heritance Kandalama Dambulla Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips A visit to the spectacular “Baker’s Falls” while on your Horton Plains hike FAQ How much time is required to explore Horton Plains? It will take about 03-05 hours depending on your fitness & efficiency level. When is the best time go Maldives? With warm temperatures year-round, the Maldives offer the most ideal weather from November to February. March to April also a great time to visit, especially for surfers. What would be the most preferred meal plan in Maldives? It would be All Inclusive Basis. Do I need a visa to visit Maldives? Visa is free on arrival for all countries.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Itinerary 7 Days
itineraries.summary: Looking for a short holiday to relax, unwind and also indulge in some fun activities? This Sri Lanka itinerary 07 days is just what you need…
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Itinerary 7 Days
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Itinerary 7 Days Looking for a short holiday to relax, unwind and also indulge in some fun activities? This Sri Lanka itinerary 07 days is just what you need… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Looking for a short holiday to relax, unwind and also indulge in some fun activities? This Sri Lanka itinerary 07 days is just what you need. A one week tour of the country’s attractions will give you a gist of beaches, hill country, wildlife and everyday life in Sri Lanka. To top it all, the luxury accommodation provides an ideal atmosphere to unwind and prepare for days full of memorable activities! Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Negombo As you exit the Bandaranaike International Airport, a Blue Lanka Representative will greet you and guide you towards a luxury chauffeur driven vehicle which will take you around the country. This representative will be your driver cum guide throughout the tour. To begin your Sri Lanka itinerary 7 days, you will be taken to the neighboring town of Negombo where you will check in to Jetwing Blue on a bed and breakfast basis for the first night. Day2 Sigiriya Early next morning, you will depart to Sigiriya. En-route, you will stop at the Pinnawela Elephant Orphanage for some one-on-one interaction with the Elephants of Sri Lanka. In the afternoon, you will visit the ‘Lion’s Rock’ in Sigiriya – approximately 1200 steps to climb. This rock climbing activity is do-able and recommended for most tourists as you get to see some beautiful engraved frescoes of the ancient era and also the remnants of a fortress at the top of the rock, not forgetting the tremendous view from the summit. Aliya resort and spa is where you will be staying in Sigiriya. Day3 Kandy After breakfast the next morning, you will head towards Kandy after exploring the nearby Dambulla Cave Temple (300-400) steps to climb. Three and a half hours later, you will reach Kandy, after having visited the Ayurvedic Herbal Gardens on the way. In Kandy, visit the Temple of the Tooth and then check in to Golden Crown Hotel on day 3 of your Sri Lanka itinerary one week. Day4 Nuwara Eliya Have an early breakfast on day 4 and then visit the Royal Botannical Gardens in Kandy to discover a wide range of fauna including some beautiful orchids. Next, prepare for a three hours drive to Nuwara Eliya. On the way, you will visit the gorgeous Ramboda waterfalls, a picturesque tea plantation and also a tour of a tea factory. In Nuwara Eliya, you will check in to the Grand Hotel for a night, inclusive of breakfast the next morning. Day5 Ella, Yala By 9.30 am on day five of this Sri Lanka itinerary one week, you have to be at the Nanu Oya railway station for the most scenic train journey in Sri Lanka – from Nanu Oya to Ella. It is advisable to make your bookings early for this train ride in order to get seats on a good carriage. Your Blue Lanka chauffeur will pick you up with your bags at the Ella railway station and then drive you to Yala (two and a half hours). You have the remaining day to relax and indulge in the luxury surroundings of Jetwing Yala. Day 6 Galle Early the next morning, a jeep safari ride will await at your hotel to take you for a game drive at the Yala National Park. Mingle with the wildlife and try to spot the rare leopard if you are lucky! The game drive should be done by early morning (before the sun gets too hot) and you can have your breakfast then (or you could opt for a picnic pack before you leave for the safari). Next, check out from the hotel and depart to Galle – three and a half hours from Yala, located on the southern coast of Sri Lanka. En route, you will visit the Weligama fish market and when in Galle, take a tour of the Dutch Fort and its surroundings. A luxury ocean view room at Amari Resort awaits you in the evening. Day7 Colombo and Transfer to Airport After breakfast on day 7, checkout and head towards the bustling city of Colombo (2 hours drive). In Colombo, you will be shown around the city and taken to visit the Gangarama Temple, Manning Market, Pettah and Fort area, Old Parliament Building, Galle Face Green, Independence Square, BMICH, Townhall and Lotus Pond area. This city tour will only be possible if your onward departure is towards the latter part of the day or better yet, towards the night. The airport is another hour’s drive from Colombo. You have now completed the Sri Lanka itinerary 07 days with Blue Lanka Tours. To book this package or make any alterations to the duration or place to visit, feel free to contact our friendly travel consultants to guide you. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 7 Days Obtaining blessings from Monks at the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic in Kandy. Getting on board for an exciting and beautiful train journey from Nanu Oya to Ella. Capturing shots of leopards and other wildlife at Yala National Park. Meet The Rescued And Abandoned Elephants In Pinnawala Summiting The Magnificent Lion Rock In Sigiriya Get The Imprint Of You Face On The Train Window Cruise In A 4wd On A Wildlife Safari Searching Leopards Places you will stay See available hotels The Golden Crown Kandy Grand Hotel Nuwara Eliya Jetwing Yala Yala See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Observe the talented stilt fishermen in Weligama. FAQ How long does it take to climb Sigiriya Lions Rock? It does take between 1.5 hours and 3 hours – depending on your fitness, how crowded the place is and how many pictures you are shooting. Can I visit Kandy Temple of Tooth at Pooja Time? Yes you can. Pooja times are 05.30AM, 09.30AM and 06.30PM. In which sites I supposed meet special guides? In UNESCO Sites such as Sigiriya Lions Rock, Dambulla Caves Temple and Kandy Temple of Tooth you will meet special site guides. Will I get a reserved seat in the Nanu Oya to Ella train? Yes, we will book the train tickets in advance on behalf of you.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Itinerary 14 Days
itineraries.summary: If two weeks is all you have, we can make the best of it in Sri Lanka. Offering a variety of landscapes – from the finest beaches to panoramic hill country views to wildlife backdrops…
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Itinerary 14 Days
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Itinerary 14 Days If two weeks is all you have, we can make the best of it in Sri Lanka. Offering a variety of landscapes – from the finest beaches to panoramic hill country views to wildlife backdrops… Explore More Tailormade This Tours If two weeks is all you have, we can make the best of it in Sri Lanka. Offering a variety of landscapes – from the finest beaches to panoramic hill country views to wildlife backdrops, this Sri Lanka itinerary 14 days will comprise of an exciting, fun-filled holiday to spend with your partner, family and/ or friends. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1, 2 Kalpitiya Upon arrival at the Bandaranaike International Airport, a Blue Lanka Representative will meet and assist you with your clearance from the airport. He will then welcome you into a luxurious private vehicle that will be with you throughout your holiday in Sri Lanka. Your first destination on this Sri Lanka itinerary 14 days will be Kalpitiya, which is about a 3-hour drive from the airport. Dolphin Beach Resort is where you will be staying for the first two days on a bed and breakfast meal plan at the hotel. Kalpitiya waters are home to Dolphins and the coastline is ideal for a relaxing beach stay. Kitesurfing is a popular activity you might like to try here. Day3 Anuradhapura From Kalpitiya, the next morning you will depart to Anuradhapura (2 hours drive). After exploring the ancient city, ruins and basic knowledge about Lankan history, you will check in to Forest Rock Garden for the night. Next day’s breakfast at the hotel is included in the package. Day4, 5 Jaffna Heading further north, this Sri Lanka itinerary 2 weeks will take you to Jaffna on day 4. From Anuradhapura, you will visit the Mihintale Temple first, Elephant Pass next, and then reach Jaffna – a journey totaling about 3 and a half hours. Jetwing Jaffna is your selected accommodation on a bed and breakfast basis for the next two nights in Jaffna. On day 5, you will visit the Jaffna Fort, Nallur Temple, Library, Local Markets and the Nagadeepa Temple. Day6, 7 Trincomalee From Jaffna, it will take about 5 hours to reach Trincomalee, your next destination on this Sri Lanka itinerary 14 days. On day 6 & 7, you will be staying at Trinco Blu, on a breakfast and dinner included meal plan. In Trincomalee, you will visit the Kinniya Bridge and the Velgam Vehera historic Buddhist Temple. Also at your hotel, relax on the beautiful east coast of Sri Lanka and indulge in some water sports activities. Day8 Sigiriya After breakfast the next morning, you will be driven to Sigiriya (2 hour’s away) where you will visit a local village by means of a Bullock Cart. After lunch you will engage in a rock climbing activity at the “Lion’s Rock” (about 1200 steps) – which is a UNESCO world heritage site and must not be missed during your tour in Sri Lanka. After a long day, you will check in to Amaya Lake for a peaceful night in a comfortable and luxurious room. Day 9 Kandy Indulge in a delicious breakfast at Amaya Lake on Day 9 and pack up for the next few nights in Sri Lanka’s beautiful hill country. Three and a half hours from Sigiriya is the hill capital of Kandy. En-route you will visit the Dambulla Cave Temples (about 300-400 steps to climb). In Kandy, visit the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic and then check in to Golden Crown Hotel for the night. Day10 Nuwara Eliya The hotel will provide breakfast the next morning, after which you will begin a three-hour journey to Nuwara Eliya. En-route, you will visit Ramboda Falls, a tea plantation and a tea factory. In Nuwara Eliya, you will be taken on a city tour and a food tour. Araliya Green Hills is where you will be staying on day 10. Day11 Ella & Yala After an early breakfast on day 11 of this Sri Lanka Itinerary 14 days, you will be transferred to the railway station for the most scenic train ride in Sri Lanka. Our driver will pick you up at the Ella railway station with all your luggage. He will take you to visit the Nine Arches Bridge in Ella and then head towards Yala – your next destination. En-route, you may also stop at the pottery place to try your hand at clay pottery making or pick up a few clay vessels for your home. Upon reaching your next destination, you will check in to Jetwing Yala for the night. Day12, 13 Bentota An early morning jeep safari ride will await at your hotel entrance the next day which will take you to the Yala National Park to explore the wildlife of Sri Lanka. After this, you will head towards the southern coast of Unawatuna for a cookery demonstration and a visit to the local market. Your next stop will be at Avani Bentota for a relaxing two nights stay on bed and breakfast basis. Bentota is on the south west coast of Sri Lanka and offers beautiful beaches for a swim and sun tan, along with adventurous water sports activities if you wish to try some. Day14 Transfer to airport This completes the Sri Lanka itinerary 2 weeks with Blue Lanka Tours and depending on the time of your flight, you will be transferred to the airport directly from Bentota (2 hour’s drive). If you wish to book this package, make any changes to it or get a completely new tailor made package for your holiday in Sri Lanka, please do get in touch with us. Our friendly team of travel consultants will happily assist you. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days Relaxing amidst some of the best beaches in Sri Lanka – Kalpitiya, Trinco, Bentota. Experiencing jet ski, banana boat ride and other adventurous water sports in Bentota. Indulging in the picturesque beauty of Sri Lanka during the epic train ride from Nuwara Eliya to Ella. Yes, Kalpitiya is the best for kite surfing in Sri Lank Generally how is the weather in Jaffna? In Jaffna, the summers are long, hot, and overcast; the winters are short, warm, wet, and partly cloudy, and it is oppressive and windy year-round. Can I customize this itinerary according to my preference? Yes you can, since we are a personalized tour company in Sri Lanka, we are more than happy to customize the itinerary according to your requirements. Is the ETA applicable for Kids? Yes, it is. itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Itinerary 21 Days Blue Lanka Tours has designed a comprehensive Sri Lanka Itinerary 21 Days where you get to experience all landscape varieties and tour attractions that the country… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Blue Lanka Tours has designed a comprehensive Sri Lanka Itinerary 21 Days where you get to experience all landscape varieties and tour attractions that the country has to offer while also having quality time to spend with family, to relax and to indulge in nature’s beauty. Make the most of your holiday in Sri Lanka and pamper yourselves at exclusive partner holiday resorts. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Colombo Blue Lanka representatives will greet you at the Bandaranaike International Airport and drive you to the capital city of Colombo (which is an hour’s drive from the airport). There you will check in to Paradise Road Tintagel for your first night of Sri Lanka 3 week’s itinerary. If time permits on day one, you can take a city tour of Colombo and learn about its culture and heritage. Day2 Anuradhapura The next morning, you will leave Colombo and drive to Anuradhapura (approximately 4 hours) to explore Sri Lankan history. After a tour of the ancient ruins, you can check in to Ulagalla by Uga Escapes for the night. The next morning, we will take you to the Ancient City of Polonnaruwa where you can explore the astonishing grounds for the beauty it holds. In the evening, we will take you on a luxurious Jeep Safari at Minneriya National Park. Day3, 4 Jaffna Early next morning, you will transfer to the northern town of Jaffna after a 4 hour drive. Jetwing Jaffna is where you will check in and freshen up in your room. You can then visit Jaffna fort towards the latter part of the day. On day 4, we have scheduled a visit to the Nagadeepa ruins and a city tour of Jaffna. Day five is free on the roster for relaxation at the luxury hotel Kandy House. Day5, 6 Sigiriya Upon checking out from the hotel on day five, another 5 hour drive will take you to Sigiriya where you will be staying at Jetwing Vil Uyana for the next two nights. Your Sigiriya visit starts with a village tour and lunch. You will then visit the “Lion’s Rock” which is a 2-3 hour rock climbing adventure along with exploring the ruins of a rock fortress at the summit. On day 6, you will explore the ancient city of Polonnaruwa, which is just 1.5 hours drive from Sigiriya. An afternoon jeep safari at the Minneriya National Park is also scheduled, after which you will be taken back to Jetwing Vil Uyana for the night. Day 7, 8, 9 Kandy The next morning you will check out from hotel and visit the Dambulla Rock Cave Temples and the scenic Ayurvedic Herbal Gardens, en route to Kandy. In Kandy, you will be staying amidst the peace and tranquil at Santani Wellness Resort and Spa. The next two days are at your discretion for relaxing and engaging in activities conducted by the hotel (such as yoga and other wellness programs). You could take an evening out to visit the Kandy city, the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic and a cultural dance show in Kandy. Day 10 Nuwara Eliya After a peaceful stay in Kandy, you will transfer to Nuwara Eliya – a popular hill country destination. En route, you will visit the beautiful Ramboda Waterfalls, a tea factory and tea plantations. After a 3-4 hour drive including these stops, you will check in to Golden Ridge Hotel for the night. Day 11, 12, 13 Haputale The next morning you will be transferred to the scenic town of Haputale where you will have a room at the Thotalagala Plantation Bungalow. The next three days are left for relaxation, enjoying the chilled weather, indulging in nature’s beauty and nearby hill country excursions. Day 14, 15 Ella One hour from Haputale is the gorgeous town of Ella where the exclusive 98 acres resort and spa is located. At this destination you have two days to explore Little Adams Peak, the urban culture of Ella and also included is a cookery demonstration amidst the hills. Day 16, 17 Yala On day 16 of your Sri Lanka itinerary 3 weeks, you depart from Ella and head towards Yala, en-route you will stop at Ravana Water Falls. At 3.00 pm, a jeep safari ride will be waiting for you to explore the Yala National Park. Leopard Nest is where you will be staying over for the next two nights. Day 17 is allocated for a full day jeep safari excursion at the Yala National Park so you have maximum opportunity to mingle with Sri Lanka’s wildlife and spot the rarest animals. Day 18, 19, 20 Galle From Yala, you will drive towards Galle for about four and a half hours. On the way to Galle, you have the opportunity to observe the age-old tradition of stilt fishing in Sri Lanka. The ultra-luxurious Fortress Resort is where you will be staying in Galle for the next three days. On day 18 you can explore the local fish market in Galle and then take a stroll around the Dutch Fort. Spend the next couple of days making the most of the stunning southern coast – clear turquoise waters, soft sands and tan perfecting sunshine! Day 21 Transfer to Airport You have now come to the end of this luxurious Sri Lanka itinerary 21 days in Colombo with Blue Lanka Tours. We hope this itinerary is appealing to you and covers places of your interest in Sri Lanka. If you would like to book this package or get a tailor made holiday package in Sri Lanka, kindly get in touch with us. On day 18 you can explore the local fish market in Galle and then take a stroll around the Dutch Fort. Spend the next couple of days making the most of the stunning southern coast – clear turquoise waters, soft sands and tan perfecting sunshine! Journey Highlights Trip Length: 21 Days Exploring ancient ruins and learn about Lankan history in Anuradhapura and Polonnaruwa. Enjoying simple, yet breathtaking rock climbing adventures in Ella. Giving yourself an Asian sun tan on the exotic beaches of Sri Lanka. Pedal Along Ancient Monasteries And Colossal Dagobas In Anuradhapura Embark On A Ferry Ride To Visit Nagadeepa Temple Waterfall Hunting In Nuwara Eliya Engage In Cooking With A Local Family And Learn Culinary Secrets They Use To Boom Your Tasty Buds Places you will stay See available hotels Jetwing Jaffna Jaffna Jetwing Vil Uyana Sigiriya Ulagalla Tirappane 98 Acres Resort Ella The Fortress Resort & Spa Galle See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Discover the lovely frescoes engraved in the “Lion’s Rock” – Sigiriya FAQ How long does it take to complete the Anuradhapura Ancient Site? It does take approximately 03-04 hours. Are there any train transfers available from Anuradhapura to Jaffna? Yes train transfers are available. Does Thotalagala Bungalow has a spa? itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Luxury Tour
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Luxury Tour Blue Lanka Tours offers luxury at its best in this uniquely designed luxury tour of Sri Lanka. In this itinerary we focus on providing top notch accommodation and transport throughout the country… Explore More Tailormade This Tours Blue Lanka Tours offers luxury at its best in this uniquely designed luxury tour of Sri Lanka. In this itinerary we focus on providing top notch accommodation and transport throughout the country, thereby making sure luxury and relaxation is your main focus in making this holiday a memorable one. Feel free to let us know if you have any other attraction preferences in mind and we can flex the itinerary accordingly. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Colombo Our representative will pick you up at the Bandaranaike International Airport and drive you to Maniumpathy in Colombo, where you will spend your first night of this two week Luxury Tour in Sri Lanka. Day2, 3 Sigiriya On day two, we will drive you to the Water Garden Sigiriya, and on the way, you will get the chance to see Sri Lanka through the eyes of the Sri Lankan Goliath at the Elephant Orphanage in Pinnawala. Before checking in to your relaxing accommodation, we will take you on a slight hike at the Sigiriya Lions Rock where you will be able to enjoy and experience a golden sunset on the summit of the Lions Rock. The next morning, we will take you to the Ancient City of Polonnaruwa where you can explore the astonishing grounds for the beauty it holds. In the evening, we will take you on a luxurious Jeep Safari at Minneriya National Park. Day4, 5 Kandy From Sigiriya, we will move on to Kandy and en route, we will visit the Cave Temple in Dambulla. In Kandy, you will be taken on a city tour to catch glimpses of the cultural city. Kandy House is where you will sleep over on day four. Day five is free on the roster for relaxation at the luxury hotel Kandy House. Day 6, 7, 8 Hatton You will begin day six with blessings from the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic and then head on to a long stay at Ceylon Tea Trails in Hatton. Days seven and eight of this luxury trip in Sri Lanka have been left for total relaxation and indulging in the beauty of the surrounding hill country. Day9 Yala On day nine, you will visit the Rawana Water Falls and begin your journey towards the city of Yala. By 13.00 hours you will check in to the camping site and engage in an evening full of camping activities. The ultra luxurious Wild Coast Tented Lodge is where you will sleep over that night. Day 10, 11, 12 Weligama After the morning game drive at the camping site on day ten, you will check out and drive to Weligama. You will visit the fish market in Weligama and observe the challenging tradition of Stilt Fishing in Sri Lanka. Your night stay will be at Cape Weligama. Days eleven and twelve are devoted towards relaxation at the beach, giving yourself a lovely Lankan sun tan and spending time with your family and friends. Day13, 14 Negombo After exploring the Galle Dutch Fort on day thirteen, you will be driven to the capital city of Colombo. A city tour is lined up here, after which you will drive towards Negombo, your final destination for the tour. Your night stay will be at Jetwing Blue in Negombo. Negombo is the closest city to the airport and this is where your extravagant Sri Lanka luxury tour will end with Blue Lanka Tours. We hope you find this itinerary attractive and we look forward to hearing from you regarding further details of this tour. What entails a luxury holiday in Sri Lanka? Blue Lanka Tours specializes in planning luxury holidays in Sri Lanka for our guests. This includes transport from airport pickup to airport drop in a very comfortable, luxury vehicle so as to ease the ‘travel’ from one place to another in Si Lanka. In addition to that, we also offer accommodation at the best luxury hotels where you can pamper yourself to the most and indulge in all the luxury amenities. Is Sri Lanka a suitable destination for a luxury holiday? With Sri Lanka seeing only growth in terms of tourists entering the country on an annual basis, the tourism industry is rapidly expanding. More and more exclusive luxury holiday resorts are available now, including high end international hotel chains such as Shangri La, Riu and Movenpick. Therefore you are surely bound to enjoy a splendid luxury holiday in Sri Lanka, be it with your loved one, family or friends. Where are some of the most exclusive luxury resorts in Sri Lanka located? Luxury resorts are located throughout the beautiful island of Sri Lanka. Hill country destinations: Nuwara Eliya Ella Kandy Cultural Triangle: Sigiriya Anuradhapura Polonnaruwa Wildlife destinations: Yala Beach destinations: Passikuda Tangalle Galle Bentota Hambantota Ahungalla What are the common facilities available at luxury resorts in Sri Lanka? Accommodation Top Notch Rooms Modern amenities such as mini bar, tea/coffee maker Lush bedding Exclusive bathrooms with bathtub/Jacuzzi, rain shower, premium fittings Private balconies with stunning views Large HD Television screens Restaurants with the best of chefs portraying the best of cuisines. Recreation Swimming Pool Spa Gym Sports such as lawn tennis, table tennis, billiards, badminton, futsal Children’s play area Private dining options Nearby excursion arrangements can be made. Other in-house facilities such as housekeeping, room service, laundry, doctor on call, etc. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days Soaking up the luxury linen and pampered surroundings of your hotel room. Exploring Sri Lanka’s wildlife in luxury camping style at Yala. Discovering Sri Lanka’s authentic culture and tradition in Kandy. 4×4 Safari At Minneriya To Witness Largest Gathering Of Asian Elephants In The World Trek Along Tea Trails And Experience The Art Of Making Ceylon Tea Get A Jump On A Leopard-Spotting Safari Through Yala National Park Take A Swim And Snorkel In The Turquoise Bay Beneath Cape Weligama Places you will stay See available hotels Maniumpathy Colombo Wild Coast Tented Lodge Palatupana The Kandy House Kandy Cape Weligama Weligama Ceylon Tea Trails Hatton Ulagalla Tirappane See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips A seamless travel experience involving all logistics such like kipping lines while journeying whenever possible, restaurant and spa reservations, attraction tickets, private airport transfers as well as internal transportation plus VIP and fast-track airport assistance in locations. Sri Lanka is a tropical country, so always pack something waterproof. The experiences of this holiday take place in and around the southwest, and the rains hit here from May to July, yet you can expect sunniest skies in December till April. Take protection from the heat while your stay in Cultural triangle! Make sure you cover up your shoulders, knees as respectful people to the culture in the temples. Keep extra water bottles and an extra towel with you when visiting Sigiriya since the temperature is quite high. As you have to climb here, go prepared with suitable gear. You will need light woolens for the hills (Nuwara Eliya, Hatton, Haputale) since it has a cold climate. Drinking hot tea, coffee, may be a beer and hot food will keep you warm there. Proposed accommodation settings can be altered based on your request as Sri Lanka possesses most markedly high-end villas, truly exceptional and boutique luxury hotels which are far more than a place to crash, and actually enhance your dream holiday. Remember to ask your guide to recommend the best street food places, sizzling beach cafes, finest fish restaurants like ‘Ministry of Crab’ (need to book well in advance) or local food markets to shop and buy local food to feed local people. We always encourage our clients to become a responsible traveler with our luxury tour! This itinerary can be customized to ensure that plenty of restful time is included between all the exciting trips and activities such as balloon rides, wildlife safaris, Ayurvedic treatments, food fests and more. Feel free to let us know if you wish to spend time in tropical hill country with a tea estate enveloped pool, lodge shrouded by green forests or by the beach in one of several minimalistic hotels designed by Geoffrey Bawa! FAQ Does Water Garden Sigiriya has a spa facility? Yes, they do have. Can I do a whale’s watching tour when in Weligama? Absolutely yes. Season is from November to April. What would be the transport mode? Transfers will be providing by a modern luxury car which is air-conditioned and private. Is Wi-Fi readily available? Internet access is good in most major areas of Sri Lank Wi-Fi is available at most restaurants, bars, cafes, hotels, and malls, although you’ll find gaps in service in remote areas and villages.

itineraries.title: Sri Lanka Round Tours
itineraries.summary: A round tour of Sri Lanka will be covering majority of the popular tourist attractions – from history and culture to unique wildlife and breathtaking natural beauty…
itineraries.detail_title: Sri Lanka Round Tours
itineraries.detail_text: Sri Lanka Round Tours A round tour of Sri Lanka will be covering majority of the popular tourist attractions – from history and culture to unique wildlife and breathtaking natural beauty… Explore More Tailormade This Tours A round tour of Sri Lanka will be covering majority of the popular tourist attractions – from history and culture to unique wildlife and breathtaking natural beauty. This fun-filled tour also includes activities such as water sports, camping, trekking and more – while unwinding at luxurious family friendly hotels and resorts across the island. If you have any particular places of interest, simply let us know and we can customize this tour for you accordingly. Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 Colombo Upon arrival, our representative will pick you up from the Bandaraniyake International airport. This person will be your private driver cum guide for the entire duration of your Sri Lanka round tour. For the first night of your tour, you will be driven to Colombo – the commercial capital of the island. You will check in to Galle Face Hotel for your first night in Sri Lanka. Day2, 3 Anuradhapura In the morning of Day Two, you will be driven to the city of Anuradhapura. This is the city of ancient ruins where you can obtain an in-depth explanation of the history of Sri Lanka. One of the stops in between would be The Ancient City of Yapahuwa: a 300 foot fortified wonder that dates back to the 13th century. During the morning of Day three, you will get the chance to visit the Mihintale Buddhist Temple where you will find more ruins amongst the first Buddhist Temple in Sri Lanka. Afterwards you can enjoy the rest of the day in the ambiance of your accommodation. Forest Rock Garden is where your accommodation will be arranged for days two and three. Day 4 Sigiriya On day four, you will move from Anuradhapura to Sigiriya where we have some adventure lined up for you amidst the dated historical ruins. Sigiriya, also known as the “Lions Rock” is a palace built by an ancient King on top of a rock. To see the palace you have to climb the rock, which is quite a do-able activity by most of us. During your climb you will see some beautiful frescos embedded in the rock and a Lion’s Gate, as the entrance to the palace. At the top you will see ruins of the palace as well as an amazingly breathtaking view of the cultural triangle of Sri Lanka. After this adventurous activity, you will be driven to Heritance Kandalama for a relaxing night stay in a luxurious atmosphere. Day5, 6 Kandy The next day you will head towards the hill capital of the Island – Kandy. On the way you will visit the Pinnawala Elephant Orphanage where you can mingle with the elephants and be a part of their lifestyle. Later in the day you can visit the beautiful Botanical Gardens in Kandy to see some of the most exquisite flora that Sri Lanka boasts of. The Golden Crown is where you will be sleeping over on day five and six. On day six you will be taken to visit the Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic. Also known as Sri Dalada Maligawa, this golden roofed Temple houses the relic of Lord Buddha’s tooth. Afterwards, you can enjoy the Kandy Town for what it is by having an amazing sightseeing experience. Towards the evening you can enjoy an entertaining Kandyan Cultural Dance Show. Day7 Nuwara Eliya On day seven, we will explore the panoramic beauty of the beautiful town of Nuwara Eliya, also known as “Little England”. En route, you will come across attractive waterfalls amongst the hills. Your driver will stop at one upon your request if you wish to take a dip. You will then check in to Heritance Tea Factory Hotel. A visit to the hotel’s neighboring tea factory and a fun-filled tea plucking experience is also lined up for you on this day. Day8, 9 Ella After touring the town of Nuwara Eliya and indulging in some delicious strawberry meals, you will head to the Nanu Oya railway station. From here you will take an absolutely stunning train journey to the charming town of Ella. Upon reaching Ella in the evening, you will check in to 98 Acres Resort & Spa for the next two nights. The next morning, we advise you to sacrifice some sleep and wake up at the crack of dawn to be able to explore the breathtaking view of sunrise from the “Ella Gap”. This is the astonishing view in between two cliffs that creeps up with the sunrise every morning. The rest of the day is left for your relaxation. Day10 Yala On day ten of your Sri Lankan Round Tour, you will depart from the city of Ella. After checking in to Cinnamon Wild Yala, you will engage in an evening full of fun-filled wildlife safari activities. Day 11, 12 Galle The next morning, you will be a part of the morning game drive at the camping site and then move on to the final lap of your tour: exploring the gorgeous combination of sun, sand and water in Sri Lanka. For exploring the beaches of Sri Lanka, you could revitalize yourself with the help of the Southern Coastline of Sri Lanka. During your two days stay at the luxurious Fortress Resort in Galle, you will be taken to visit the Galle Dutch Fort, where you will study the well preserved fort from the time of the Portuguese rule. In neighbouring towns, you can visit the turtle hatchery in Kosgoda, coral reefs in Hikkaduwa and also indulge in some exciting water sports along the Southern Coastline. Day13 Colombo Towards the latter half of day thirteen, you will depart to the city of Colombo. Upon reaching Colombo, Galle Face Hotel is where you will sleep over for the night. The next morning is left free for you to shop and explore Colombo. Depending on the time of your flight, you will be driven to the airport two-three hours in advance. This is the end of the memorable Sri Lankan Round Tour with Blue Lanka. This itinerary is focused on showing you the most popular attractions around the island with a dedicated, private driver cum guide who will be with you all along. Journey Highlights Trip Length: 14 Days Explore the beautifully engraved frescoes inside the Rock Fortress – Sigiriya. Attend a unique and entertaining Kandyan cultural dance show. Enjoy a swim in the turquoise blue waters of the Indian Ocean in Galle or Passikuda. Cycle Through Sprawling Legendary City Monuments In Anuradhapura Authentic Tea Plucking Experience With Estate Workers In Hill Country Spend Sunset At Little Adam’s Park Enjoying 360-Degree Views Grab An Instagrammable Picture At ‘Frog Rock’ Places you will stay See available hotels The Golden Crown Kandy Forest Rock Garden Anuradhapura The Fortress Resort & Spa Galle Shangri-La Hotel – Colombo Colombo Heritance Kandalama Dambulla Cinnamon Wild Yala Heritance Tea Factory Nuwara Eliya Uga Bay Kalkudha 98 Acres Resort Ella See available Hotels View All Insightful Tips Enjoy a traditional tea plucking experience at one of the many tea estates upcountry. FAQ How many steps are there in Sigiriya Lions Rock? To the top of Sigiriya Lion Rock Fortress, it is around 1200 steps. What type of jeep model will be used for the safaris? Toyota Hilux (Open Air Safari Jeep). Are baby seats available if needed? Yes, we will provide baby seats. Is it easy to find vegetarian food? Most of the hotels, restaurants have a vegetarian menu. Most of Indian restaurants are 100% vegetarian.

itineraries.title: UNESCO World Heritage Sites Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.summary: UNESCO’s World Heritage Site List includes eight unique attractions in Sri Lanka that are considered part of the world’s heritage and should be safeguarded.
itineraries.detail_title: UNESCO World Heritage Sites Tour in Sri Lanka
itineraries.detail_text: UNESCO World Heritage Sites Tour in Sri Lanka UNESCO’s World Heritage Site List includes eight unique attractions in Sri Lanka that are considered part of the world’s heritage and should be safeguarded. These include the remains of Anuradhapura, which existed from the 5th century BC to the 10th century; the remnants of the successor kingdom, Polonnaruwa (9th to 14th century); and the spectacular Sigiriya Rock, which became a fortification in the 5th century. Explore More Tailormade This Tours UNESCO’s World Heritage Site Tour in Sri Lanka by Blue Lanka Tours is yet another stunning itinerary which captures all locations in one go. Kandy (the seat of monarchs from the 16th to the 19th centuries) and its venerated temple are also World Heritage sites, as are the Dambulla caverns and temple from the 1st century BC. Galle Fort, built by the Portuguese in 1588, is ranked 200 on the UNESCO list. The UNESCO World Heritage Sites Tour also includes two natural landmarks: the Sinharaja Forest Reserve and the Central Highlands (Peak Wilderness, Horton Plains, and the Knuckles Conservation Forest.) Boasting the pride of Sri Lanka, The UNESCO World Heritage Sites Tour can be tailored according to your preference and shuffled around as you wish. Main UNESCO World Heritage Sites in a SnapShot Anuradhapura Ancient City Polonnaruwa Ancient City Sigiriya Lions Rock Fortress Dambulla Caves Temple Sacred City of Kandy Central Highlands Sinharaja Rain Forest Galle Dutch Fort and Old Town Travel itinerary Highlights Destinations Places you will stay FAQs & Tips Day1 BIA to Negombo Begin your journey with a comfortable airport pickup and transfer to the coastal town of Negombo. Enjoy an overnight stay in Negombo, known for its beautiful beaches and vibrant atmosphere. Day2 Negombo to Anuradhapura Depart Negombo for Anuradhapura. Explore the ancient city of Anuradhapura by bicycles, immersing yourself in its rich history and cultural heritage. Spend the night in Anuradhapura, experiencing the tranquility of this ancient city. Day 3 Anuradhapura to Polonnaruwa to Dambulla Journey from Anuradhapura to Polonnaruwa, another UNESCO World Heritage Site. Discover the Polonnaruwa Ancient City by bicycles, marveling at its ancient ruins and impressive architecture. Continue to Dambulla and explore the iconic Dambulla Cave Temple in the evening. Overnight stay in Dambulla, surrounded by history and natural beauty. Day 4 Dambulla to Kandy Explore the majestic Sigiriya Lions Rock Fortress in the morning. Transfer to Kandy, the cultural capital of Sri Lanka. Delve into the Sacred City of Kandy, including a visit to the revered Temple of the Tooth Relic. Relax and unwind with an overnight stay in Kandy, soaking in its rich heritage. Day 5 Kandy Spend the day exploring the breathtaking Knuckles Mountain Range, enjoying its scenic beauty and hiking trails. Return to Kandy for another peaceful night’s rest. Day 6 Kandy to Nuwaraeliya Depart Kandy for Nuwara Eliya, the picturesque hill station known for its tea plantations. En-route, admire the stunning Ramboda Water Falls and visit the Pedro Tea Factory for a tea plucking experience. Relax and rejuvenate with an overnight stay in Nuwara Eliya. Day7 Nuwaraeliya Embark on a morning excursion to Horton Plains and witness the awe-inspiring World’s End, a scenic hiking tour. Spend the remainder of the day relaxing amidst the serene surroundings of Nuwara Eliya. Day8 Nuwaraeliya to Ella to Udawalawa Optional scenic train journey from Nanu Oya to Ella. Transfer to Udawalawe, with a stop to admire the enchanting Ravana Water Falls. Overnight stay in Udawalawe, surrounded by natural beauty and wildlife. Day 9 Udawalawa to Sinharaja Begin the day with an exciting jeep safari in Udawalawe National Park, home to diverse flora and fauna. Continue your journey towards Sinharaja, a UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for its biodiversity. Overnight stay in Sinharaja, immersing yourself in the wonders of nature. Day10 Sinharaja Explore the pristine Sinharaja Rain Forest with a knowledgeable naturalist, discovering its unique flora and fauna. Enjoy another night amidst the lush greenery of Sinharaja. Day11 Sinharaja to Galle Transfer to Galle, a charming coastal city famed for its Dutch Fort and colonial architecture. Explore the historic Galle Dutch Fort and stroll through the picturesque old town. Overnight stay in Galle, soaking in the coastal vibes and cultural heritage. Day12 Galle Leisure day in Galle, offering the opportunity to relax or explore more of the city’s attractions. Overnight stay in Galle, enjoying the laid-back ambiance of this coastal gem. Day13 Galle Another leisure day in Galle, allowing for further relaxation or exploration. Spend your final night in Galle, savoring the coastal breeze and stunning views. Day14 Galle to BIA Conclude your memorable journey with a transfer to Bandaranaike International Airport for your departure, filled with cherished memories of your Sri Lankan adventure. Journey Highlights Cycling through Polonnaruwa, soaking in ancient wonders. Marveling at panoramic views atop Lion’s Rock. Exploring the vibrant streets of Kandy City on foot. Embarking on a mesmerizing night train journey through Sinharaja Rainforest. Pedaling through the historic sites of the Anuradhapura Kingdom. Relishing a rejuvenating herbal massage post-Sigiriya climb. Adventuring through the mysteries of the Sinharaja Night Trail. Delving into the spiritual ambiance of Dambulla Caves Temple. Insightful Tips Wear comfortable hiking shoes and lightweight, breathable clothing during the Sinharaja Rainforest Trekking. Bring insect repellent, sunscreen, and a hat to protect against the sun and insects. Bring a camera to capture the picturesque views and historic architecture. Take your time to wander through the narrow streets and explore hidden gems within the fort in Galle. Start early to avoid crowds and the heat of the day when heading to explore Sigiriya Rock Fortress. Enjoy the sights and sounds of the city as you zip around in the iconic three-wheeler. Come hungry and ready to learn about Sri Lankan cuisine. Book your train tickets in advance, especially if you want to secure a seat with scenic views. Keep noise levels to a minimum to avoid disturbing wildlife during the safaris. FAQ How long does it take to climb up & down Sigiriya? Approximately around 2 hrs but depends from person to person What do you mean by special Guides for UNESCO Sites? Special guides will be arranged by Blue Lanka Tours for every UNESCO site except for Galle. Special UNESCO site guides are knowledgeable in the specific UNESCO site and impart all related information and knowledge to clients. How is the climate in Sinharaja? It’s mostly chilled and could be wet. Is the Sinharaja Night Trail a guided excursion? Yes, It is guided by an expert naturalist.
